excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Scotiabank American Express® card, a no-fee credit card by Scotiabank that earns modest rewards.

acf.subtitle:
5,000 Scene+ points

$60 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 2,500 Scene+ points upon spending $250 in the first three months†
  • Earn an additional 2,500 Scene+ points upon spending $1,000 in the first three months†
  • Also, earn 3x Scene+ points on Empire grocery stores such as Sobeys, IGA, Safeway, Foodland, and FreshCo
  • Plus, earn 2x Scene+ points on dining, groceries, gas, transit, and streaming purchases
  • Enjoy the exclusive benefits of being an American Express cardholder
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_title: Best offer is available publicly until July 1, 2026†

card_benefits.b_content:
2,500 Scene+ points upon spending $250 in the first three months<sup>†</sup>
2,500 Scene+ points upon spending $1,000 in the first three months<sup>†</sup>
Total of up to 5,000 Scene+ points<sup>†</sup>
$0<sup>†</sup>

19.99% purchases<sup>†</sup>
22.99% cash advances<sup>†</sup>
22.99% balance transfers<sup>†</sup>

3 Scene+ points on Empire grocery stores such as Sobeys, IGA, Safeway, Foodland, FreshCo, and more<sup>†</sup>
2 Scene+ points per dollar spent on dining, groceries, movie theatres, gas, transit, and streaming services<sup>†</sup>
1 Scene+ point per dollar spent on all purchases<sup>†</sup>
Standard

card_content.c_content: The Scotiabank American Express® Card is Scotiabank's entry-level Scene+ rewards credit card on the American Express network. This card is best-suited to casual travellers who are looking to avoid credit card annual fees, while earning points in important bonus categories to reduce travel expenses. [read-more ids="30129"]

The Scotiabank American Express® Card is offering a welcome bonus of up to 5,000 Scene+ points for new applicants,† distributed as follows:
  • Earn 2,500 Scene+ points upon spending $250 in the first three months†
  • Earn an additional 2,500 Scene+ points upon spending $1,000 in the first three months†

The card has no annual fee for the primary or supplementary cardholders.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

Here's a breakdown of what the welcome bonus for the Scotiabank American Express® Card is worth. Keep in mind that this doesn't take into consideration the potential value of other card benefits, which we'll outline in detail below. We value Scene+ points at 1 cent per point, and using that value, we'd peg a value of $50 on the welcome bonus alone. Plus, assuming the card's baseline earning rate of 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent, you'd earn an additional 1,000 Scene+ points for meeting the $1,000 total minimum spending requirement. We'd value these points at an additional $10. Since this card has no annual fee, your net return upon meeting the minimum spending requirement is $60. Of course, you can certainly squeeze more value out of the card by leveraging the category earning rates and the card's other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value.

As part of a partnership with Empire grocery stores, you can earn 3 Scene+ points per dollar spent at Empire grocery stores such as Sobeys, IGA, Safeway, Foodland, FreshCo, and more.†

In addition, you'll earn 2 Scene+ points per dollar spent on dining, groceries, movies, gas, transit, and streaming services – equivalent to a flat 2% return.†

There is an earning rate of 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent on all other purchases.†

Keep in mind that accelerated earning rates are capped at $50,000 per calendar year and not per category. You'll earn 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent thereafter.

For a no-fee card, these elevated earning rates are excellent, representing mostly all average daily spending categories and offering a 1–3% return on spending. This is definitely a card you want to consider as a daily driver if you're just starting out. If you'd like to estimate how much value you'll earn each month, feel free to use our rewards calculator below. [convertcalculator id="zQuPK2LrJg5zg9mSj"]

If you're interested in stronger returns from your spending, consider the Scotiabank Gold American Express® Card, which comes with a stronger 5x earning rate on groceries, dining, and entertainment – all with a reasonable annual fee of $120.

Scene+ is one of the most flexible fixed-value points programs in Canada. Points have a value of 1 cent per point, and can be easily credited towards any travel expense up to 12 months after the date of purchase.

This is far superior to fixed-value points programs from Canada's other major banks, as most of them force you to book your travel through their in-house travel agencies. With those programs, you’re effectively limited to using your points for flights, hotels, car rentals, and vacations at the rates set in the online travel agencies.

On the other hand, with Scene+ points, you can apply your points to things like vacation rentals, plane tickets, train tickets, hotel stays, award booking taxes and fees, and much more. Simply charge the travel expense to your credit card, and then redeem points to "offset" the charge.

The best part of this is that since you can book directly with travel vendors, you'll get the full entitlement to earning elite benefits, and you'll also be covered by the card's insurance coverage (outlined below).
Your Scene+ points won’t expire for as long as you’re a cardholder. If you cancel the card, you’ll have 60 days to redeem your Scene+ points before they’re gone.

Despite not being issued by American Express, this card still gets many of the perks of being on the American Express payment network. You'll be able to manually register for the potentially lucrative Amex Offers, use Amex Front of the Line for presale event tickets, and use American Express Invites for exclusive experiences.† Be sure to keep an eye out on the Amex Offers website, and then manually register your card if you see anything that catches your eye.

The insurance on the Scotiabank American Express® Card is for a no-fee product. Coverage includes:
  • Mobile device insurance of up to $500 in the event a new phone or tablet is lost, damaged, or stolen.†
  • Extended warranty of up to one more year and purchase protection of 90 days against damage, loss, or theft of new items.†

colum_block.col1_text:
There is no minimum income requirement associated with the Scotiabank American Express® Card. However, you'd need to be approved for a credit limit of at least $1,000 to be eligible.

Follow the link below to the Scotiabank website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: † See Card Provider's website and Card Application for complete card details, terms and current offers. Reasonable efforts are made to maintain accuracy of information.

Enjoy six months of Instacart+ and a $10 monthly Instacart credit when you enroll your eligible BMO Credit Card
Comprehensive

card_content.c_content: The BMO Ascend World Elite®* Mastercard®* is long-reigning backbone of the BMO Rewards program, and it also comes with a great insurance package.

The BMO Ascend World Elite®* Mastercard®* offers a welcome bonus of up to 100,000 BMO Rewards points, distributed as follows:
  • 45,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending $5,000 in the first 110 days
  • 20,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending $10,000 in the first 180 days
  • 35,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending $20,000 in the first 365 days

The card comes with an annual fee of $150, which is waived in your first anniversary for both primary cardholders and authorized users.

The earning rate on the BMO Ascend World Elite®* Mastercard®* is as follows:

  • 5 BMO Rewards points per dollar spent on travel, up to $15,000 per calendar year

  • 3 BMO Rewards points per dollar spent on dining, entertainment, and recurring bill payments, up to $10,000 per category per calendar year

  • 1 BMO Rewards points per dollar spent on all other purchases

Since BMO Rewards can be redeemed at the rate of 0.67cpp, these earning rates are equivalent to an effective 3.35%, 2.01% and 0.67% return, respectively.

These are respectable returns on a premium card, although some might choose to earn more valuable points currencies through a product like the American Express Platinum Card instead (since BMO Rewards points can only be redeemed at a fixed value, rather than transferring out to higher-value airline partners).

However, the BMO Ascend World Elite®* still makes for a solid Mastercard option to keep in your wallet alongside an Amex product as well.

BMO Rewards points are worth 0.67 cents per point (cpp) when redeemed against the cost of travel booked via the BMO Rewards online portal (at a fixed rate of 150 BMO Rewards points = $1).

Therefore, the total signup bonus of 100,000 BMO Rewards points can be redeemed as $670 worth of travel. Plus, you'll earn more points from everyday spending as you work towards the spending requirements.

BMO Rewards points can also be redeemed at 0.5cpp against any statement purchase (at the rate of 200 BMO Rewards points = $1), but the value in doing so is clearly not as strong, so you should always aim to use your BMO Rewards points to book travel, if possible.

In terms of perks and benefits, this card offers four annual complimentary lounge passes with DragonPass lounge membership, which allows you to access the airport lounge and relax before your flight (either on four occasions for a solo traveller, two occasions as a couple, etc.) Additional lounge visits will cost $32 (USD) apiece.

You'll also get up to $200 rebate on NEXUS application fee in the first year.

Plus, You'll enjoy six months of Instacart+ and a $10 monthly Instacart credit when you enroll your eligible BMO Credit Card.*

The BMO Ascend World Elite®* Mastercard®* offers an outstanding insurance package, including:

  • Emergency medical insurance of up to $5 million for the first 21 days on out-of-province trips

  • Trip cancellation insurance for up to $1,500 per insured person and $5,000 per trip overall

  • Trip interruption insurance for up to $2,000 per insured person and $10,000 per trip overall

  • Flight delay benefits of up to $500 per trip for reasonable expenses incurred on a flight delay of four hours or more

  • Car rental insurance and Collision Damage Waiver benefits, which allow you to save on the insurance fees that car rental companies usually charge

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite®* product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $80,000 (individual) or a $150,000 (household).

Consider supporting Prince of Travel by applying for the card via our affiliate link below.

historical_offers.notes: Can also earn 2,500 for each month of spending $2k in months 4-15
Can also earn 3,750 for each month of spending $2.5k in months 4-15

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. BMO is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the MBNA Rewards Platinum Plus® Mastercard®, one of the best no-annual-fee credit cards in Canada.

acf.subtitle:
10,000 MBNA Rewards points†

$105 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 5,000 MBNA Rewards points† upon spending $500 within the first 90 days of account opening
  • Plus, earn 5,000 MBNA Rewards points† for opting into electronic statements within the first 90 days of account opening
  • Also, earn 4x MBNA Rewards points† on restaurants, grocery, digital media, membership, and household utilities in the first three months
  • Then, earn 2x MBNA Rewards points† on restaurant, grocery, digital media, membership, and household utilities
  • Use your points for any travel bookings available on MBNA Rewards†
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content:
5,000 MBNA Rewards points† upon spending $500 within the first 90 days of account opening
5,000 MBNA Rewards points† for opting into electronic statements within the first 90 days of account opening
4x points† on restaurants, groceries, digital media, membership, and household utilities
Total of 10,000 MBNA Rewards points†

21.99% purchases
22.99% cash advances (21.99% QC)
22.99% balance transfers (21.99% QC)

2 points† per dollar spent on restaurants, groceries, digital media, membership, and household utilities
1 point† per dollar spent on all other purchases
Basic

card_content.c_content:
The MBNA Rewards Platinum Plus® Mastercard® is a solid no-fee card. It’s also a good downgrade candidate from MBNA’s premium cards if you wish to keep an account open with the bank, but don’t want to pay annual fees. As a Platinum Plus® card, it also has above-average insurance for a no-fee card. This card would be a good choice for anyone who often shops at Costco or other merchants that accept Mastercard®. The card is strongest for anyone who redeems their rewards for travel but isn’t beholden to a single loyalty program and may not qualify for the MBNA World Elite® Mastercard®.
[read-more ids="30103"]

This card currently offers a welcome bonus for up to 10,000 MBNA Rewards points†, structured as follows:
  • 5,000 MBNA Rewards points† upon spending $500 on the card within the first 90 days of account opening
  • 5,000 MBNA Rewards points† for enrolling in paperless statements within the first 90 days of account opening
  • 4 MBNA Rewards points per dollar spent† on restaurant, groceries, digital media, membership, and household utilities within the first 90 days of card membership
The elevated earning rate replaces the base earning rate on those categories, and is limited to $10,000 spent per category. Quebec residents aren’t eligible for the elevated earning rates, and can instead earn up to 10,000 MBNA Rewards points by completing the following two requirements:
  • 5,000 bonus MBNA Rewards points†† ($25 in cash back value) after your first eligible purchase within the first 90 days of account opening.
  • 5,000 MBNA Rewards points† for enrolling in paperless statements
The card has no annual fee for the primary cardholder and up to nine additional cardholders†.

This card offers a tiered rewards structure:
  • 2 MBNA Rewards points per dollar spent† on restaurant, grocery, digital media, membership, and household utility purchases
  • 1 MBNA Rewards point per dollar spent† on all other purchases
The accelerated rate is limited to $10,000 in purchases per year for each merchant category†. If you exceed that threshold, you’ll earn the base rate of 1 point per dollar spent on any further purchases. Spending during the welcome bonus period counts towards these limits. There’s also a birthday bonus every 12 months after holding the card†. The bonus is 10% more points, up to 10,000 extra points†.

MBNA Rewards can be redeemed through the bank’s online travel booking portal†. You can book flights, hotels, car rentals, vacation packages, and cruises. Points can be used for bookings made through the travel portal at a rate of 100 points = $1, or 1 cent per point†. For other purchases, you can use your points for statement credit at a rate of 120 points = $1, or 0.83 cents per point or 1.67% return on spending†. Even if you don’t use your rewards for their optimal travel value, this rate is still superior to similar Mastercard® World Elite® products from other banks. You can also exchange your points for gift cards†. Rates vary depending on the brand and value of the gift card, generally at least 0.87 cents per point and occasionally even exceeding the value of travel redemptions. An interesting development is the partnership between MBNA and Atmos Rewards (formerly Alaska Airlines), through which cardholders will be able to transfer their MBNA Rewards at a 1:1 ratio to Atmos Rewards†. When this materializes, it’ll present Canadians with the only meaningful way to access Atmos Rewards miles. MBNA Rewards never expire as long as you remain a cardholder†.
Cardholders can get car rental discounts at Avis and Budget: 10% in Canada or the US, and 5% internationally†.

As a no-fee card, this card comes with relatively basic insurance benefits, with the notable exception being mobile device insurance†. With up to $1,000 in coverage in the event of loss, theft, or accidental damage, this is a good perk to have and use if you’re looking to buy a new device such as phone or tablet†.

colum_block.col1_text:
This credit card has no minimum income requirement. Even if you have a weaker credit score, you're likely to be approved as long as you don't have a recent history of excessive credit-seeking behaviour.

Follow the link below to the MBNA® website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †, ✪, Terms and Conditions apply. Sponsored advertising. MBNA is a division of The Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD) and TD is not responsible for the contents of this site including any editorials or reviews that may appear on this site. For complete information on this MBNA credit card, please click on the “Apply Now” button. The Toronto-Dominion Bank is the issuer of this credit card. MBNA is a division of The Toronto-Dominion Bank. ®MBNA and other-trademarks are the property of The Toronto-Dominion Bank.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the PC Financial World Elite Mastercard, a no-fee credit card issued by PC Financial with compelling grocery rewards.

acf.subtitle: 20,000 PC Optimum points

features.feature: $120 annual fee, waived for the first year
Earn more on groceries, gas, drugstores & travel

card_benefits.b_content: 20,000 PC Optimum points upon first purchase at participating stores
$0 for the first year, then $120

45 PC Optimum points per dollar spent at Shoppers Drug Mart
30 PC Optimum points per dollar spent at Loblaw's grocery stores, Esso gas, and PC Travel
10 PC Optimum points per dollar spent on all other purchases
Basic

card_content.c_content: The PC Financial World Elite Mastercard is a fine choice for people who frequently shop at Loblaw's brands, but otherwise has limited appeal.

The card currently has a welcome bonus of 20,000 PC Optimum points upon making their first purchase at a store that participates in the PC Optimum program. Occasionally, new applicants can earn a welcome bonus of 20,000 PC Optimum points upon making their first purchase at a store that participates in the PC Optimum program. If you're interested in the card, consider waiting for this offer to make an appearance before applying. The card has an annual fee of $120, which is waived in the first year.

The card has category bonuses for shopping at specific stores:
  • Earn at least 30 Optimum points per dollar spent at Loblaw's brands of grocery stores and drugstores, including Loblaw's, No Frills, Real Canadian Superstore, Your Independent Grocer, and Wholesale Club
    • All Optimum members earn an additional 15 points per dollar spent at Shoppers Drug Mart and Pharmaprix, regardless of which credit card is used to pay
  • Earn at least 30 Optimum points per litre at Esso and Mobil gas stations
  • Earn 30 Optimum points per dollar spent at PC Travel
  • Earn 10 Optimum points per dollar spent on all other purchases
While these rates cover a wide range of categories, they only apply at these particular merchants. So while the card may appear to have strong earning rates, it only benefits cardholders who already shop at those stores, or are willing to change their shopping habits.
PC Optimum points have a value of 0.1 cents per point, with occasional 50% bonus redemption events. Points can be redeemed at PC Optimum's partner grocery stores and drugstores.
The PC Financial World Elite Mastercard has no meaningful perks or benefits to speak of.

The card has a modest insurance package, including emergency medical insurance for trips up to 10 days, and standard extended warranty and purchase protection. It also includes rental car collision insurance for cars up to an MSRP of $65,000. However, it doesn't offer the specific provisions that are important to travellers, and the coverage it does provide can also be found on many other cards.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $80,000 (individual) or $150,000 (household).

Follow the below link to the PC Financial website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Neo World Mastercard®, one of the newest credit cards from Neo Financial that offers cash back rewards.

acf.subtitle: Up to 4% cash back†

features.feature: Access to more cash back with Neo Partners

acf.featured_info:
• 2% cash back on groceries, gas, EV charging, and recurring bill payments†
  • 0.5% cash back on everything else†
  • Boosted rate of 4% cash back when used in combination with a Neo Everyday account†
  • $50,000 minimum personal income†
  • $80,000 minimum household income†
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content: This card currently does not offer a welcome bonus
There are no additional benefits
Strong

card_content.c_content: Neo Financial has three personal cash back credit cards in their line up. The Neo World Mastercard® is Neo Financial’s mid-tiered credit card that comes with decent cash back earning potential, especially for a card with no annual fee. You can earn upwards of 4% on groceries, recurring bills, gas, and EV charging if you meet Neo Financial’s requirements for the boosted earning rate.

The Neo World Mastercard® earns cash back rewards at the following rates for all card holders:
  • 2% cash back per $1 spent on groceries on the first $1,000 each month
  • 2% cash back per $1 spent on recurring bills on the first $500 each month
  • 2% cash back per $1 spent on gas and EV charging on the first $1,000 each month
  • 0.5% cash back per $1 spent on everything else on the first $2,500 each month
If you have a Neo Everyday account you’ll get a boost in your earning rate depending on how much you keep in your account. If you have at least $5,000 in your Neo Everyday Account you’ll earn:
  • 3% cash back per $1 spent on groceries on the first $1,000 each month
  • 3% cash back per $1 spent on recurring bills on the first $500 each month
  • 3% cash back per $1 spent on gas and EV charging on the first $1,000 each month
  • 0.5% cash back per $1 spent on everything else on the first $2,500 each month
If you have at least $10,000 in your Neo Everyday Account you’ll earn:
  • 4% cash back per $1 spent on groceries on the first $1,000 each month
  • 4% cash back per $1 spent on recurring bills on the first $500 each month
  • 4% cash back per $1 spent on gas and EV Charging on the first $1,000 each month
  • 1% cash back per $1 spent on everything else on the first $2,500 each month

With the Neo World Mastercard® you earn cash back instantly on your purchases. Once earned, your cash back is available immediately in your account. From there you can use the cash back to reduce the balance on your Neo World Mastercard®, add the cash back to your Neo Everyday account, or redeem cash back for merchandise at the Neo Store.

The Neo World Mastercard® offers elevated earning rates and includes standard World Mastercard benefits, such as:
  • Discounts with Participating Partners: Special offers and savings with select merchants.
  • Mastercard Travel Rewards: Cash back offers when shopping and dining abroad.
  • Mastercard Travel Pass provided by DragonPass: Access to over 1,000 airport lounges worldwide (fees may apply).

The card offers the following insurance benefits:
  • Travel Emergency Medical Insurance: up to $1,000,000 per insurance person up to a maximum age of 60, and for for 14 days.
  • Trip Cancellation & Trip Interruption Insurance: up to $1,000 per person per trip
  • Flight Delay Insurance: up to $250 per day to a maximum of $500
  • Delayed Baggage and Lost/Damaged Baggage: up to $1,000 per person per trip for delayed, lost, or damaged bagged
  • Hotel and Motel Burglary: up to $1,000
  • Auto Rental Collision/Loss Damage Insurance: up to 48 consecutive days
  • Auto Rental Personal Effects Insurance: up to $1,000 per person for personal effects that are damaged or lossed in a car rental
  • Auto Rental Accidental Death and Dismemberment Insurance: up to $100,000
  • Purchase Protection Insurance: up to $1,000 per item for 90 days from the purchase date
  • Extended Warranty Insurance: doubling the original warranty to one additional year up to $1,000 per item

colum_block.col1_text:
The Neo World Mastercard® has a minimum personal annual income requirement of $50,000 or a minimum household income of $80,000. You'll also need to be a Canadian resident and of the age of majority in your province/territory of residence to be eligible. 

Follow the link below to the Neo Financial website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. Neo Financial is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the 'Apply Now' link for the most up to date information.

title.rendered: Meridian Visa Infinite* Travel Rewards Card

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Meridian Visa Infinite* Travel Rewards Card, a credit card by Meridian that earns travel rewards.

acf.subtitle: Earn 3% back on foreign purchases
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 6 Meridian Rewards Points per $2 spent in foreign currencies outside of Canada
  • Plus, earn 3 Meridian Rewards Points per $2 spent on all other eligible purchases
  • Visa Infinite* benefits
  • Minimum income requirement: $60,000 (personal), $100,000 (household)
  • Annual fee: $99 (rebated in the first year)
acf.fees: $99 Annual fee, rebated for the first year

card_benefits.b_content: No signup bonus
$0 for the first year, then $99

19.5% on purchases
21.99% on cash advances

6 Meridian Rewards Points per $2 spent in foreign currencies outside of Canada
3 Meridian Rewards Points per $2 spent on all other eligible purchases

Visa Infinite Concierge
Visa Infinite Luxury Dining Collection
Strong

card_content.c_content: The Meridian Visa Infinite* Travel Rewards Card is Meridian's top-tier card geared towards travellers. It's worth considering for its insurance benefits on cash travel bookings, especially for seniors who travel with their grandchildren.

Currently, the Meridian Visa Infinite* Travel Rewards Card does not offer any signup bonus.

This card comes with a $0 annual fee.

You can also order supplementary cards which carry a $30 annual fee, but are also waived for the first year.

The Meridian Visa Infinite Travel Rewards Card earns Meridian Rewards Points as follows:
  • 6 Meridian Rewards Points per $2 spent on purchases made outside Canada in foreign currencies
  • 3 Meridian Rewards Points per $2 spent on all other eligible purchases
If you redeem Meridian Rewards Points at 1 cent per point, you are looking at an earning rate of 3% on foreign transactions, and 1.5% on all other purchases. However, it’s important to note that the card comes with a 2.5% foreign currency conversion fee on all purchases in foreign currencies outside of Canada, which you should factor into the valuation of your rewards.

Your Meridian Rewards Points can be redeemed in a variety of ways, including the following:
  • Merchandise rewards
  • Travel awards
  • Account credit and gift card rewards
You can redeem your points for most redemptions online. However, if you want to redeem Meridian Rewards Points for vacation packages, or cruises, you have to call in at 1 (833) 998-4692. The amount your Meridian Rewards Points are worth will depend on what you are looking to redeem your points for. It's possible to get value as high as 1 cent per point, which is what you should aim for if you'd like to maximize your rewards.

The card comes with all of the standard Visa Infinite* benefits, including:
  • Visa Infinite Concierge
  • Visa Infinite Luxury Hotel Collection
  • Visa Infinite Dining Series
  • Visa Infinite Wine Country Program

The Meridian Visa Infinite* Cash Back Card offers the following insurance benefits:
  • Travel Accident Insurance up to $1,000,000 per person in the event of accidental death or injury
  • Travel Emergency Medical Insurance up to $5,000,000 per insured person per trip
    • Age 59 and under: trips up to 48 days
    • Age 60 to 64: trips up to 23 days
    • Age 65 to 75: trips up to 15 days
  • Trip Cancellation & Trip Interruption/Delay Insurance up to $2,000 per person
  • Delayed and Lost/Damaged Baggage Insurance of up to $500 per trip (delay) or $1,000 per trip (loss, theft, or damage)
  • Auto Rental Collision/Loss Damage Insurance up to 48 consecutive days
  • Mobile Device Insurance up to $1,000
  • Purchase Protection Insurance up to $10,000 per item for 90 days from the purchase date
  • Extended Warranty Insurance doubling the original warranty to one additional year up to $10,000 per item
  • Price Protection Service reimburses the price difference if a lower price is found within 60 days of purchase of to $100 per item
  • 24/7 Travel Assistance Service 
This card also extends insurance to the cardholder's grandchildren, if they are travelling together. Intergenerational coverage applies to trip cancellation, lost baggage, emergency medical assistance, and common carrier accident protection. While the card has decent insurance for trip interruption and baggage issues, you have to pay for the full ticket price with the card. That means that award tickets aren't covered, as a mix of points and cash.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a Visa Infinite product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $60,000 (individual) or $100,000 (household).

Follow the below link to the Meridian website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. †Offer only available to residents of Ontario. Meridian Credit Union is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the 'Apply Now' link for the most up to date information.

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 4 Meridian Rewards Points per $1 spent on gas and groceries
  • Plus, earn 2 Meridian Rewards Points per $1 spent on pharmacy and utility bill payments
  • Then, earn 1 Meridian Rewards Point per $1 spent on all other eligible purchases
  • Minimum income requirement: $60,000 (personal), $100,000 (household)
  • Annual fee: $99 (rebated in the first year)
acf.fees: $99 annual fee, rebated for the first year

card_benefits.b_content: No signup bonus
$0 for the first year, then $99

19.5% on purchases
21.99% on cash advances

4 Meridian Rewards Points per $1 spent on gas and groceries

Visa Infinite Concierge

card_content.c_content: The Meridian Visa Infinite* Cash Back Card is a solid choice for an everyday Visa card. It has good earning rates on spending, with a lower annual fee than similar cards offered by bigger banks. This card is perfect if you want a Meridian credit card with added insurance coverage, and are comfortable with a modest annual fee. [read-more ids="30102"]

Currently, the Meridian Visa Infinite* Cash Back Card does not offer any signup bonus.

This card comes with a $0 annual fee.

You can also order supplementary cards which carry a $30 annual fee, but are also waived for the first year.

The Meridian Visa Infinite* Cash Back Card earns Meridian Rewards Points at the following rates:
  • 4 Meridian Rewards Points per $1 spent on gas and groceries
  • 2 Meridian Rewards Points per $1 spent on pharmacy and utility bill payments
  • 1 Meridian Rewards Point per $1 spent on all other eligible purchases
If you redeem Meridian Rewards Points at 1 cent per point, you are looking at an earn rate of 4%, 2%, and 1% respectively. Note that there's a $25,000 annual cap for elevated earning rates on gas, groceries, pharmacy, and utility bill payments. Once you've reached the maximum, you'll earn 1 Meridian Rewards Point per dollar spent in those categories for the remainder of the year. The reset date is January 1 of each year.

Your Meridian Rewards Points can be redeemed in a variety of ways, including the following:
  • Merchandise rewards
  • Travel awards
  • Account credit and gift card rewards
You can redeem your points for most redemptions online. However, if you want to redeem Meridian Rewards Points for vacation packages, or cruises, you have to call in at 1 (833) 998-4692. The amount your Meridian Rewards Points are worth will depend on what you are looking to redeem your points for. It's possible to get value as high as 1 cent per point, which is what you should aim for if you'd like to maximize your rewards.

The card comes with all of the standard Visa Infinite benefits, including:
  • Visa Infinite Concierge
  • Visa Infinite Luxury Hotel Collection
  • Visa Infinite Dining Series
  • Visa Infinite Wine Country Program

The Meridian Visa Infinite* Cash Back Card offers the following insurance benefits:
  • Travel Accident Insurance up to $1,000,000 per person in the event of accidental death or injury
  • Travel Emergency Medical Insurance up to $5,000,000 per insured person per trip
    • Age 59 and under: trips up to 48 days
    • Age 60 to 64: trips up to 23 days
    • Age 65 to 75: trips up to 15 days
  • Trip Cancellation & Trip Interruption/Delay Insurance up to $2,000 per person
  • Delayed and Lost/Damaged Baggage Insurance of up to $500 per trip (delay) or $1,000 per trip (loss, theft, or damage)
  • Auto Rental Collision/Loss Damage Insurance up to 48 consecutive days
  • Mobile Device Insurance up to $1,000
  • Purchase Protection Insurance up to $10,000 per item for 90 days from the purchase date
  • Extended Warranty Insurance doubling the original warranty to one additional year up to $10,000 per item
  • Price Protection Service reimburses the price difference if a lower price is found within 60 days of purchase of to $100 per item
  • 24/7 Travel Assistance Service 
This card also extends insurance to the cardholder's grandchildren, if they are travelling together. Intergenerational coverage applies to trip cancellation, lost baggage, emergency medical assistance, and common carrier accident protection. While the card has decent insurance for trip interruption and baggage issues, you have to pay for the full ticket price with the card. That means that award tickets aren't covered, as a mix of points and cash.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a Visa Infinite product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $60,000 (individual) or $100,000 (household).

Follow the below link to the Meridian website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers:
†Terms and conditions apply.

†Offer only available to residents of Ontario.

Meridian Credit Union is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the 'Apply Now' link for the most up to date information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the MBNA Rewards World Elite® Mastercard®, MBNA’s flagship travel rewards credit card.

acf.subtitle:
30,000 MBNA Rewards points†

$200 first-year value
acf.reward_points: 30,000 MBNA Rewards points30,000 MBNA Rewards points†

features.feature: High rewards on everyday spendingHigh rewards on everyday spending†

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 20,000 MBNA Rewards points† upon spending $2,000 within the first 90 days of account opening
  • Plus, earn 10,000 MBNA Rewards points† for opting into electronic statements within the first 90 days of account opening
  • Earn 1x 5x MBNA Rewards points† on groceries, restaurants, digital media, memberships, and utilities
  • Use your points for any travel bookings available on MBNA Rewards†
  • Strong travel insurance and mobile device protection†
  • Annual fee: $120

card_benefits.b_content:
20,000 MBNA Rewards points† upon spending $2,000 within the first 90 days of account opening
10,000 MBNA Rewards points† for opting into electronic statements† within the first 90 days of account opening
Total of 30,000 MBNA Rewards points

21.99% purchases
22.99% cash advances (21.99% QC)
22.99% balance transfers (21.99% QC)

5 MBNA Rewards points† per dollar spent on eligible restaurants, groceries, digital media, membership, and utility purchases
1 MBNA Rewards point† per dollar spent all other eligible purchases
Discounts on car rentals at Avis and Budget†
Strong

card_content.c_content: The MBNA Rewards World Elite® Mastercard® is a compelling all-purpose Mastercard®. It has an achievable welcome bonus, an exceptional everyday earn rate, and a few unique insurance features. In particular, this card would be a good choice for anyone who often shops at Costco or other merchants that accept Mastercard®. The card is strongest for anyone who redeems their rewards for travel, but isn't beholden to a single loyalty program, although the cash back value is also extremely competitive.

As a premium credit card, this card offers the biggest welcome bonus for MBNA's in-house travel rewards program, up to 30,000 MBNA Rewards points†:
  • 20,000 MBNA Rewards points† upon spending $2,000 within the first 90 days of account opening
  • 10,000 MBNA Rewards points† upon approval
Quebec residents are eligible for a smaller bonus, up to 20,000 MBNA Rewards points†:
  • 15,000 MBNA Rewards points† upon making a purchase with the card and keeping the account in good standing for 90 days
  • 5,000 MBNA Rewards points† for enrolling in paperless statements
The annual fee for this card is $120†. Also, there is a $50 fee for one authorized user, $0 for subsequent authorized users.

The MBNA Rewards World Elite® Mastercard® has a simple rewards structure. The base earning rate is 1 MBNA Rewards point per dollar spent†, with a category rate of 5 MBNA Rewards points per dollar spent† on groceries, restaurants, digital media, memberships, and household utilities, up to a limit of $50,000 spent in each category per calendar year†. There's also a birthday bonus every 12 months after holding the card†. The bonus is 10% more points, up to 30,000 extra points†. Indeed, this is a very good return for categorized spending, yielding a healthy return on popular categories and with high spend caps to boot.

MBNA Rewards can be redeemed through the bank's online travel booking portal. You can book flights, hotels, car rentals, vacation packages, and cruises. Points can be used for bookings made through the travel portal at a rate of 100 points = $1, or 1 cent per point†. For other purchases, you can use your points for statement credit at a rate of 120 points = $1, or 0.83 cents per point†. Even if you don't use your rewards for their optimal travel value, this rate is still superior to similar Mastercard® World Elite® products from other banks. You can also exchange your points for gift cards†. Rates vary depending on the brand and value of the gift card, generally at least 0.87 cents per point and occasionally even exceeding the value of travel redemptions†. MBNA Rewards never expire as long as you remain a cardholder†.

This card comes with access to the Mastercard® World Elite® Concierge service, which can be used to book travel or exclusive dinner reservations and entertainment tickets. Cardholders can also get car rental discounts at Avis and Budget: 10% in Canada or the US, and 5% internationally†.

This card comes with the best insurance package amongst the MBNA credit card line-up. Coverage for a common carrier accident or an unexpected return home is valid as long as the ticket was paid at least in part on the card†. Moreover, the card offers coverage for flight and baggage delays, trip cancellation, and interruption†. Lastly, this card also provides better coverage than most car rental insurance policies, since it covers personal injuries and car burglaries†. The insurance coverage also extends to the cardholder's spouse and children, and they don't need to be travelling together with the primary cardholder to be insured†. There's also mobile device insurance available to cardholders†. By paying for your smartphone, tablet, or smartwatch using your MBNA Rewards World Elite Mastercard, you'll get coverage of up to $1,000 in the event your mobile device is lost, stolen, or accidentally damaged, for up to 24 months†.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite® product, this credit card has a minimum personal annual income requirement of $80,000 or a minimum household income of $150,000 to be eligible.

Follow the link below to the MBNA® website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †, ✪, Terms and Conditions apply. Sponsored advertising. MBNA is a division of The Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD) and TD is not responsible for the contents of this site including any editorials or reviews that may appear on this site. For complete information on this MBNA credit card, please click on the “Apply Now” button. The Toronto-Dominion Bank is the issuer of this credit card. MBNA is a division of The Toronto-Dominion Bank. ®MBNA and other-trademarks are the property of The Toronto-Dominion Bank.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Scotiabank® Scene+™ Visa* Card, a no-fee credit card by Scotiabank that earns modest rewards.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 10,000 Scene+ points

$110 first-year value

features.feature: 2x points at Empire grocery stores, Cineplex, and Home Hardware

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 2,500 bonus Scene+ points by making at least $250 in everyday eligible purchases in your first three months
  • Then, earn an additional 7,500 Scene+ point bonus when you spend at least $1,000 in everyday eligible purchases in your first three months
  • Also, earn 2 Scene+ points per dollar spent at Sobeys, Safeway, Foodland & Participating Co-ops, FreshCo and more
  • Plus, earn 2x Scene+ points per dollar spent on Cineplex and Home Hardware purchases
  • Earn 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent on all other eligible everyday purchases
  • Redeem Scene+ points against travel purchases and more
  • Annual fee: $0
  • Offer valid through July 1, 2026

card_benefits.b_title: Best offer is available publicly until July 1, 2026<sup>†</sup>

card_benefits.b_content:
2,500 bonus Scene+ points by making at least $250 in everyday eligible purchases in your first three months<sup>†</sup>
7,500 Scene+ point bonus when you spend at least $1,000 in everyday eligible purchases in your first three months<sup>†</sup>
Total of up to 10,000 Scene+ points

20.99% purchases
22.99% cash advances
22.99% balance transfers

4 Scene+ points for every dollar spent on hotel bookings, car rentals, and things to do with Scene+ Travel<sup>†</sup>
2 Scene+ points per dollar spent spent at Sobeys, Safeway, Foodland & Participating Co-ops, FreshCo and more<sup>†</sup>
2 Scene+ points per dollar spent on Cineplex and Home Hardware purchases<sup>†</sup>
1 Scene+ point for every dollar spent on all other eligible everyday purchases<sup>†</sup>
Basic

card_content.c_content: The Scotiabank® Scene+™ Visa* Card is Scotiabank's entry-level travel rewards credit card on the Visa payment network. This card is best-suited to casual travellers with good credit who are looking to avoid credit card annual fees, while earning fixed-value points in common bonus categories to reduce travel expenses.

The Scotiabank® Scene+™ Visa* Card is offering a modest welcome bonus of up to 10,000 Scene+ points for new applicants, distributed as follows:
  • Earn 2,500 bonus Scene+ points by making at least $250 in everyday eligible purchases in your first three months
  • Then, earn an additional 7,500 Scene+ point bonus when you spend at least $1,000 in everyday eligible purchases in your first three months

The card has no annual fee for the primary or supplementary cardholders.

The Scotiabank® Scene+™ Visa* Card offers an elevated 4 Scene+ points for every dollar spent on hotel bookings, car rentals, and things to do with Scene+ Travel.

Plus, you'll also earn 2 Scene+ per dollar spent at Empire grocery stores such as Sobeys, Safeway, Foodland & Participating Co-ops, FreshCo and more.

You'll also earn 2 Scene+ points per dollar spent at Cineplex and Home Hardware stores.

Then, you'll earn 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases.

Keep in mind that accelerated earning rates are capped at $50,000 per calendar year and not per category. If you cross that threshold, you'll earn 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent thereafter.

If you're interested in stronger returns from your spending, consider the Scotiabank Gold American Express® card, which comes with a stronger earning rates on groceries, dining, and entertainment – all with no annual fee.

Scene+ is one of the most flexible fixed-value points programs in Canada. Points have a value of 1 cent per point, and can be easily credited towards any travel expense.

This is far superior to fixed-value points programs from Canada's other major banks, which force you to book your travel through their in-house travel agencies. With those programs, you’re effectively limited to using your points for flights, hotels, car rentals, and vacations.

On the other hand, with Scene+ points, you can apply your points to things like short-term rentals, train tickets, and award taxes and fees. Simply charge the travel expense to your credit card, and then redeem points to "offset" the charge.

Your points won't expire for as long as you're a cardholder. If you cancel the card, you'll have 60 days to redeem your Scene+ points before they're gone.

As a Scotiabank® SCENE+™ Visa* Cardholder, you can save up to 25% off base rates at participating AVIS locations and at participating Budget locations in Canada and the U.S. when you pay with your Scotiabank Scene+ Visa card.†

There's only optional insurance available on the Scotiabank® Scene+™ Visa* Card, and you can choose to pay for Basic Protection or Comprehensive Protection bundles. More information about the optional coverage is available on the Scotiabank website.

colum_block.col1_text:
There is no minimum income requirement associated with the Scotiabank® Scene+™ Visa* Card. However, you'd need to be approved for a credit limit of at least $500 to be eligible.

Follow the link below to the Scotiabank website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. See Card Provider's website and Card Application for complete card details, terms and current offers. Reasonable efforts are made to maintain accuracy of information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite* Card, the flagship travel rewards credit card issued by Scotiabank.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 60,000 Scene+ points

$450 first-year value

features.feature: 2x Scene+ points on groceries, dining, entertainment, and transit
6 Visa Airport Companion lounge visits per year

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 40,000 Scene+ points upon spending $2,000 in the first three months
  • Earn 10,000 Scene+ points upon spending $10,000 in the first six months
  • Plus, earn an additional 10,000 Scene+ points upon spending $40,000 annually
  • Earn 2x Scene+ points on groceries, dining, entertainment, and transit
  • Plus, earn 3x Scene+ points on grocery purchases at Sobeys, IGA, Safeway, and FreshCo
  • Visa Airport Companion membership with six free lounge visits per year
  • Redeem points for a statement credit against any travel expense
  • Minimum income: $60,000 personal or $100,000 household
  • Annual fee: $150

card_benefits.b_content:
40,000 Scene+ points upon spending $2,000 in the first three months
10,000 Scene+ points upon spending $10,000 in the first six months
10,000 Scene+ points upon spending $40,000 each year
Total of up to 60,000 Scene+ points

20.99% purchases
22.99% cash advances
22.99% balance transfers

3 Scene+ points per dollar spent at Sobeys, IGA, Safeway and FreshCo
2 Scene+ points per dollar spent on transit, groceries, dining, and entertainment
1 Scene+ point per dollar spent on all other purchases

No foreign transaction fees
Six Visa Airport Companion Program lounge visits per year<sup>†</sup>
Comprehensive

card_content.c_content:
The Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite* Card is one of the best all-around travel credit cards in Canada, and one that we recommend for every Canadian traveller. It's also an excellent option for earning fixed-value points, due to the sheer ease of redeeming Scene+ points.

Throw in its popular no foreign transaction fees feature, and this card is a clear winner, with a relatively low annual fee to boot. [read-more ids="30129,30104"]

The Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite* Card currently offers up to 60,000 Scene+ points.

You'll earn 40,000 Scene+ points after spending $2,000 in the first 3 months, which we value at up to $400 when redeemed as a statement credit against eligible travel purchases (based on our 1 cent per point valuation). Then, you can earn an additional 10,000 Scene+ points after spending $10,000 in the first 6 months. Finally, the card offers an ongoing annual spend bonus: you can earn 10,000 Scene+ points each year after spending $40,000. The annual fee is $150. You'll also get one free supplementary card; additional supplementary cards cost $50 each.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

Here's a breakdown of what the Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite* Card's offer is worth using our Scene+ valuation of 1 cent per point. This focuses strictly on points and fees, not the card's other perks (which can absolutely move the needle depending on your travel habits). You'll earn 60,000 Scene+ points from the welcome bonus (40,000 points after spending $2,000 in 3 months, plus 10,000 points after spending $10,000 in 6 months, plus 10,000 points after spending $40,000 in 12 months), which we'd value at $600. On top of that, assuming the card's baseline earning rate of 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent, you'd earn 40,000 Scene+ points from $40,000 in spending, which we'd value at $400. That brings your total first-year points value to $1,000. After factoring in the card's $150 annual fee, you're looking at a net return of $850 for meeting all three welcome offer thresholds. You can also earn 10,000 Scene+ points annually upon spending $40,000 each year. That's worth about $100 by our valuation. But realistically, if you're already spending $10,000 to earn the second welcome bonus, the incremental spend required to reach $40,000 is another $30,000. For many cardholders, that $30,000 is better directed to other cards with stronger everyday earn rates, better category multipliers, or a more rewarding long-term setup. So: nice if you naturally put $40,000 on this card anyway, but not something most people should chase deliberately. Of course, you can certainly squeeze more value out of the card by leveraging the category earning rates and the card's many other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value.

The Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite* Card offers 2 Scene+ points per dollar spent on groceries, dining, entertainment purchases, and daily transit. Entertainment includes movies, theatre, and live performances, while transit covers railroads, local transit, taxis, and ride-sharing services like Uber and Lyft.

As part of a partnership with Empire grocery stores, you can earn 3 Scene+ points per dollar spent on Empire grocery stores such as Sobeys, IGA, Safeway, Foodland, and FreshCo. Purchases made at other grocery stores will continue earning 2 points per dollar.

While this is a decent return, you'll note that the Scotiabank Gold American Express® Card actually offers a higher 5% return on groceries, dining, and entertainment, so that'd be a better card to use for spending on these categories. Of course, Visa is more widely accepted than Amex, so that's another thing to take into account.

On all other spending, you'll earn a standard 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent.

It's worth noting that the category earning rates also apply to purchases made outside of Canada! Moreover, it's important to note that the accelerated earning rates are capped at $50,000 per calendar year, and not per category. You'll earn 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent thereafter.
If you'd like to estimate how much value you'll earn each month, feel free to use our rewards calculator below.

Scene+ is one of the most flexible fixed-value points programs in Canada. Points have a value of 1 cent per point, and can be easily credited towards any travel expense up to 12 months after the date of purchase.

This is far superior to fixed-value points programs from Canada's other major banks, as most of them force you to book your travel through their in-house travel agencies. With those programs, you’re effectively limited to using your points for flights, hotels, car rentals, and vacations at the rates set in the online travel agencies.

On the other hand, with Scene+ points, you can apply your points to things like vacation rentals, plane tickets, train tickets, hotel stays, award booking taxes and fees, and much more. Simply charge the travel expense to your credit card, and then redeem points to "offset" the charge.

The best part of this is that since you can book directly with travel vendors, you'll get the full entitlement to earning elite benefits, and you'll also be covered by the card's insurance coverage (outlined below).
Your Scene+ points won’t expire for as long as you’re a cardholder. If you cancel the card, you’ll have 60 days to redeem your Scene+ points before they’re gone.

One of the main features of this card it that it has no foreign transaction fees – indeed, it’s the first credit card offered by the Big 5 banks in Canada to include such a benefit. While most other credit cards charge a 2.5% markup on foreign transactions, the Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite* Card uses the Visa mid-market rate.

This is therefore an excellent card to use when you’re travelling abroad. In fact, if you were to make more than $6,000 in foreign purchases in one year, then you'd have saved enough to cover the card's annual fee.

This is because most other cards that charge foreign transaction fees would have levied $150 in fees after spending $6,000, with a 2.5% foreign exchange fee.

$6,000 a year isn’t a lot of money to spend on foreign purchases if you’re a regular traveller. Throw in six complimentary Visa Airport Companion Program lounge visits, and the Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite* Card is a great addition to your wallet.

The Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite* Card is a premium travel credit card, and so comes with very strong insurance benefits. Coverage includes:

  • Emergency medical insurance of up to $1 million for you, your spouse, and dependent children on unexpected medical illness or injury when travelling out-of-province for up to 25 days (3 days for age 65 and up)

  • Trip cancellation and interruption insurance of up to $1,500 and $2,000 per person, respectively

  • Flight delay insurance for delays of more than four hours, up to $500 per person

  • Delayed and lost baggage insurance of up to $1,000

  • Rental car collision or damage insurance, which can help you save on the insurance fees usually charged by car rental companies for vehicles with a MSRP of up to $65,000

  • Hotel burglary insurance of up to $1,000 in the event of theft
  • Mobile device insurance of up to $1,000 if your device is lost, stolen, or accidentally damaged.

Plus, on the purchase protection side, you’ll get a 90-day protection period against damage, loss, or theft for most new items. In addition, you also get extended warranty of up to one year.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a Visa Infinite product, this credit card has a minimum personal annual income requirement of $60,000 or a minimum household income of $100,000, or have $250,000 in assets under management with Scotiabank to be eligible. You'll also need to be approved for a credit limit of at least $5,000.

Follow the link below to the Scotiabank website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

additional 10k pts upon spending $40k in 12 months

acf.subtitle:
15,000 WestJet points†

$121 first-year value

features.feature: Complimentary DoorDash DashPass for six months†

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 5,000 WestJet points upon first purchase $1,000†
  • Earn 10,000 WestJet points upon spending $1,000 in the first three months†
  • Plus, receive an annual companion voucher for discounted travel on WestJet flights, with a co-pay starting at $199 plus taxes and fees†
  • Earn 1.5 WestJet points per dollar spent on WestJet flights or WestJet Vacations packages†
  • Use your WestJet points to pay for all or part of a WestJet flight, WestJet Vacations package, or Sunwing Vacations package†
  • No minimum income requirement†
  • Annual fee: $39†

card_benefits.b_content:
Earn 5,000 WestJet points upon first purchase†
Earn 5,000 WestJet points upon spending $1,000 in the first three months†
$39<sup>†</sup>

20.99% purchases
22.99% cash advances (21.99% QC)
22.99% balance transfers (21.99% QC)

Earn 1.5 WestJet points per dollar spent on WestJet flights and WestJet Vacations<sup>†</sup>
Earn 1.5 WestJet points per dollar spent on restaurants, food delivery, digital subscriptions, streaming services and digital games<sup>†</sup>
Earn 1 WestJet point per dollar spent on all other purchases<sup>†</sup>
Annual WestJet round-trip companion voucher upon spending $2,500 annually<sup>†</sup>
Standard

card_content.c_content: The WestJet RBC® Mastercard is a scaled-down version of the WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard. We'd recommend the WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard to anyone who qualifies for it, as it has a stronger welcome bonus, a more powerful companion pass, and more card benefits. However, the WestJet RBC® Mastercard could be a good fit for those that don't qualify for the World Elite card but like to travel with WestJet.

Currently, the signup offer on the WestJet RBC® Mastercard is 10,000 WestJet dollars structured as follows:

  • 10,000 WestJet dollars upon spending $1,000 in the first three months†

The annual fee is $39, with supplementary cardholders costing $19 each.†

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

Here's a breakdown of what the welcome bonus for the WestJet RBC® Mastercard is worth. Keep in mind that this doesn't take into consideration the potential value of other card benefits, which we'll outline in detail below. We value WestJet points at 1 cent per point, and using that value, we'd estimate that the total welcome offer of 10,000 WestJet dollars† is worth $100. Plus, assuming the card's baseline earning rate of 1 WestJet point per dollar spent, you'd earn an additional 1,000 WestJet points (at least) for meeting the $1,000 minimum spending requirement. We'd value these points at an additional $10. Therefore, in the first year as a cardholder, the minimum amount of net value you can expect to get from this card's welcome bonus is $71: $100 from the total of 10,000 points from the welcome bonus, $10 by spending the bare minimum $1,000 to earn the second part of the welcome bonus (and assuming the card's baseline earning rate), and then minus the card's annual fee of $39. Of course, you can certainly squeeze more value out of the card by leveraging the category earning rates and the card's other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value.

With the WestJet RBC® Mastercard, you’ll earn:

  • 1.5 WestJet points per dollar spent on eligible WestJet flights, WestJet Vacations, and Sunwing Vacations†
  • 1.5 WestJet points per dollar spent on restaurants, food delivery, digital subscriptions, streaming services and digital games†
  • 1 WestJet point per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases†

WestJet Rewards prides itself on being a straightforward loyalty program that's easy to understand. For the most part, each WestJet point is worth 1 cent per point, which means that 100 WestJet points is worth $1, 1,000 WestJet points is worth $10, 10,000 WestJet points is worth $100, and so on. At the time of booking, WestJet points can be redeemed against the base fare, Other Air Transportation Charges ("Other ATC"), extra baggage fees, and seat selection fees at a value of 1 cent per point. You need to redeem at least 2,500 WestJet points for a flight, or at least 5,000 WestJet points for a WestJet Vacations package. After you've made a full redemption for everything on the ticket, if you have enough WestJet points remaining to cover the taxes and fees, you can choose to do so at a rate of 105–110 WestJet points = $1 (CAD) towards the taxes and fees. This results in a value of 0.91–0.95 cents per point. Unlike many other loyalty programs, the opportunities to score outsized value from your points are quite limited with the WestJet Rewards program. One way to squeeze more value is by combining a WestJet points redemption with a Member Exclusive Fare booking. While WestJet advertises Member Exclusive Fares as offering up to 65% off of base fares, you're much more likely to come across discounts of 5–10%. Indeed, this is the most valuable way to redeem WestJet points. Just be sure to compare the fare inclusions and exclusions of Member Exclusive Fares, since you may be better off with other fare types. [read-more ids="165246"]

The major selling point of the WestJet RBC Mastercard is an annual round-trip companion voucher for travel on WestJet.† To receive the voucher, you must spend at least $2,500 on the card each year.

Once unlocked, the voucher can be used for round-trip travel within Canada and to/from the continental U.S. (excluding Hawaii), starting from $199 CAD plus taxes, fees, surcharges and other ATC for the second traveller. It applies to UltraBasic, Econo, EconoFlex, Premium, or Premium Flex fares, and the first passenger pays the regular fare. Taxes and fees still needs to be paid on the companion ticket, and the voucher can't be used for business class. If you'll be taking advantage of the companion voucher, the $39 annual fee is a relatively small price to pay. If you don't want to use it as a companion voucher, you may instead exchange it for a 25% flight discount for one traveller or for 10,000 WestJet points.† Other benefits include the ability to save 3¢/L on fuel at Petro-Canada whilst earning 20% more PetroPoints,† and receiving six months of complimentary DoorDash DashPass.†

As an entry-level travel card, this card comes with basic travel insurance, including:

  • Auto rental damage and theft insurance for MSRPs of up to $65,000, which can help you save on the insurance fees that rental companies like to impose†

  • Hotel burglary insurance of up to $2,500 in total†

  • Travel accident insurance of up to $500,000 against accidental death or dismemberment†

  • Mobile device insurance of up to $1,500 in the event your mobile device is lost, stolen, suffers accidental damage or experiences mechanical failure†

You'll also enjoy an extended warranty that doubles the original manufacturer's warranty for up to one year†, and coverage against accidental loss or damage of personal property for up to 90 days from the date of purchase.†

colum_block.col1_text:
The WestJet RBC® Mastercard has no minimum income requirement.

Follow the below link to the RBC® website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. RBC is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the RBC® Avion Visa Platinum†, the entry-level travel rewards credit card issued by RBC.

acf.subtitle:
55,000 RBC Avion points^

$1,080 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 35,000 Avion points upon approval^
  • Earn 20,000 Avion points upon spending $5,000 in the first six months^
  • Earn 1 Avion point per dollar spent† on all qualifying purchases
  • Transfer Avion points to British Airways Executive Club and other frequent flyer programs for premium flights†
  • Redeem Avion points with the RBC Air Travel Redemption Schedule†
  • No minimum income requirement
  • Annual fee: $120

card_benefits.b_content:
35,000 Avion points upon approval^
20,000 Avion points upon spending $5,000 in the first six months of account opening^
For a total of up to 55,000 Avion Points^

20.99% purchases
22.99% cash advances (21.99% QC)
22.99% balance transfers (21.99% QC)
1 Avion point per dollar spent on all qualifying purchases
Standard

card_content.c_content: The RBC® Avion Visa Platinum† is the mid-tier offering for RBC®'s Avion line-up, offering a way to earn RBC Avion points for those who may not qualify for the Visa Infinite cards. That being said, the sign-up offer is currently equivalent to RBC®'s flagship RBC® Avion Visa Infinite† product, making this an excellent value proposition.

This card is currently offering a welcome bonus of up to 55,000 RBC Avion points upon approval^ for new cardholders. You'll earn 35,000 Avion points^upon approval and 20,000 bonus Avion points when you spend $5,000 in your first 6 months.^ 55,000 Avion points are enough for a roundtrip flight to fly from Eastern Canada/U.S. to Mexico, Hawaii, Alaska† or Western Canada to Bermuda, Central America, Caribbean†. The card has an annual fee of $120 and $50 fee for supplementary cardholders. If you have an RBC® VIP Banking Account, you'll be able to get an annual fee rebated on one of RBC®'s premium credit cards, including this one.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

Here's a breakdown of what the welcome bonus for the RBC® Avion Visa Platinum† is worth. Keep in mind that this doesn't take into consideration the potential value of other card benefits, which we'll outline in detail below. We value Avion points at 2 cents per point, placing the 35,000 welcome points on approval at at $700 in value†. By completing the $5,000 minimum spending requirement in the first six months, you'll unlock an additional 20,000 bonus points worth $400†. On top of that, you'll earn 5,000 points from spending $5,000 at the base earn rate, valued at $100†. That brings the total of up to 55,000 points worth $1,100†. After subtracting the $120 annual fee, your net first-year value comes to $980†—and that's before factoring in the card's ongoing benefits. Alternatively, you can get the same value by redeeming via the Air Travel Redemption Schedule†, which lets you use Avion points to book round-trip economy class tickets departing from Eastern Canada/U.S. to Mexico, Hawaii, Alaska† or Western Canada to Bermuda, Central America, Caribbean†. If you can redeem your Avion points this way, your net first-year value also comes to $980† after subtracting the annual fee of $120. Keep in mind that this doesn't take into consideration the potential value of other card benefits, which we'll outline in detail below. Of course, your actual value could be even higher by leveraging the card's other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value. As a baseline, you can also apply Avion points to any travel purchase at a rate of 1 cent per point†.

With this card you'll earn 1 RBC Avion point per dollar spent on all qualifying purchases.

In terms of everyday spending, this card has a standard earning rate, and is on par with many other entry-level cards whilst earning a flexible rewards currency.

There are a variety of ways to redeem Avion points for a high value. As a transferable currency, eligible cardholders can convert their Avion points into The British Airways Club†, Cathay Pacific Asia Miles†, and WestJet Rewards† at a 1:1 ratio, as well as American Airlines AAdvantage† at a 1:0.7 ratio. Avios, Asia Miles, and AAdvantage will all provide a means to book business class and First Class flights on oneworld airlines, which will be by far the best value for your Avion points if you're interested in travelling internationally in style. Look out for 10–50% transfer bonuses to these partners too, which can boost the value of your points even further.

These can be some of highest value uses of Avion points. With a new RBC® Avion Visa Platinum†  approval, you’ll typically earn between 35,000 and 55,000 Avion points as part of the promotional offer.

If you transfer the points over to Avios during a 30% promotion, you'd get 45,500–71,500 Avios — good for a number of short-haul flights, or enough to book a one-way flight in Qatar Airways Qsuites from North America to the Doha or a multi-carrier ticket.

You can also redeem via the Air Travel Redemption Schedule†, which lets you use Avion points to book round-trip economy class tickets departing from Canada, up to a certain maximum ticket price. As a baseline, you can also apply Avion points to any travel purchase at a rate of 1 cent per point†. [read-more ids="17474"] Your Avion points never expire for as long as you're a cardholder. If you cancel or switch to a different RBC® product, you have 90 days to redeem your Avion points before they go away†.

The RBC® Avion Visa Platinum Card† includes a host of interesting benefits, including 12 months of free DashPass from DoorDash†. It also includes a 3¢/L saving on fuel at Petro-Canada^, along with earning 20% more Petro-Points and Avion points upon using your card to pay^. Furthermore, RBC Avion cardholders can save 20% at Hertz^, as well as earn 2x the points if using your card. Finally, you can earn 50 Be Well points for every $1 spent on eligible products at Rexall^, as well as an improved redeeming ratio of 25,000 Be Well points = $10†.

The RBC® Avion Visa Platinum† comes with decent insurance coverage, as would be expected for a card with a $120 annual fee:

  • Travel accident insurance, covering death or dismemberment, of up to $500,000

  • Car rental collision/loss damage insurance, which allows you to save on the daily insurance charged by car rental companies for vehicles up to a MSRP of $65,000

  • Trip cancellation insurance of up to $5,000 and trip interruption insurance of up to $5,000

  • Flight delay insurance of up to $500 for reasonable expenses incurred

There's also a very compelling purchase protection scheme in place that will reimburse you for accidentally lost or damaged personal property within 90 days of purchase, up to a maximum of $50,000 per year, as well as an extended warranty† feature that automatically doubles the original manufacturer's warranty.

Overall, the insurance on this card is better than other entry-level travel cards in the market, making it a solid option if you can't meet the income requirements or if just getting started in the game.

colum_block.col1_text:
This credit card has no minimum income requirement. Follow the below link to the RBC website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

Refer to RBC® page for up to date offer terms and conditions.

acf.legal_disclaimers:
†Terms and conditions apply. RBC Bank is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the 'Apply Now' link and refer to RBC page for up to date offer terms and conditions.

^This offer only applies to new applications for an RBC Avion Visa Infinite or RBC Avion Visa Platinum credit card (each, an “Eligible Credit Card”) received between “January 21, 2026” and “June 15, 2026” (“Offer Period”) and approved by Royal Bank of Canada (“Royal Bank”) subject to the conditions below. Additional cardholders(s) (co-applicant and authorized user(s)) on the Eligible Credit Card account, as well as existing cardholders on any RBC Royal Bank personal credit card account transferring to an Eligible Credit Card account during the Offer Period, are not eligible for the offer. 35,000 welcome Avion points (“Welcome Points”) will appear on your monthly credit card statement within sixty (60) days after your application has been approved, provided that your Eligible Credit Card account remains open and in good standing at the time the Welcome points are credited to your account, “good standing” meaning that your Eligible Credit Card account must not be past due for more than two (2) consecutive credit card account statement periods, closed, charged off or in credit revoked status. You will receive 20,000 bonus Avion points (“Bonus Points”) if a total of $5,000 or more (“Qualifying Transaction Amount”) in qualifying net purchases and/or pre-authorized bill payment transactions (“Qualifying Transactions”) are posted to your Eligible Credit Card account within the first six (6) months from your Eligible Credit Card account opening date (“Period”). The Bonus Points will appear on your monthly credit card statement within sixty days (60) after you reach $5,000 in Qualifying Transactions, provided your Credit Card Account remains open and in good standing at the time the Bonus Points are credited to your account. Qualifying Transactions exclude cash advances (including balance transfers, cash-like transactions and bill payments that are not pre-authorized charges that you set up with a merchant), interest charges and fees. Credits for returns and adjustments posted to your Credit Card account during the Period will reduce the amount of your Qualifying Transactions. The Qualifying Transaction Amount is based on the transaction posting date of each Qualifying Transaction, not the transaction date. This means that transactions that take place prior to the last day of the Period will not be considered for the Qualifying Transaction Amount if they have not yet been posted or processed by the merchant, the payment network (Visa) or Royal Bank by the last day of the Period. If you close or change your Eligible Credit Card for another type of RBC Royal Bank credit card before the Welcome Points and/or the Bonus Points have been credited to your account, you may lose the benefit of this offer. This offer may not be combined or used in conjunction with any other offer. Royal Bank reserves the right to cancel, modify or withdraw this offer at any time without notice, even after your application has been approved, including if we suspect that you may be manipulating or abusing it, or engaging in any suspicious or fraudulent activity, as determined by Royal Bank in its sole discretion. For complete terms and conditions that apply to the Avion Rewards Program, please visit www.avionrewards.com or call 1-800-769-2512. †For Air Travel Reward redemptions from the Air Travel Redemption Schedule with a maximum ticket price of $1,100, starting at 55,000 Avion points, you can redeem for a round trip flight from eastern Canada or the United States to Mexico, Hawaii or Alaska; or from western Canada or the United States to Bermuda, Central America or the Caribbean. All applicable taxes, service fees and surcharges are the responsibility of the traveller. For more details, including information on redeeming for first class and business class seats, visit: www.avionrewards.com/travel/index.html. For general redemption terms, conditions and restrictions that apply to the Avion Rewards program, please visit: www.avionrewards.com or call 1-800 ROYAL 1-2 (1-800-769-2512).

^Air Travel Reward redemptions from the Air Travel Redemption Schedule start at 7,500 points for a one-way short-haul flight within or to an adjacent province/territory or U.S. state in economy class with a maximum ticket price of $175 (and start at 15,000 points for a round-trip short-haul flight within or to an adjacent province/territory or U.S. state in economy class with a maximum ticket price of $350). All applicable taxes, service fees and surcharges are the responsibility of the traveller. For more details, including guidelines on redeeming for business class seats, visit www.avionrewards.com/travel/. Fora general redemption terms, conditions and restrictions that apply to the Avion Rewards program, please visit: www.avionrewards.com or call 1-800 ROYAL 1-2 (1-800-769-2512).
