excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Marriott Bonvoy American Express Card, Canada’s leading hotel rewards credit card.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 110,000 Bonvoy points

$616 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 80,000 Bonvoy points upon spending $6,000 in the first six months
  • Earn an additional 30,000 Bonvoy points upon making a purchase in the 15th month of Cardmembership.
  • Also, receive an annual Free Night Award worth 35,000 Bonvoy points starting in your second year with the card
  • Also, receive 15 elite-qualifying nights every year and automatic Marriott Bonvoy Silver Elite status
  • Bonus Bonvoy points for referring family and friends
  • Annual fee: $120

card_benefits.b_title: Best offer is available through a referral link until April 7, 2026

card_benefits.b_content:
80,000 Bonvoy points upon spending $6,000 in the first six months
30,000 Bonvoy points upon making a purchase in the 15th month of cardmembership
Total of up to 110,000 Bonvoy points

21.99% purchases
21.99% cash advances
21.99% balance transfers

5 Bonvoy points per dollar spent at Marriott hotels
2 Bonvoy points per dollar spent on all other purchases
10,000 Bonvoy points per referral, up to a maximum of 150,000 Bonvoy points per calendar year

15 Elite Qualifying Nights
Anniversary Free Night Award worth up to 35,000 Bonvoy points
Standard
Credit card

card_content.c_content: The Marriott Bonvoy® American Express®* Card is the go-to credit card in Canada for those who stay at Marriott hotels. This card consistently provides excellent value for its annual fee year after year in the form of a Free Night Award upon cardholder anniversary. [read-more ids="30107"]

The Marriott Bonvoy® American Express®* Card offers 80,000 Marriott Bonvoy points as welcome bonus, upon spending $6,000 in the first six months of card membership. Additionally, you can earn 30,000 Marriott Bonvoy points upon making a purchase in the 15th month of your cardmembership. The card has an annual fee of $120, which isn't rebated as part of the current offer. You can refer friends and family from the Marriott Bonvoy Card to the same card or the Marriott Bonvoy Business American Express Card, earning a referral bonus of 10,000 Marriott Bonvoy points(occasionally elevated to 15,000-20,000) each time, up to a maximum of 150,000 Marriott Bonvoy points in a calendar year. Finally, supplementary cards are free; as with all American Express cards, you're allowed up to nine supplementary cards per card account.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

We value Marriott Bonvoy points at 0.8 cents per point, which is our baseline for running the numbers.
First-year value In the first year, you’ll earn:
  • 80,000 Bonvoy points from the welcome bonus, worth about $640
  • 12,000 Bonvoy points from putting the required $6,000 in spending on the card at the base rate of 2 points per dollar, worth about $96
That’s a total of 92,000 points, which we value at roughly $736. After subtracting the $120 annual fee, you’re left with an estimated net first-year value of about $616. Second-year value If you keep the card into the second year, you unlock two additional pieces of value:
  • 30,000 Bonvoy points when you make a purchase in your 15th month of cardmembership, worth about $240
  • The annual Free Night Award worth up to 35,000 points, which we peg at roughly $280 based on our 0.8 cents per point valuation
That’s about $520 in value, and once you subtract the second-year $120 annual fee, you get an estimated net second-year value of around $400. Combined over two years, that’s about $1,016 in net value. Keep in mind, these are conservative baselines based on our in-house valuation. If you redeem your points and Free Night Award at higher-end or peak-demand properties where cash rates are steep – especially when you top up the certificate to reach hotels pricing up to 50,000 points – your real-world value can be significantly higher.

In terms of the return on everyday spending, you'll earn 5 Bonvoy points per dollar spent at all Marriott hotels, including room charges, food and beverages, and incidentals. You'll earn 2 Bonvoy points per dollar spent on all other purchases.

Earning 2 Marriott points on your purchases is fine if you're primarily looking to use your Marriott points on hotel stays; however, if you're interested in transferring to airlines like Aeroplan, then it'd be a better idea to put the charges on the American Express Platinum Card.

As one of the only major hotel brands to offer a co-branded credit card in Canada, the Marriott Bonvoy American Express Card makes Marriott the preferred brand of choice for most Canadians when booking hotels around the world.

All Marriott hotels are priced dynamically, depending on the season, and even the day of the week. That being said, there are some general ranges you can expect hotels to price at:

  • Low-end hotels: 5,000 points – 15,000 points per night
  • Mid-tier hotels: 20,000 points – 80,000 points per night
  • High-end hotels: 80,000+ points per night
Keep in mind that the “tier” the hotel falls into is also highly dependent on the market it’s in, so you can expect the Fairfield Inn and Suites near Disneyland, California to price at mid-tier levels even though it’s a limited service brand. With the 55,000 points you earn as a sign-up bonus, you can stay for 5+ nights at low-end hotels, or a night or two at mid-tier hotels. Marriott’s “Stay 5, Pay for 4” policy makes one of the nights free on redemption bookings, further sweetening the deal with booking hotels on points. [read-more ids="17481"]

The Marriott Bonvoy co-branded cards also offer unique benefits to make your stays at Marriott hotels more memorable. Cardholders are rewarded with 15 elite qualifying nights within the program, which gives you instant Silver Elite status and can go a long way towards helping you qualify for higher status tiers. Note that the elite qualifying nights from the Business Bonvoy Card and the Personal Bonvoy Card don't stack.

Moreover, cardholders can also enjoy an anniversary Free Night Award worth up to 35,000 points on every card anniversary date, which, if redeemed wisely, can easily outweigh the card’s $120 annual fee.

The insurance provided by the Amex Bonvoy Card is decently far-reaching, and includes coverage such as:

  • Travel accident insurance, up to $500,000, for you, your spouse, and any dependent children under 23

  • Baggage delay insurance, up to $1,000 (aggregate total with Flight Delay insurance), for items purchased within four days when your baggage is delayed

  • Car rental theft and damage insurance, which helps you save the daily insurance fee that most car rental companies charge for vehicles with an MSRP of up to $85,000

Additionally, the card also offers Purchase Security and Extended Warranty Protection, covering most eligible items you’ve purchased with your card should they be stolen or damaged within 90 days of purchase, as well as an extended one-year warranty period compared to the manufacturer’s warranty.

colum_block.col1_text:
This credit card does not have a minimum income requirement to be eligible.

Follow the link below to the American Express website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

historical_offers.notes: $3,000 must be on food and drinks
Additional 5x on gas and eligible travel, up to extra 15,000 points upon $5,000 spend
$20k spend for additional 30k points, and 15k points for making a purchase between 15-17 months
30,000 points upon making a purchase in the 15th month

title.rendered: Simplii Financial™ Cash Back Visa* Card

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Simplii Financial™ Cash Back Visa* Card, a no annual fee card with elevated cash back on everyday spending categories.

acf.subtitle: Up to $100 cash back

features.feature: Elevated cash back on everyday spending categories

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 20% cash back† for your first three months on up to $500 in gas, groceries, drugstore purchases, and pre-authorized payments†
  • Then, earn 4% cash back on eligible restaurant, bar and coffee shop purchases, up to $5,000 per year†
  • Plus, earn 1.5% cash back on eligible gas, grocery, and drugstore purchases, as well as pre-authorized payments, up to $15,000 per year†
  • $15,000 minimum income requirement
  • No annual fee
acf.credit_card_short_name: Simplii Financial™ Cash Back Visa* Card

card_benefits.b_content: Earn 20% cash back<sup>†</sup> for your first three months on up to $500 of eligible spend on gas, groceries, drugstore purchases, and pre-authorized payments<sup>†</sup>

21.99%‡ purchase 
22.99%‡ cash advance
22.99%‡ balance transfer

4% cash back on eligible restaurant, bar, and coffee-shop purchases, up to $5,000 per year<sup>†</sup>
1.5% cash back on eligible gas, grocery, and drugstore purchases, as well as pre-authorized payments, up to $15,000 per year<sup>†</sup>
0.5% cash back on all other eligible purchases<sup>†</sup>
Get cash back when you send Global Money Transfer with your Simplii Financial™ Cash Back Visa* Card
Basic

card_content.c_content: The Simplii Financial™ Cash Back Visa* Card is the sole credit card offering of digital bank Simplii Financial™. It’s a cash back card with no annual fee and a low income requirement, making it a good choice as a starter card. The card offers elevated cash back earning in several categories. Of particular interest is the 4% cash back on eligible restaurant, bar, and coffee-shop purchases, up to $5,000 per year. If you’re starting a new job or starting in Canada as a newcomer, this card is worth considering.

As its welcome bonus, the Simplii Financial™ Cash Back Visa* Card lets you earn 20% cash back† for your first three months on up to $500 of eligible spend on gas, groceries, drugstore purchases, and pre-authorized payments† That works out to $100 bonus cash back if you maximize the current offer. The card has no annual fee, making it a good choice to keep open as a free primary or backup credit card, all while supporting the longevity of your credit history. Moreover, you may add up to three additional cardholders at no fee, and you’ll have the option to set a spending limit on each card.

The Simplii Financial™ Cash Back Visa* Card offers the following cash back structure:
  • 4% cash back on eligible restaurant, bar and coffee shop purchases, up to $5,000 per year†
  • 1.5% cash back on eligible gas, grocery, and drugstore purchases, as well as pre-authorized payments, up to $15,000 per year†
  • 0.5% cash back on all other eligible purchases†
When you reach the limit on the elevated categories, your purchases will earn 0.5% cash back thereafter. If you maximize the elevated earning rates, you may effectively earn up to $200 on the 4% cash back categories, and up to $225 on the 1.5% cash back categories. While 4% and 1.5% cash back are great for a starter credit card, the limits on the elevated categories are rather low. Hence, you may use another card when you reach the spending limits to continue maximizing your rewards.
The cash back you earned from January to December will be applied as a credit on the statement for the following January. Your cash back is determined per monthly statement period.

Uniquely, the Simplii Financial™ Cash Back Visa* Card lets you send global money transfers with no interest, while also earning cash back, provided you pay the balance in full by the statement due date.

This feature might come in handy for newcomers to Canada.

The Simplii Financial™ Cash Back Visa* Card offers limited insurance coverage on retail purchases, but not on travel. Its extended warranty coverage doubles the manufacturer's warranty for most items purchased up to one year. Meanwhile, its purchase protection coverage allows items purchased with the card to be repaired, replaced, or reimbursed in case of loss, theft, or physical damage anywhere in the world. The protection is valid for 90 days after purchase. For both coverages, the maximum total limit of liability per cardholder is $60,000, regardless of the number of Simplii Financial™ products you hold.

colum_block.col1_text:
There is a $15,000 minimum income requirement for this product.

Learn more about the card and its current offer via our affiliate link below.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. Click ‘Apply Now’ for complete details. Banking services are not available in Quebec. ‡ "Purchase" means any Transaction other than a Cash Advance, Balance Transfer or Convenience Cheque. "Cash" means a Cash Advance, Balance Transfer and Convenience Cheque. Interest rates may change from time to time

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Tangerine Money-Back Mastercard, a no-fee cash back credit card with flexible rewards categories.

acf.subtitle:
10% cash back

$100 first-year value

features.feature: Choose your own cash back categories

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 10% cash back in the first two months on up to $1,000 spent
  • Then, earn 2% cash back on up to three categories of your choice
  • Plus, earn 0.5% cash back on all other purchases
  • For your bonus rewards, choose from groceries, furniture, restaurants, hotel/motel, gas, recurring bill payments, drugstores, home improvement, entertainment, public transportation & parking, e-games, fitness & sports clubs, and foreign currency spend
  • No minimum income requirement
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_title: Best offer is available until April 30, 2026

card_benefits.b_content: 10% cash back in the first two months, up to $100 total cash back for spending $1,000

2% cash back on up to three categories of your choice out of groceries, furniture, restaurants, hotel/motel, gas, recurring bill payments, drug store, home improvement, entertainment, public transportation & parking, e-games, fitness & sports clubs, and foreign currency spend
0.5% cash back on all other purchases
Basic

card_content.c_content: The Tangerine Money-Back Mastercard is a scaled-down version of the Tangerine® Money-Back World Mastercard®*. The two products have an identical earning and redeeming structure, with the biggest difference coming down to the Tangerine® Money-Back World Mastercard* offering much more in the way of insurance. The Tangerine Money-Back Mastercard is a good no-fee option for lower-income individuals to optimize their cash back categories.

Until January 30, 2026, this card is offering 10% cash back on the first $1,000 spent within the first two months, up to a total of $100. That's in addition to the regular earning rate, so you could earn up to 12% return on spend on some purchases. There is no annual fee, and supplementary cards are also free.

The Tangerine Money-Back Mastercard earns 2% cash back on up to three categories of your choice:
  • Groceries
  • Furniture
  • Restaurants
  • Hotel/Motel
  • Gas
  • Recurring bill payments
  • Drug Store
  • Home Improvement
  • Entertainment
  • Public transportation & parking
  • E-games
  • Fitness & sports clubs
  • Foreign currency spend
It earns 0.5% cash back in all other categories. 2% cash back is a generous offering for a no-fee card. Plus, being able to select your preferred categories makes this a highly flexible card, since every cardholder is able to select their 2% cash back categories to match their spending patterns.
Cash back is paid monthly, applied either directly to your credit card balance or into your Tangerine Savings account.
The Tangerine Money-Back Mastercard has no notable perks or benefits to speak of.

The Tangerine Money-Back Mastercard offers purchase assurance and extended warranty.

Purchase assurance protects against loss, theft, or damage on most purchased items for 90 days from the date of purchase and extended warranty doubles the period of manufacturer warranty, up to one year.

colum_block.col1_text: Click here to apply for the Tangerine Money-Back Mastercard with no annual fee and no income requirement.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. Tangerine is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Tangerine Money-Back World Mastercard, a no-fee cash back credit card with flexible rewards categories.

acf.subtitle: $120 cash back

features.feature: Choose your own cash back categories

acf.featured_info:
• Earn $120 cash back upon spending $1,500 in the first three months
  • Then, earn 2% cash back on two categories of your choice
  • Plus, get an extra bonus category when you redeem your rewards into a Tangerine Savings account
  • For your bonus rewards, choose from groceries, furniture, restaurants, hotel/motel, gas, recurring bill payments, drugstores, home improvement, entertainment, public transportation & parking, e-games, fitness & sports clubs, and foreign currency spend
  • Mobile device insurance when you buy your phone with the card
  • Minimum income: $50,000 personal or $80,000 household
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_title: Best offer is available until April 30, 2026

card_benefits.b_content: $120 cash back upon spending $1,500 in the first three months

20.95% on purchases
22.95% on balance transfers
22.95% on cash advances

2% cash back on up to three categories of your choice out of groceries, furniture, restaurants, hotel/motel, gas, recurring bill payments, drug store, home improvement, entertainment, public transportation & parking, e-games, fitness & sport clubs, and foreign currency spend
0.5% cash back on all other purchases
Basic

card_content.c_content: The Tangerine® Money-Back World Mastercard®* has a uniquely flexible rewards structure which you can tailor to your spending patterns. [read-more ids="30103"]
The Tangerine® Money-Back World Mastercard®* is offering $120 cash back upon spending $1,500 in the first three months. There is no annual fee for this card, and supplementary cards are also free.

The Tangerine® Money-Back World Mastercard®* earns 2% cash back on up to three categories of your choice:
  • Groceries
  • Furniture
  • Restaurants
  • Hotel/Motel
  • Gas
  • Recurring bill payments
  • Drug Store
  • Home Improvement
  • Entertainment
  • Public transportation & parking
  • E-games
  • Fitness & sport clubs
  • Foreign currency spend
It earns 0.5% cash back in all other categories. 2% cash back is a generous offering for a no-fee card and being able to select your preferred categories make this a great cash back option for matching your spending patterns to your rewards. All cardholders can select two bonus categories. Plus, if you have Tangerine Savings account, you can add a third category at the 2% rate.
Cash back is paid monthly, applied either directly to your credit card balance or into your Tangerine Savings account.

This card includes a Mastercard Travel Pass by DragonPass membership, although visits are subject to a $32 (USD) fee each time. You'll also enjoy a FlexiRoam benefit, which offers a complimentary 1GB Global Data Roaming Plan and 15% off the purchase of Data Plans. A full list of card benefits can be found on the Tangerine website.

Insurance is on par with the competition, with rental car insurance covering up to an MSRP of $65,000 and an extended warranty that doubles the manufacturer's up to one additional year.

More interestingly, this card offers 90 days of purchase protection and mobile device protection to the tune of $1,000 every 2 years. In particular, mobile device insurance isn't that common on cards with no annual fee, making this card stand out in that regard.

colums.col2_text: Click here to apply for the Tangerine Money-Back World Mastercard (minimum $50,000 in personal annual income or $80,000 in household annual income).
Click here to apply for the Tangerine Money-Back Mastercard (lower income requirement, but fewer benefits).

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the RBC® ION+, a low-fee credit card issued by RBC® that earns modest rewards.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 21,000 Avion points^
$387
$ first-year value

features.feature: 3x points† on streaming, digital gaming, and subscriptions

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 7,000 Avion points upon approval^
  • Earn 14,000 Avion points upon spending $1,500 in the first six months^
  • Earn 3x points† on qualifying grocery, dining, food delivery, gas, rideshare, daily public transit, electric vehicle charging, streaming, digital gaming and digital subscriptions†
  • Earn 1 extra Moi Rewards point for every two dollars spent at Metro, Food Basics, Super C, Jean Coutu, Brunet and Première Moisson when you scan your Moi card and pay with your linked RBC Card (minimum purchase required).¹¹
  • Mobile device insurance†
  • Annual fee: $48†

card_benefits.b_content:
7,000 Avion points upon approval^
14,000 Avion points upon spending $1,500 in the first six months^
For a total of up to 21,000 Avion points^

$48<sup>†</sup>
Waived for students with RBC Advantage™ Banking Account

20.99% purchases 
22.99% cash advances (21.99% QC)
22.99% balance transfers (21.99% QC)

3 Avion points† per dollar spent on qualifying grocery, dining, food delivery, gas, rideshare, daily public transit, electric vehicle charging, streaming, digital gaming and digital subscriptions<sup>†</sup>
1 Avion point† per dollar spent on all other qualifying purchases<sup>†</sup>
1 Moi Rewards point per two dollars spent at Metro, Food Basics, Super C, Jean Coutu, Brunet and Première Moisson when you scan your Moi card and pay with your linked RBC Card (minimum purchase required)<sup>†</sup>
Standard

card_content.c_content: The RBC® ION+ Visa presents an enticing choice for casual travellers who are digital savvy and frequently utilize apps like grocery delivery services, ride shares, and subscription services. The added value of $48 per year is justified by the enhanced benefits, rendering it a better option when compared to the basic RBC® ION Visa. For a modest annual fee, there is also a standard insurance coverage including mobile device insurance, purchase security, and extended warranty.

The promotional offer on the RBC® ION+ Visa is up to 21,000 Avion points: 7,000 Avion points are awarded upon approval, and and additional 14,000 Avion points are earned after spending $1,500 in the first six months of account opening.^ The card has an annual fee of $48 for primary cardholders† and no fee for supplementary cardholders†. However, if you're a student with an RBC Advantage™ Banking Account, you can get this fee fully rebated every year, making the ION+ effectively a $0 annual fee card while you’re in school.
You must apply on or after May 6, 2026 to be eligible for this offer†.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

If you were to redeem the 21,000 Avion points from approval, that’s up to $150 of value in gift cards!† It’s one of the easiest and most straight forward to redeem Avion points.
However, you can typically get a little more value by redeeming your Avion points for travel.
Here’s a breakdown of what the welcome bonus for the RBC® ION+ Visa is worth if you redeem your travel towards travel. Keep in mind that this doesn’t take into consideration the potential value of other card benefits, which we’ll outline in detail below. We value Avion points at 2 cents per point, placing the 7,000 Avion points on approval at at $140 in value†.
By completing the $1,500 minimum spending requirement in the first six months, you’ll unlock an additional 14,000 Avion points worth $280†. On top of that, you’ll earn 1,500 points from spending $1,500 at the base earn rate, valued at $30†.
After subtracting the $48 annual fee, your net first-year value comes to $387†—and that’s before factoring in the card’s ongoing benefits. Of course, your actual value could be even higher by leveraging the card’s other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value.

The RBC® ION+ Visa offers the following earning rates:
  • 3 Avion points per dollar spent† on qualifying grocery, dining, food delivery, gas, rideshare, daily public transit, electric vehicle charging, streaming, digital gaming and digital subscriptions†
  • 1 Avion point per dollar spent† on all other qualifying purchases†
  • 1 Moi Rewards point per two dollars spent† at Metro, Food Basics, Super C, Jean Coutu, Brunet and Première Moisson when you scan your Moi card and pay with your linked ION+ Visa card (minimum purchase required)†
The first thing you'll notice is not only the unique categorized earning, but the incredibly high elevated earning rate of 3x on streaming, digital gaming, and digital subscriptions. Compared to the basic RBC® ION Visa, this is double the categorized earning for just $48 a year. The baseline earn rate of 1 Avion point per dollar spent is on par with many mid-tier credit cards as well, offering a decent value for its annual fee. Additionally, you'll earn 1 Moi Rewards point per two dollars spent† at select Metro group stores by scanning your linked Moi card and pay with your ION+ Visa card. To get a better idea on how much you can earn each month with this card, feel free to use our rewards calculator below.

Although the RBC® ION+ Visa earns Avion points, as is the case on the higher-tier RBC® Avion credit cards, the card earns a different tier, known as Premium. These points can't be transferred to three of four airline partners, except for WestJet Rewards, where the prevailing 1:1 ratio (1 Avion point = 1 WestJet point) applies†. Also, ION and ION+ cardholders can't redeem points via the Air Travel Redemption Schedule, which lets you use Avion points to book round-trip economy class tickets departing from Canada, up to a certain maximum ticket price. Instead, you’re limited to using your points at a flat rate of 0.58 cents per point (cpp) for travel booked via the Avion Rewards centre†, which is weaker than the 1cpp redemption rate that you get from holding a higher-tier Avion card. Luckily, if you ever decide to upgrade your ION card to one of the RBC® Avion cards, any points you've collected will be upgraded as well. Then, you'll have the ability to access higher-value travel redemptions that are exclusive to Avion cardholders. Furthermore, if you also happen to have an RBC® Avion card, you can convert the Avion points earned on your ION cards into the more powerful tier of Avion points at a 1:1 ratio. This way, you can benefit from the elevated earning rates on the RBC® ION+ Visa, with the more flexible redemption options through Avion points. At Prince of Travel, we value these Avion points in the elite tier at 2 cents per point, given their transferability to airline partners and premium travel redemptions. Indeed, this is what we refer to as The Optimized RBC Credit Card Portfolio, as you leverage the stronger earning rates on the RBC® ION+ Visa with the more powerful tier of points with an eligible Avion card. [read-more title="Read More" ids="176807"] Your points never expire for as long as you're a cardholder. If you cancel or switch to a different RBC® product, you have 90 days to redeem your points before they go away. Moi Rewards points can be redeemed starting at 500 points ($4 value), equivalent to 0.8 cents per point (cpp). After that, redemptions can be made in flexible $1 increments (125 points each).

RBC® ION+ Visa cardholders can save 3¢/L on fuel at Petro-Canada, and will earn 20% more Petro-Points and Avion points upon linking their card to their Petro-Points account†. By adding your RBC® ION+ Visa card to your DoorDash account, you can receive six months of complimentary DashPass subscription†.

Lastly, ION cardholders can earn 50 Be Well points for every $1 spent on eligible purchases at Rexall when you pay with your linked RBC card and scan your Be Well card†.
The RBC® ION+ Visa offers a standard insurance coverage, which is decent for its minimal annual fee. There is mobile device insurance, purchase security, and extended warranty insurance†.

colum_block.col1_text:
The RBC ION+ Visa has no minimum income requirement to be eligible to apply.

Refer to the RBC® page for up to date offer terms and conditions.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. RBC is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information. ^This offer only applies to new applications for an RBC ION+ Visa credit card received between January 21, 2026 and May 6, 2026 (“Offer Period”) and approved by Royal Bank of Canada (“Royal Bank”), subject to the conditions below. Additional cardholders(s) (co-applicant and authorized user(s)) on the RBC ION+ Visa credit card account, as well as existing cardholders on any RBC Royal Bank personal credit card transferring to an RBC ION+ Visa credit card during the Offer Period, are not eligible for this offer. 7,000 Avion points (“Welcome Points”) will appear on your monthly credit card statement within sixty (60) days after your application has been approved, provided that your RBC ION+ Visa credit card account (“Credit Card Account”) remains open and in good standing at the time the Welcome points are credited to your account, “good standing” meaning that your Credit Card Account must not be past due for more than two (2) consecutive credit card account statement periods, closed, charged off or in credit revoked status. 14,000 additional Avion points (“Bonus Points”) will be awarded to you if a total of $1,500 (“Qualifying Transaction Amount”) or more in qualifying net purchases and/or pre-authorized bill payment transactions (“Qualifying Transactions”) are posted to your Credit Card Account within the first six (6) months from your Credit Card Account opening date (“Period”). The Bonus Points will appear on your monthly credit card statement within sixty (60) days after you reach $1,500 in Qualifying Transactions, provided your Credit Card Account is in good standing at the time the Bonus Points are credited to your account. Qualifying Transactions exclude cash advances (including balance transfers, cash-like transactions and bill payments that are not pre-authorized charges that you set up with a merchant), interest charges and fees, and credits for returns and adjustments posted to your Credit Card Account during the Period will reduce the amount of your Qualifying Transactions. The Qualifying Transaction Amount is based on the transaction posting date of each Qualifying Transaction, not the transaction date. This means that transactions that take place prior to the last day of the Period will not be considered for the Qualifying Transaction Amount if they have not yet been posted or processed by the merchant, the payment network (Visa) or Royal Bank by the last day of the Period. If you close or change your RBC ION+ Visa credit card for another type of RBC Royal Bank credit card before the Welcome Points and/or the Bonus Points have been credited to your account, you may lose the benefit of this offer. This offer may not be combined or used in conjunction with any other offer. Royal Bank of Canada reserves the right to cancel, modify or withdraw this offer at any time without notice, even after your application has been approved, including if we suspect that you may be manipulating or abusing it, or engaging in any suspicious or fraudulent activity, as determined by Royal Bank in its sole discretion. For complete terms and conditions that apply to the Avion Rewards Program, please visit www.avionrewards.com or call 1-800-769-2512. † To get $150 worth of gift cards/certificates, you will need a total of 21,000 Avion points. Minimum redemption of 1,400 Avion points for most $10 gift cards/certificates. Some exceptions apply. Point redemption values may fluctuate. For general redemption terms, conditions and restrictions that apply to the Avion Rewards program, as well as for current point redemption values, please visit https://www.avionrewards.com/terms-and-conditions.html or call 1-800-769-2512.

card_content.c_content:
The RBC<sup>®</sup> ION Visa is a great no-annual fee credit card for casual travellers who spend a fair amount on transportation, streaming, and video games. The RBC® ION Visa also provides access to Avion Rewards and it is the only card to target spending in these categories, that will likely appeal to younger millennials and Gen Z who wish to earn flexible rewards.

[read-more ids="30109"]

The promotional offer on the RBC® ION+ Visa is up to 11,000 Avion points: 4,000 Avion points are awarded upon approval, and and additional 7,000 Avion points are earned after spending $500 in the first three months of account opening.^ The card has no annual fee for primary or supplementary cardholders†. The offer is available until  May 6, 2026†.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

If you were to redeem the 11,000 Avion points from approval, that's up to $75 of value in gift cards!† It's one of the easiest and most straight forward to redeem Avion points. However, you can typically get a little more value by redeeming your Avion points for travel. Here’s a breakdown of what the welcome bonus for the RBC® ION Visa is worth. Keep in mind that this doesn’t take into consideration the potential value of other card benefits, which we’ll outline in detail below. We value Avion points at 2 cents per point, placing the 4,000 Avion points on approval at at $80 in value†. By completing the $500 minimum spending requirement in the first three months, you’ll unlock an additional 7,000 Avion points worth $140†. On top of that, you’ll earn 500 points from spending $500 at the base earn rate, valued at $10†. Since the card has no annual fee, your net first-year value is also $230†—and that’s before factoring in the card’s ongoing benefits. Of course, your actual value could be even higher by leveraging the card’s other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value.

The RBC® ION Visa offers the following earning rates:
  • 1.5 Avion points per dollar spent† on qualifying grocery, gas, rideshare, daily public transit, electric vehicle charging, streaming, digital gaming and digital subscriptions†
  • 1 Avion point per dollar spent† on all other qualifying purchases†
  • 1 Moi Rewards point per two dollars spent† at Metro, Food Basics, Super C, Jean Coutu, Brunet and Première Moisson when you scan your Moi card and pay with your linked ION Visa card (minimum purchase required)†
The first thing you'll notice is the unique categorized earning, as this is one of the only cards on the market with no annual fee to offer elevated rewards for streaming, digital gaming, and digital subscriptions. If you spend a significant amount on digital gaming and streaming, I would pick this card up with no hesitation, especially for no annual fee. The baseline earn rate of 1 Avion point per $1 spent is on par with many mid-tier credit cards as well, offering great value on this entry-level card†. The RBC® ION Visa is also well-suited for those who are interested in collecting Avion points while building up a relatively new credit file or restricting themselves to lower annual fees and credit limits. Additionally, you'll earn 1 Moi Rewards point per two dollars spent† at select Metro group stores by scanning your linked Moi card and pay with your RBC® ION Visa card.

Although the RBC® ION Visa earns Avion points, as is the case on the higher-tier RBC® Avion credit cards, the card earns a different tier, known as Premium. These points can't be transferred to three of four airline partners, except for WestJet Rewards, where the prevailing 1:1 ratio (1 Avion point = 1 WestJet point) applies†. Also, ION and ION+ cardholders can't redeem points via the Air Travel Redemption Schedule, which lets you use Avion points to book round-trip economy class tickets departing from Canada, up to a certain maximum ticket price. Instead, you’re limited to using your points at a flat rate of 0.58 cents per point (cpp) for travel booked via the Avion Rewards centre†, which is weaker than the 1cpp redemption rate that you get from holding a higher-tier Avion card. Luckily, if you ever decide to upgrade your ION card to one of the RBC® Avion cards, any points you've collected will be upgraded as well. Then, you'll have the ability to access higher-value travel redemptions that are exclusive to Avion cardholders. Furthermore, if you also happen to have an RBC® Avion card, you can convert the Avion points earned on your ION cards into the more powerful tier of Avion points at a 1:1 ratio. This allows you to combine the points earned from the RBC® ION Visa with the more flexible redemption options available through Avion points in the elite tier. At Prince of Travel, we value these Avion points in the elite tier at 2 cents per point, given their transferability to airline partners and premium travel redemptions. Indeed, this is what we refer to as The Optimized RBC Credit Card Portfolio, as you leverage the stronger earning rates on the RBC® ION Visa with the more powerful tier of points with an eligible Avion card. [read-more title="Read More" ids="176807"] Your points never expire for as long as you're a cardholder. If you cancel or switch to a different RBC® product, you have 90 days to redeem your points before they go away. Moi Rewards points can be redeemed starting at 500 points ($4 value), equivalent to 0.8 cents per point (cpp). After that, redemptions can be made in flexible $1 increments (125 points each).

RBC® ION Visa cardholders can save 3¢/L on fuel at Petro-Canada,† and will earn 20% more Petro-Points and Avion points upon linking their card to their Petro-Points account†. By adding your RBC® ION Visa card to your DoorDash account, you can receive six months of complimentary DashPass subscription†. Lastly, ION cardholders can earn 50 Be Well points for every $1 spent on eligible purchases at Rexall when you pay with your linked RBC card and scan your Be Well card†.
The RBC® ION Visa extends the manufacturer’s warranty by up to one additional year,† and provides 90 days of purchase protection†.

colum_block.col1_text:
The RBC® ION Visa has no minimum income requirement.

Refer to the RBC® page for up to date offer terms and conditions.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. RBC is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information. ^ This offer only applies to new applications for an RBC ION Visa credit card received between January 21, 2026 and May 6, 2026 (“Offer Period”) and approved by Royal Bank of Canada (“Royal Bank”), subject to the conditions below. Additional cardholders(s) (co-applicant and authorized user(s)) on the RBC ION Visa credit card account, as well as existing cardholders on any RBC Royal Bank personal credit card transferring to an RBC ION Visa credit card during the Offer Period, are not eligible for this offer. 4,000 Avion points (“Welcome Points”) will appear on your monthly credit card statement within sixty (60) days after your application has been approved, provided that your RBC ION Visa credit card account (“Credit Card Account”) remains open and in good standing at the time the Welcome points are credited to your account, “good standing” meaning that your Credit Card Account must not be past due for more than two (2) consecutive credit card account statement periods, closed, charged off or in credit revoked status. 7,000 additional Avion points (“Bonus Points”) will be awarded to you if a total of $500 or more (“Qualifying Transaction Amount”) in qualifying net purchases and/or pre-authorized bill payment transactions (“Qualifying Transactions”) are posted to your Credit Card Account within the first three (3) months from your Credit Card Account opening date (“Period”). The Bonus Points will appear on your monthly credit card statement within sixty (60) days after you reach $500 in Qualifying Transactions, provided your Credit Card Account is in good standing at the time the Bonus Points are credited to your account. Qualifying Transactions exclude cash advances (including balance transfers, cash-like transactions and bill payments that are not pre-authorized charges that you set up with a merchant), interest charges and fees, and credits for returns and adjustments posted to your Credit Card Account during the Period will reduce the amount of your Qualifying Transactions. The Qualifying Transaction Amount is based on the transaction posting date of each Qualifying Transaction, not the transaction date. This means that transactions that take place prior to the last day of the Period will not be considered for the Qualifying Transaction Amount if they have not yet been posted or processed by the merchant, the payment network (Visa) or Royal Bank by the last day of the Period. If you close or change your RBC ION Visa credit card for another type of RBC Royal Bank credit card before the Welcome Points and/or the Bonus Points have been credited to your account, you may lose the benefit of this offer. This offer may not be combined or used in conjunction with any other offer. Royal Bank reserves the right to cancel, modify or withdraw this offer at any time without notice, even after your application has been approved, including if we suspect that you may be manipulating or abusing it, or engaging in any suspicious or fraudulent activity, as determined by Royal Bank in its sole discretion. For complete terms and conditions that apply to the Avion Rewards Program, please visit www.avionrewards.com or call 1-800-769-2512. † To get $75 worth of gift cards/certificates, you will need a total of 11,000 Avion points. Minimum redemption of 1,400 Avion points for most $10 gift cards/certificates. Some exceptions apply. Point redemption values may fluctuate. For general redemption terms, conditions and restrictions that apply to the Avion Rewards program, as well as for current point redemption values, please visit https://www.avionrewards.com/terms-and-conditions.html or call 1-800-769-2512.

title.rendered: TD Cash Back Visa Infinite* Card

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the TD Cash Back Visa Infinite* Card, the flagship cash back credit card issued by TD.

acf.subtitle:
Up to $350 cash back†

$385 first-year value

features.feature: 3% cash back on groceries, gas, recurring bills, and more†

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 10% cash back† in the first three months, on up to $3,500 spent on eligible groceries, gas, electric vehicle charging, public transit, recurring bills, streaming, digital gaming, and media purchases†
  • Then, earn 3% cash back† on eligible groceries, gas, electric vehicle charging, public transit, recurring bill payments, streaming, digital gaming, and media†
  • Free Deluxe TD Auto Club Membership for roadside assistance†
  • Minimum income: $60,000 personal or $100,000 household
  • Annual fee: $139, rebated for the first year†

card_benefits.b_content: 10% cash back<sup>†</sup> on the first $3,500 spent in the first three months<sup>†</sup> on eligible gas, electric vehicle charging, public transit, grocery, recurring bill payments, streaming, digital gaming, and media purchases<sup>†</sup>

$139, rebated for the first year<sup>†</sup>
$50 for additional cardholders<sup>†</sup>

21.99% purchase
22.99% cash advance (21.99% in Quebec)
22.99% balance transfer (21.99% in Quebec)

3% cash back† on eligible groceries, gas, electric vehicle charging, public transit, recurring bill payments, streaming, digital gaming, and media<sup>†</sup>
1% cash back† on all other purchases<sup>†</sup>
Deluxe TD Auto Club Membership<sup>†</sup>
Average
Offer may be changed, withdrawn, or extended at any time and cannot be combined with any other offer unless otherwise indicated.

card_content.c_content: The TD Cash Back Visa Infinite<sup>*</sup> Card is a competitive all-around offering in terms of flagship cash back cards. This is a good go-to card for those who want to keep it simple, as it earns a flat 3% cash back on many common expenses.

The TD Cash Back Visa Infinite* Card is offering 10% cash back† on the first $3,500 of purchases in the first three months, equivalent to $350 cash back.† It's important to point out that this elevated 10% cash back in the first three months only applies on eligible gas, electric vehicle charging, public transit, grocery, recurring bill payments, streaming, digital gaming, and media.† The annual fee is $139, and is currently rebated for the first year.† Supplementary cards cost $50 for the first one, and $0 thereafter.† Note that in order to receive the first-year annual fee rebate, you must activate your card and make your first purchase on the account within the first three months after account opening.

The TD Cash Back Visa Infinite* Card earns 3% cash back† on groceries, gas, electric vehicle charging, recurring bill payments, streaming, digital gaming, and media† with an annual maximum of $15,000 in spending in each category.† It earns 1% cash back on all other purchases.†

You can redeem your cash back dollars to pay down your account at any time with a minimum of $25<sup>†</sup>. Otherwise, you can set up your account to do this automatically each January with no minimum cash back amount.

As a cardholder, you'll automatically be enrolled in a free Deluxe TD Auto Club Membership.† You'll get emergency road services anywhere in Canada and the continental US, including emergency service calls, lockout service, towing, winching, and personal transportation. You can read more about the TD Auto Club on TD's website.† You'll also get the standard Visa Infinite* Concierge services†, which you can use to help make travel or lifestyle bookings.

This card offers emergency medical insurance for 10 days for those under 65, and four days for those 65 and older for up to $2,000,000 per person.† It also comes with baggage delay and loss insurance of $1,000 per person, as well as rental car insurance up to an MSRP of $65,000.† Additionally, the TD Cash Back Visa Infinite* Card will double the manufacturer's warranty up to one additional year, as well as provide 90 days of purchase protection.† Plus, you'll be covered by mobile device insurance, which offers up to $1,000 of coverage in the event of loss, theft, accidental damage, or mechanical breakdown of eligible mobile devices.†

colum_block.col1_text:
As a Visa Infinite* product, this credit card has a minimum personal annual income of $60,000 or minimum household income of $100,000 to be eligible. You must also be a Canadian resident and the age of majority in your province/territory of residence to be eligible.

Follow the link below to the TD® website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers:
† Terms and conditions apply.

† The Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD) is not responsible for the contents of this site including any editorials or reviews that may appear on this site. For complete and current information on any TD product, please click the Apply Now button.

itemListElement.name: TD Cash Back Visa Infinite* Card

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the TD Cash Back Visa Card, a no-fee cash back credit card issued by TD that earns modest rewards.

acf.subtitle: Earn Cash Back Dollars.†

features.feature: 1% cash back on groceries, gas, recurring bills, and more

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 1% cash back†on eligible grocery, gas, electric vehicle charging, public transit, recurring bill payments, streaming, digital gaming, and media purchases†
  • Then, earn 0.5% cash back† on all other eligible purchases†
  • No minimum income requirement
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content: This card does not currently offer a signup bonus.

21.99% purchase
22.99% cash advance (21.99% in Quebec)
22.99% balance transfer (21.99% in Quebec)

1% cash back on groceries, gas, electric vehicle charging, public transit, recurring bill payments, streaming, digital gaming, and media purchases†
0.5% cash back on all other purchases†
Basic

card_content.c_content: The TD Cash Back Visa* Card is a card geared towards students, newcomers to Canada, or individuals with new or recovering credit files who want to build a strong relationship with TD while earning some cash back.

The card has no annual fee for both the primary cardholder and any supplementary cardholders. It’s a solid choice to keep open as a free primary or backup credit card, all while supporting the longevity of your credit history.

The TD Cash Back Visa* Card earns 1% cash back on groceries, gas, electric vehicle charging, public transit, recurring bill payments, streaming, digital gaming, and media, with a maximum of $5,000 spent per year for gas, groceries, electric vehicle charging, and public transit combined, and a maximum of $5,000 for recurring bills, streaming, digital gaming, and media combined. It earns 0.5% cash back on all other purchases.

You can redeem your cash back dollars to pay down your account at any time with a minimum of $25. Otherwise, you can set up your account to do this automatically each January with no minimum cashback amount.

Cardholders save a minimum of 10% off base rates at Avis and Budget when renting within Canada and the U.S., and a minimum of 5% off elsewhere.

You can link TD Cash Back Visa* Card to earn 50% more Stars at participating Starbucks stores. Conditions apply.
This card doubles the manufacturer's warranty up to one additional year as well as provides 90 days of purchase protection.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a basic no-fee product, the TD Cash Back Visa Card has no minimum income requirement.

Follow the below link to the TD website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers:
† Terms and conditions apply.

† The Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD) is not responsible for the contents of this site including any editorials or reviews that may appear on this site. For complete and current information on any TD product, please click the Apply Now button.

title.rendered: TD First Class Travel® Visa Infinite* Card

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the TD First Class Travel® Visa Infinite* Card, the flagship travel credit card within the TD Rewards Points program.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 165,000 TD Rewards Points†

$900 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 20,000 TD Rewards Points upon making your first purchase†
  • Earn 145,000 TD Rewards Points upon spending $7,500 within 180 days of account opening†
  • Plus, earn a Birthday Bonus of up to 10,000 TD Rewards Points†
  • Plus, earn 8x TD Rewards Points† on eligible travel purchases when you book through Expedia® for TD†
  • Get an annual TD Travel Credit† of $100 when you book through Expedia® for TD†
  • Use your rewards for any travel bookings available on Expedia® for TD†
  • Four complimentary lounge visits through the Visa Airport Companion Program†
  • Minimum income: $60,000 personal or $100,000 household
  • Annual fee: $139, receive an annual fee rebate for the first year†

card_benefits.b_content:
20,000 TD Rewards Points upon first purchase<sup>†</sup>
145,000 TD Rewards Points upon spending $7,500 within 180 days of account opening<sup>†</sup>
Total of up to 165,000 TD Rewards Points<sup>†</sup>
Plus, earn a Birthday Bonus of up to 10,000 TD Rewards<sup>†</sup>
$139, rebated for the first year<sup>†</sup>

21.99% purchase
22.99% cash advance (21.99% in Quebec)
22.99% balance transfer (21.99% in Quebec)

8 TD Rewards Points per dollar spent on eligible travel booked through Expedia® for TD<sup>†</sup>
6 TD Rewards Points per dollar spent on eligible groceries, dining, and public transit purchases<sup>†</sup>
4 TD Rewards Points per dollar spent on eligible recurring bill payments, streaming, digital gaming, and media<sup>†</sup>
2 TD Rewards Points per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases<sup>†</sup>
Strong
Offer may be changed, withdrawn, or extended at any time and cannot be combined with any other offer unless otherwise indicated.

card_content.c_content: The TD First Class Travel® Visa Infinite* Card is one of the most popular credit cards that allows you to earn points in the bank's proprietary TD Rewards program. With regular high-volume welcome bonuses, a strong return on travel purchases booked through Expedia, and a competitive insurance package, the TD First Class Travel® Visa Infinite* Card is a solid choice to incorporate into your TD credit card strategy. [read-more ids="30107,30130"]

TD First Class Travel® Visa Infinite* Card is offering up to 165,000 TD Rewards Points† as a signup bonus, distributed as follows:
  • 20,000 TD Rewards Points upon first purchase†
  • 145,000 TD Rewards Points upon spending $7,500 within 180 days of account opening†
Additionally, you can earn a Birthday Bonus of up to 10,000 TD Rewards points† each year. Although this offer isn't the highest offer we've seen on this card, It's a great option for those who are looking for the best cash deals, or who want more flexibility with your points, since TD Rewards Points can be redeemed against cash travel bookings. The card has an annual fee of $139, which is rebated for the first year.† To be eligible for the first-year annual fee rebate, you must activate your card and make your first purchase within three months of account opening.† Additionally, holding a TD Unlimited or All-Inclusive chequing account will also provide an annual fee rebate on one TD credit card of your choice, including the TD First Class Travel® Visa Infinite* Card, on an ongoing basis. Supplementary cards cost $50 apiece,† and the current first-year fee annual fee rebate also applies to the first supplementary cardholder.†

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

We value TD Rewards Points at 0.5 cents per point, which makes the TD First Class Travel® Visa Infinite* Card a solid travel rewards card — particularly with its generous front-loaded welcome bonus and first-year annual fee rebate. The current welcome offer is structured as follows:
  • 20,000 TD Rewards upon first purchase = $100
  • 145,000 TD Rewards after spending $7,500 within 180 days = $725
Spending $7,500 at the base earn rate of 2 points per dollar spent earns an additional 15,000 TD Rewards Points, worth $75. That brings your total first-year value to $900. Since the $139 annual fee is rebated in the first year, your net first-year value is also $900. While TD Rewards aren’t as valuable on a per-point basis as airline miles, the flexibility to redeem for nearly any travel purchase via Expedia for TD — plus the low-effort earning structure — makes this card an attractive option for casual travellers. As always, our estimate reflects minimum value based on standard redemption rates. With strategic use or promotional offers, you may be able to squeeze a bit more out of your TD Rewards Points.

The card’s earning rates are as follows:
  • 8 TD Rewards Points† per dollar spent on travel booked through Expedia® for TD†
  • 6 TD Rewards Points† per dollar spent on groceries, dining, and public transit†
  • 4 TD Rewards Points† per dollar spent on recurring bills, digital gaming, streaming, and media†
  • 2 TD Rewards Points† per dollar spent on all other purchases†
Since TD Rewards Points are typically worth 0.5¢ each, this works out to a return of 4%, 3%, 2%, and 1%, respectively. Use the calculator below to estimate how many TD Rewards Points you can earn based on your monthly spending:

The best way to redeem TD Rewards Points is by booking travel through Expedia® for TD, an online travel portal operated in partnership with Expedia®. You can book flights, hotels, car rentals, vacation packages, and anything else that you would normally be able to book via Expedia®. You'd apply your TD Rewards Points to the purchase at a rate of 200 points = $1, or 0.5 cents per point. If you'd rather not book through Expedia® for TD, you can also redeem TD Rewards Points directly against any travel purchase that you purchase with your TD First Class Travel® Visa Infinite* Card. However, the rate isn't quite as competitive at 250 points = $1, or 0.4 cents per point. Redeeming for statement credits or gift cards is also possible, although the rate is even less appealing at 400 points = $1, or 0.25 cents per point. Generally speaking, you should always strive to redeem your TD Rewards Points for travel through Expedia® for TD whenever possible, in order to maximize their value. TD Rewards Points never expire as long as you're a cardholder.† If you cancel or switch your card to a different product, you'll have 90 days' time to redeem your TD Rewards Points before they go away. However, you'll lose the ability to book through Expedia® for TD for 0.5 cents per point, and you'll be limited to redeeming against any travel purchase for only 0.4 cents per point.

TD First Class Travel® Visa Infinite* cardholders are eligible to earn a $100 credit† on accommodations and vacation packages booked through Expedia® for TD.† This benefit is available annually to cardholders, and notably does not include flights. However, it certainly helps to offset the $139 annual fee that the card commands, beginning in the second year. You'll also get four complimentary lounge visits each year through the Visa Airport Companion Program, hosted by DragonPass.† Cardholders can also get car rental discounts at Avis and Budget: 10% in Canada or the US, and 5% internationally.†

As one of TD's flagship travel rewards credit cards, the TD First Class Travel® Visa Infinite* Card offers a strong set of insurance provisions. The card comes with 21 days of travel medical insurance of up to $2,000,000 for travellers aged under 65, and four days of coverage for travellers aged 65+.† There's also trip cancellation and trip interruption insurance of up to $1,500 and $5,000 per person, up to a maximum of $5,000 and $25,000 per trip, respectively.† While flying, cardholders are covered for up to $500 in flight delay insurance and an aggregate amount of $1,000 for lost, stolen, or delayed baggage.† And when making purchases, the card offers extended warranty of up to one additional year, as well as purchase protection that insures you against damage or theft of an item for up to 90 days after your purchase.† Lastly, it also comes with Mobile Device Insurance. By paying for your smartphone, tablet, or smartwatch using your TD First Class Travel Visa Infinite Card, you’ll get coverage of up to $1,000 in the event your mobile device is lost, stolen, or accidentally damaged, for up to 24 months!†

colum_block.col1_text:
As a Visa Infinite* product, this credit card has a minimum personal annual income of $60,000 or a minimum household income of $100,000 to be eligible. You must also be a Canadian resident, and the age of majority in your province/territory of residence.

Follow the link below to the TD website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers:
† Terms and conditions apply.

† The Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD) is not responsible for the contents of this site including any editorials or reviews that may appear on this site. For complete and current information on any TD product, please click the Apply Now button.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the TD® Rewards Visa Card, a no-fee credit card that earns modest rewards within the TD Rewards Points program.

acf.subtitle:
15,152 TD Rewards Points†

$75 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 15,152 TD Rewards Points when you spend $500 within 90 days of Account opening+†
  • Plus, earn 4x TD Rewards Points† on eligible travel purchases when you book through Expedia® for TD†
  • Use your rewards for any travel bookings available on Expedia® for TD†
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content:
15,152 TD Rewards Points when you spend $500 within 90 days of Account opening<sup>†</sup>
Offer may be changed, withdrawn, or extended at any time and cannot be combined with any other offer unless otherwise indicated<sup>†</sup>

21.99% purchase<sup>†</sup>
22.99% cash advance<sup>†</sup> (21.99% in Quebec)
22.99% balance transfe<sup>†</sup>r (21.99% in Quebec)

4 TD Rewards Points<sup>†</sup> per dollar spent on eligible Expedia® for TD purchases<sup>†</sup>
3 TD Rewards Points<sup>†</sup> per dollar spent on eligible groceries, dining, and public transit purchases<sup>†</sup>
2 TD Rewards Points<sup>†</sup> per dollar spent on eligible recurring bill payments, streaming, digital gaming, and media<sup>†</sup>
1 TD Rewards Point<sup>†</sup> per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases<sup>†</sup>
Basic

card_content.c_content: The TD Rewards Visa* Card is a basic no-fee credit card that lets you earn points in the bank's proprietary TD Rewards program. The card is a good fit for casual travellers who are looking to earn points consistently with a fixed and predictable rate of return, but without paying an annual fee. [read-more ids="30130"]

TD Rewards Visa* Card is offering 15,152 TD Rewards Points† as a signup bonus, when you spend $500 within 90 days of Account opening.† The card has no annual fee for primary or supplementary cardholders.†

The card's earning rates are as follows:
  • 4 TD Rewards Points† per dollar spent on eligible Expedia® for TD purchases†
  • 3 TD Rewards Points† per dollar spent on eligible groceries, dining, and public transit purchases†
  • 2 TD Rewards Points† per dollar spent on eligible recurring bill payments, streaming, digital gaming, and media†
  • 1 TD Rewards Point† per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases†
Since TD Rewards Points can be redeemed optimally at a value of 0.5 cents per point, these are equivalent to a 2%, 1.5%, 1%, and 0.5% return, respectively.

The best way to redeem TD Rewards Points is by booking travel through Expedia® for TD, an online travel portal operated in partnership with Expedia®. You can book flights, hotels, car rentals, vacation packages, and anything else that you would normally be able to book via Expedia®. You'd apply your TD Rewards Points to the purchase at a rate of 200 points = $1, or 0.5 cents per point. If you'd rather not book through Expedia® for TD, you can also redeem TD Rewards Points directly against any travel purchase that you purchase with your TD Rewards Visa* Card. However, the rate isn't quite as competitive at 250 points = $1, or 0.4 cents per point. Redeeming for statement credits, gift cards, or Amazon purchases is also possible, although the rate is even less appealing at 300-400 points = $1, or 0.25–0.33 cents per point. Generally speaking, you should always strive to redeem your TD Rewards Points for travel through Expedia® for TD whenever possible, in order to maximize their value. TD Rewards Points never expire as long as you're a cardholder. If you cancel or switch your card to a different product, you'll have 90 days' time to redeem your TD Rewards Points before they go away, although you'll lose the ability to book through Expedia® for TD for 0.5 cents per point, and you'll be limited to redeeming against any travel purchase for only 0.4 cents per point.
The TD Rewards Visa* Card comes with car rental discounts at Avis and Budget: 10% in Canada or the US, and 5% internationally.†

As a no-fee card, the TD Rewards Visa* Card is fairly weak when it comes to insurance coverage. In particular, there is no travel insurance of any kind. The only types of coverage are found when making purchases: cardholders will get extended warranty of up to one additional year†, as well as purchase protection that insures you against damage or theft of an item for up to 90 days after purchase.† Lastly, it also comes with Mobile Device Insurance. By paying for your smartphone, tablet, or smartwatch using your TD Rewards Visa* Card, you’ll get coverage of up to $1,000 in the event your mobile device is lost, stolen, or accidentally damaged, for up to 24 months!†

colum_block.col1_text:
As a basic no-fee product, this credit card has no minimum income requirement. You must also be a Canadian resident, and the age of majority in your province/territory of residence, to be eligible.

Follow the link below to the TD® website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers:
† Terms and conditions apply.

† The Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD) is not responsible for the contents of this site including any editorials or reviews that may appear on this site. For complete and current information on any TD product, please click the Apply Now button.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Aeroplan® Credit Card, a card that earns Aeroplan points.

acf.subtitle: Earn 60,000 points

features.feature: 3x points on groceries, dining & Air Canada purchases<sup>†</sup>
Preferred pricing on Aeroplan flight rewards<sup>†</sup>
Complimentary Aeroplan 25K Status through the following year<sup>†</sup>

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 60,000 bonus points after you spend $3,000 on purchases in the first 3 months your account is open†
  • Earn 3 points dollar spent at grocery stores†
  • Earn 3 points per dollar spent on dining, including takeout and eligible delivery services†
  • Preferred Aeroplan pricing and free checked bag on Air Canada® flights†
  • Global Entry, TSA PreCheck, or NEXUS application fee credit of up to $120 every 4 years†
  • No foreign transaction fees†
  • Annual fee: $95

card_benefits.b_content: This offer is available exclusively to U.S. residents
60,000 bonus points after you spend $3,000 on purchases in the first 3 months your account is open<sup>†</sup>
$95<sup>†</sup>

Ongoing Purchase APR: 19.99% – 28.49% Variable<sup>†</sup>
Ongoing Balance Transfer APR: 19.99% – 28.49% Variable<sup>†</sup>
Cash Advance APR: 28.74% Variable<sup>†</sup>

3 points per dollar spent at grocery stores<sup>†</sup>
3 points per dollar spent on dining at restaurants including takeout and eligible delivery services<sup>†</sup>
3 points per dollar spent on purchases made directly with Air Canada<sup>†</sup>
1 point per dollar spent on all other purchases<sup>†</sup>
Plus, earn 500 bonus points for every $2,000 you spend in a calendar month – up to 1,500 points per month<sup>†</sup>

Free first checked bag for you and up to 8 companions on the same Air Canada reservation<sup>†</sup>
Preferred pricing on Aeroplan redemptions<sup>†</sup>
Aeroplan Elite Status earning accelerators<sup>†</sup>
Global Entry, TSA PreCheck, or NEXUS application fee credit: up to $120 every 4 years<sup>†</sup>
No foreign transaction fees<sup>†</sup>
Comprehensive

card_content.c_content:
The Aeroplan® Credit Card is a great choice if you’re looking to maximize Aeroplan points and enjoy travel perks with Air Canada and its partners.

With a compelling welcome bonus, strong everyday earning categories, and a solid set of cardholder benefits, it stands out as a practical option for frequent travelers.

With the current offer on the Aeroplan® Credit Card, you can earn 60,000 bonus points<sup>†</sup> after spending $3,000 in the first three months of account opening<sup>†</sup>.

The annual fee is $95<sup>†</sup> and is not waived in the first year. You can also add authorized users at no extra cost<sup>†</sup>, making it easier for families or couples to pool spending towards bonus thresholds.

The Aeroplan<sup>®</sup> Credit Card earns 3 points per dollar spent at grocery stores, dining (including takeout and eligible delivery services), and purchases made directly with Air Canada.<sup>†</sup>

You'll earn 1 point per dollar spent on all other purchases.<sup>†</sup>

In addition, cardholders can collect 500 bonus points for every $2,000 spent each calendar month, up to 1,500 bonus points per month.<sup>†</sup>

This accelerator is an easy way to earn extra rewards if you regularly put significant spend on the card.

Aeroplan provides access to over 1,300 destinations worldwide through Air Canada and Star Alliance partners. Short-haul flights within North America can start from 6,000 points one-way, while long-haul flights are available at varying point levels under the Aeroplan Flight Reward Chart. As a cardholder, you’ll also benefit from preferred pricing on Air Canada flight rewards†, often requiring fewer points compared to members without the Aeroplan® Credit Card. [read-more ids="24541"]

The Aeroplan® Credit Card comes with a strong set of Air Canada travel benefits. As a cardholder, you’ll receive:
  • Free first checked bag on Air Canada flights for you and up to eight travel companions on the same booking†
  • No foreign transaction fees†
  • Aeroplan 25K Status for the remainder of the calendar year in which your account is opened, plus the following calendar year†
  • The ability to maintain 25K Status with $15,000 in annual spend, or earn a one-level Status Level Up with $50,000 in annual spend†
  • Up to a $120 statement credit every four years for Global Entry, TSA PreCheck, or NEXUS†
  • Complimentary DashPass membership for 12 months when activated by December 31, 2027†
  • A 10% bonus on Chase Ultimate Rewards transfers of 50,000 points or more†
Cardholders with high annual spending can also unlock extra rewards, including Priority Reward vouchers and the Global +1 companion benefit.†

The Aeroplan® Credit Card offers a wide range of insurance benefits, including:
  • Trip cancellation and trip interruption insurance: Reimburses up to $1,500 per covered traveler and $6,000 per trip for pre-paid, non-refundable passenger fares if your trip is cancelled or cut short by sickness, severe weather, or other covered situations†
  • Trip delay reimbursement: If your common carrier travel is delayed more than 12 hours or requires an overnight stay, you’re covered for expenses like meals and lodging up to $500 per covered traveler†
  • Baggage delay Insurance: Covers up to $100 a day for up to 3 days when your baggage is delayed more than 6 hours†
  • Auto rental collision damage waiver: Provides coverage for theft and collision damage when you decline the rental company’s insurance and charge the entire rental cost to your card†
  • Roadside assistance: Access to emergency help such as towing or lockout service, with fees billed to your card at time of dispatch†
  • Purchase protection: Covers eligible new purchases for 120 days against damage or theft, up to $500 per item†
These coverages provide added peace of mind whenever you travel, whether redeeming Aeroplan points or paying cash for flights. Coverage is subject to terms, conditions, and exclusions. See your Guide to Benefits for full details.

colum_block.col1_text: For more information about the Aeroplan® Credit Card, please click the 'Learn More' button.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. Advertiser Disclosure: The offers that appear on this site are from companies from which Prince of Travel receives compensation. Editorial Disclosure: Opinions expressed here are the author’s alone, not those of any bank, credit card issuer, airline, or hotel chain, and have not been reviewed, approved, or otherwise endorsed by any of these entities.
