excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card, a travel rewards card by CIBC that earns CIBC Aventura Points.

acf.subtitle:
60,000 CIBC Aventura Points†

$650 first-year value

features.feature: Annual fee rebated for the first year<sup>†</sup>

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 15,000 CIBC Aventura Points after making your first purchase†
  • Earn 30,000 CIBC Aventura Points after spending $3,000 in the first four monthly statement periods†
  • Earn 15,000 CIBC Aventura Points after spending $5,000 in the first four monthly statement periods†
  • Earn 2x CIBC Aventura Points on all eligible purchases through the CIBC Rewards Centre†
  • Earn 1.5x CIBC Aventura Points on eligible gas, groceries, and drugstore purchases†
  • Four complimentary lounge visits per year with the Visa Airport Companion Program†
  • NEXUS application fee rebate of up to $200 (CAD)†
  • 12-month Skip+ free trial and other exclusive benefits when you link your CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card with Skip†
  • Minimum income: $15,000 household†
  • Annual fee: $139 (rebated for the first year)†

card_benefits.b_content:
15,000 Aventura Points when you make your first purchase† 30,000 Aventura Points when you spend $3,000 or more in the first four monthly statement periods† 15,000 Aventura Points when you spend $5,000 or more in the first four monthly statement periods† Total of up to 60,000 Aventura Points†
12-month Skip+ free trial and other exclusive benefits when you link your CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card with Skip†
$139 (rebated in the first year<sup>†</sup>)

21.99% on purchases<sup>†</sup>
22.99% on cash advances<sup>†</sup> (21.99% for Quebec residents)
22.99% on balance transfers<sup>†</sup> (21.99% for Quebec residents)

2 Aventura Points per dollar spent on eligible travel purchases via the CIBC Rewards Centre<sup>†</sup>
1.5 Aventura Points per dollar spent on eligible gas stations, grocery stores, EV charging, and drugstores<sup>†</sup>
1 Aventura Point per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases<sup>†</sup>

Four lounge visits through Visa Airport Companion Program<sup>†</sup>
NEXUS application fee rebate of up to $200 (CAD)<sup>†</sup>
EV charging multiplier<sup>†</sup>
Comprehensive

card_content.c_content:
The CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card is a near identical copy of the CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite* Card, with a lower minimum income requirement and less travel insurance.

This card is right for you if you have a more modest income and want to earn Aventura Points to put towards travel.

The CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card is currently offering a bonus of up to 60,000 Aventura Points,† structured as follows:

  • 15,000 Aventura points when you make your first purchase†
  • 30,000 Aventura points when you spend $3,000 (CAD) or more in the first four monthly statement periods†
  • 15,000 Aventura points when you spend $5,000 (CAD) or more in the first four monthly statement periods†

The $139 annual fee is currently rebated in the first year†; otherwise, if that offer weren’t available, it can be rebated if you have a CIBC Smart Plus Chequing Account.2

Plus, you can enjoy a first-year annual fee rebate for up to three supplementary cardholders as part of the current offer, that values up $150 in savings.†

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

We value CIBC Aventura Points at 1 cent per point, which makes the CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa Card* a solid option for those looking to earn flexible travel rewards — especially with the first-year annual fee fully rebated. The current welcome bonus is structured as follows:
  • 15,000 Aventura Points upon first purchase = $150
  • 30,000 Aventura Points after spending $3,000 in the first four statement periods = $300
  • 15,000 Aventura Points after spending $5,000 in the first four statement periods = $150
  • Total: 60,000 Aventura Points, worth $600
Spending $3,000 at the base earn rate of 1 point per dollar earns you an additional 5,000 points, worth $50. This brings your total first-year value to $650. And since the $139 annual fee is rebated in the first year, your net first-year value is also $650. Of course, this is a conservative estimate based on minimum spend and baseline earn rates. Your value could be even higher if you redeem Aventura Points strategically or take advantage of other card benefits like insurance and airport lounges access.

This credit card earns CIBC Aventura Points on daily spending at the following rates:
  • Earn 2 Aventura Points per dollar spent on eligible travel booked through the CIBC Rewards Centre†
  • Earn 1.5 Aventura Points per dollar spent on eligible gas stations, grocery stores, electric vehicle charging, and drugstores†
  • Earn 1 Aventura Point per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases†

CIBC Aventura Points can be redeemed in a variety of ways. However, the most valuable way to redeem Aventura Points is on travel purchases. For example, you can redeem Aventura Points for eligible flights booked through the CIBC Rewards Centre at a rate of 1 cent per point. You can also use CIBC Aventura Points towards an $800 (CAD) airline ticket in the “Canada and U.S. (Long-Haul)” category on the Aventura Flight Rewards Chart, which could increase the value of your points from 1 cent per point (cpp) to 2.28 cents per point. Generally speaking, it's recommended to redeem Aventura Points via the Aventura Flight Rewards Chart whenever possible, since it offers the maximum value for your points. It's worth noting that this must be done through the CIBC Rewards Centre. [read-more title="Read More" ids="159973"] However, Aventura Points can also be useful for offsetting the cost of travel that's otherwise difficult to book with points. For example, Aventura Points can be used to cover the cost of eligible independent hotels, short-term rentals, car rentals, trains, or flights that can't be booked with other airline- or hotel-specific loyalty programs. [read-more title="Read More" ids="81635" ]

As the primary cardholder, you’ll enjoy four complimentary lounge visits per membership year for you and any guests, which can be used at 1,200+ lounges globally through the Visa Airport Companion Program.† Four lounge visits per year can be useful if you don't tend to travel very often and don't otherwise have lounge access with another credit card. Another unique feature is the NEXUS application fee rebate, which provides one rebate of up to $200 (CAD) every four years towards the cost of your NEXUS application.† NEXUS is a frequent traveller program designed to speed up your travels between Canada and the United States, and you’ll receive a full rebate simply by charging the application fee to your CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card. You’ll also enjoy a 12-month free trial of Skip+ and other exclusive benefits when you link your CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card with your Skip account.† Plus, cardholders can save up to 10¢ per litre at participating Pioneer, Fas Gas, Ultramar, and Chevron gas stations.† To benefit from this discount, you'll need to link your card with Journie Rewards, and then make qualifying purchases at eligible gas stations.†

The CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card is effectively a version of the CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite* Card for those who don't meet the higher income requirements. In fact, insurance is the only major aspect in which these two cards differ.

The CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card features out-of-province emergency medical insurance of up to $5,000,000,† plus coverage for flight and baggage delays,† hotel burglary,† and car rental loss or damage.† It's worth noting that the CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card doesn't feature Trip Cancellation/Trip Interruption insurance, or emergency medical insurance for anyone over the age of 64, which are offered on the CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite* Card.†

There’s also Mobile Device Insurance available to cardholders.† By paying for your smartphone, tablet, or smartwatch using your CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card, you’ll get coverage of up to $1,000 in the event your mobile device is lost, stolen, or accidentally damaged, for up to 24 months!†

That can be beneficial for many people, and so the next time you’re in the market for a new mobile device, it’s worth considering using the CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card to fund your purchase.

colums.col2_text:
As a Visa product, this credit card has a minimum household income of $15,000.

Follow the below link to the CIBC website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

There’s also a higher-tier card called the CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite* Card with many of the same benefits described above. There's a higher income requirement of either $60,000 (personal) or $100,000 (household), but slightly stronger insurance coverage.

Follow the below link to the CIBC website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. The information for the CIBC Aventura® Gold Visa* Card has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Scotiabank Gold American Express Card, a powerful credit card issued by Scotiabank with 5x points on food and drinks.

acf.subtitle:
45,000 Scene+ points

$405 first-year value

features.feature: 5x points on groceries, dining, and entertainment

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 25,000 Scene+ points upon spending $2,000 in the first three months
  • Plus, earn an additional 20,000 Scene+ points upon spending $7,500 in the first year
  • Earn 6x Scene+ points at Sobeys, IGA, Safeway, FreshCo, and more
  • Plus, earn 5x Scene+ points on groceries, dining, and entertainment
  • Also, earn 3x Scene+ points on gas, transit, and select streaming services
  • Redeem points for a statement credit for any travel expense
  • No foreign transaction fees
  • Enjoy the exclusive benefits of being an American Express cardholder
  • Annual fee: $120 (waived for the first year)

card_benefits.b_title: Best offer is available until July 1, 2026<sup>†</sup>

card_benefits.b_content:
25,000 Scene+ points upon spending $2,000 in the first three months<sup>†</sup>
20,000 Scene+ points upon spending $7,500 in the first year†
Total of up to 45,000 Scene+ points<sup>†</sup>

20.99% purchases
22.99% cash advances
22.99% balance transfers

6 Scene+ points per dollar spent on Sobeys, IGA, Safeway, FreshCo, and more<sup>†</sup>
5 Scene+ points per dollar spent on groceries, dining, and entertainment<sup>†</sup>
3 Scene+ points per dollar spent on gas, transit, and streaming services<sup>†</sup>
1 Scene+ point per dollar spent on all other purchases†
Comprehensive

card_content.c_content:
The Scotiabank Gold American Express® Card is an excellent card in terms of its welcome bonus, earning rates on everyday spending, no foreign transaction fees, and more.

This card is suitable for a wide swath of people, and it's one of the strongest all-around credit cards in Canada if you're looking to save money on travel.

[read-more ids="30129,30104,30102,30107"]

The Scotiabank Gold American Express® Card is offering up to 45,000 Scene+ points for new cardholders,† structured as follows:

  • 25,000 Scene+ points upon spending $2,000 in the first three months†
  • 20,000 Scene+ points upon spending $7,500 in the first year†
Overall, this is a solid welcome offer—though not necessarily the strongest we’ve seen. In the past, the card has reached as high as 50,000 points with a first-year fee waiver, so this one sits more in the “decent” category rather than a must-jump-on deal. Still, the second spending threshold is very achievable, and the card’s strong everyday earning rates make it easy to work toward organically.

There's an annual fee of $120, and supplementary cards cost $29 apiece.†

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

Here's a breakdown of what the welcome bonus for the Scotiabank Gold American Express® Card is worth. Keep in mind that this doesn't take into consideration the potential value of other card benefits, which we'll outline in detail below. We value Scene+ points at 1 cent per point, and using that value, we'd peg a value of $450 on the welcome bonus alone. Plus, assuming the card's baseline earning rate of 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent, you'd earn an additional 7,500 Scene+ points for meeting the $7,500 total minimum spending requirement in the first year. We'd value these points at an additional $75. That brings the total first-year value to $525, and after accounting for the $120 annual fee, your net return works out to $405 in year one. Of course, you can certainly squeeze more value out of the card by leveraging the category earning rates and the card's other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value.

This card is an excellent choice for everyday spending. You’ll earn 5 Scene+ points per dollar spent at grocery stores, food and drink, and entertainment venues.† That’s pretty much equivalent to 5% cash back on these categories, which is a fantastic proposition.

In addition to the strong 5x earning rate, as part of a partnership with Empire grocery stores, you can earn 6 Scene+ points per dollar spent on Empire grocery stores such as Sobeys, IGA, Safeway, Foodland, FreshCo, and more.† This is a very impressive earning rate, and is on par with the highest everyday earning rate in a specific merchant category. You'll also earn 4 Scene+ points per dollar spent on hotel bookings, car rentals, and "things to do" booked through Scene+ Travel.†

Meanwhile, you’ll earn 3 Scene+ points per dollar spent on gas, daily transit, and streaming services, which is a highly competitive earning rate for some spending categories that otherwise don’t get much coverage.†

On all other spending, you’ll earn 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent.†

It’s important to note that the accelerated earning rates are capped at $50,000 per calendar year. After reaching that threshold, you’ll earn 1 Scene+ point per dollar spent.

To get a better idea on how much you can earn each month with this card, feel free to use our rewards calculator below.

Scene+ is one of the most flexible fixed-value points programs in Canada. Points have a value of 1 cent per point, and can be easily credited towards any travel expense up to 12 months after the date of purchase.

This is far superior to fixed-value points programs from Canada's other major banks, as most of them force you to book your travel through their in-house travel agencies. With those programs, you’re effectively limited to using your points for flights, hotels, car rentals, and vacations at the rates set in the online travel agencies.

On the other hand, with Scene+ points, you can apply your points to things like vacation rentals, plane tickets, train tickets, hotel stays, award booking taxes and fees, and much more. Simply charge the travel expense to your credit card, and then redeem points to "offset" the charge.

The best part of this is that since you can book directly with travel vendors, you'll get the full entitlement to earning elite benefits, and you'll also be covered by the card's insurance coverage (outlined below).
Your Scene+ points won’t expire for as long as you’re a cardholder. If you cancel the card, you’ll have 60 days to redeem your Scene+ points before they’re gone.

This card has no foreign transaction fees,† an area where Scotiabank leads the Big 5 by a wide margin. However, you'll only earn 1 point per dollar spent on purchases made in foreign currencies, and Amex isn't accepted quite as widely around the world as other cards. Despite not being issued by American Express the lender, this card still gets many of the perks of being on the American Express payment network. You'll be able to register for some Amex Offers including Shop Small, use Amex Front of the Line for presale event tickets, and use American Express Invites for exclusive experiences.† Be sure to keep an eye out on the Amex Offers website, and then manually register your card if you see anything that catches your eye.

The insurance on the Scotiabank Gold American Express® Card is as strong as you'd expect for a premium travel credit card. Coverage includes:

  • The essential emergency medical insurance of up to $1 million for you, your spouse, and dependent children on unexpected medical illness or injury when travelling out-of-province for up to 25 days (3 days for age 65 and up)†
  • Trip cancellation and interruption insurance of up to $1,500 per person†
  • Flight delay insurance for delays of more than four hours, up to $500 per person†
  • Delayed and lost baggage insurance of up to $1,000†
  • Common carrier travel accident insurance of up to $500,000 per insured person and up to $1,000,000 per occurrence, when you charge at least 75% of your trip to your card†
  • Rental car collision or damage insurance, which can help you save on the insurance fees usually charged by car rental companies for vehicles with a MSRP of $65,000†
  • Hotel burglary insurance of up to $1,000†
  • Mobile device insurance of up to $1,000, if the device is lost, stolen, or accidentally damaged†

Plus, on the purchase protection side, you’ll get a 90-day protection period against damage, loss, or theft for most new items. In addition, you also get extended warranty of up to one year.†

colum_block.col1_text:
You'll need to have a minimum personal annual income of at least $12,000 to be eligible for the Scotiabank Gold American Express Card, and you'll need to be approved for a credit limit of at least $5,000 to be eligible.

Follow the link below to the Scotiabank website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. See Card Provider's website and Card Application for complete card details, terms and current offers. Reasonable efforts are made to maintain accuracy of information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®*, the flagship VIPorter co-branded credit card issued by BMO.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 70,000 VIPorter points

$735 first-year value

features.feature: Free carry-on bag and checked bag on Porter flights

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 20,000 VIPorter points upon spending $5,000 in the first 110 days
  • Plus, earn an additional 20,000 VIPorter points upon spending $9,000 in the first 180 days
  • Plus, earn an additional 30,000 VIPorter points upon spending $18,000 in the first 365 days
  • Then, earn 3x VIPorter points per dollar spent on Porter purchases
  • And, earn 2x VIPorter points per dollar spent on gas, transportation, dining, groceries, and hotels
  • Get one free checked bag and one free carry-on bag for yourself and up to eight guests on the same booking
  • Priority airport services & priority rebooking in the event of delays
  • Earn Qualifying Spend towards Avid Traveller status
  • Annual fee: $199 (rebated in the first year)

card_benefits.b_content:
Earn 20,000 VIPorter points upon spending $5,000 in the first 110 days
Earn 20,000 VIPorter points upon spending $9,000 in the first 180 days
Earn 30,000 VIPorter points upon spending $18,000 in the first 365 days
Total of up to 70,000 VIPorter points
$199 (rebated in the first year)

21.99% purchases
23.99% cash advances (21.99% for Quebec residents)
23.99% balance transfers (21.99% for Quebec residents)

3 VIPorter points per dollar spent on Porter purchases
2 VIPorter points per dollar spent on gas, transportation, dining, groceries, and hotels
1 VIPorter point per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases

Free first checked bag for cardholder and up to eight guests on the same booking
Free carry-on bag for cardholder and up to eight guests on the same booking
Complimentary PorterClassic seat selection (rows 8+) for up to two passengers
Priority airport services
Priority rebooking in the event of delays
Six months of Instacart+ and a $10 monthly Instacart credit on orders of $50 or more
Comprehensive

card_content.c_content: The BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®* is a must-have card for frequent flyers with Porter Airlines, and there's a strong argument to be made that it deserves a spot in many Canadians' wallets due to its strong welcome bonus, generous cardholder benefits, and more.

The BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®* is currently offering a welcome bonus of up to 70,000 VIPorter points, which are awarded in three swaths broken down as follows:
  • Earn 20,000 VIPorter points upon spending $5,000 in the first 110 days
  • Earn 20,000 VIPorter points upon spending $9,000 in the first 180 days
  • Earn 30,000 VIPorter points upon spending $18,000 in the first 365 days
In order to fulfill the full minimum spending requirement of $18,000 in the first 365 days, you'll need to spend an average of $1,500 per month. The final allotment of 30,000 VIPorter points is awarded in your second year as a cardholder. This card comes with an annual fee of $199, which is rebated in the first year as part of this offer.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

Here's a breakdown of what the welcome bonus for the BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®* is worth. Keep in mind that this doesn't take into consideration the potential value of other card benefits, which we'll outline in detail below. We value VIPorter points at 1.5 cents per point, and using that value, we'd peg a value of $1,050 on the welcome bonus alone. Plus, assuming the card's baseline earning rate of 1 VIPorter point per dollar spent, you'd earn an additional 18,000 VIPorter points for meeting the minimum spending requirement. We'd value these points at an additional $270. Therefore, in the first two years as a cardholder, the minimum amount of net value you can expect to get from this card's welcome bonus is $1,121: $1,050 from the 70,000 points from the welcome bonus, $270 by spending the bare minimum to earn the welcome bonus (and assuming the card's baseline earning rate), and then minus the card's annual fee of $199 in the second year. Of course, you can certainly squeeze more value out of the card by leveraging the category earning rates and the card's other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value.

The BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®* has a 3-2-1 earning structure, which allows you to earn VIPorter points on your daily spending that you can then redeem for travel with Porter Airlines and its partner airlines. The card's earning rates are broken down as follows:
  • Earn 3 VIPorter points per dollar spent on Porter purchases
  • Earn 2 VIPorter points per dollar spent on gas, transportation, dining, groceries, and hotels
  • Earn 1 VIPorter point per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases
It's important to note that there are spending caps in the elevated category earning rates, which are calculated based on the calendar year. The spending caps are as follows:
  • Earn 3 VIPorter points per dollar spent on up to $20,000 of Porter purchases (and earn 1 VIPorter point per dollar spent thereafter)
  • Earn 2 VIPorter points per dollar spent on up to $5,000 of gas & transportation purchases (and earn 1 VIPorter point per dollar spent thereafter)
  • Earn 2 VIPorter points per dollar spent on up to $10,000 of dining and grocery purchases (and earn 1 VIPorter point per dollar spent thereafter)
  • Earn 2 VIPorter points per dollar spent on up to $5,000 of hotel purchases (and earn 1 VIPorter point per dollar spent thereafter)
If you're someone who may tend to max out any of the elevated category earning rates each year, be sure to consider switching your purchases to a different card once you've hit the cap to ensure you're getting the best return on your spending. If you’d like to estimate how much value you’ll earn each month, feel free to use our rewards calculator below.

You'll earn VIPorter points on the BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®*, which can be redeemed for flights with Porter Airlines and its partners. The VIPorter program doesn't publish an award chart, which means that you might see different prices for the same flight, depending on the demand. Plus, you aren't able to redeem them against the taxes and fees for your booking, so be prepared to pay a modest amount on each flight, which usually doesn't exceed $100 or so. Porter Airlines has a growing network of flights across Canada and the United States, and it's likely that its route map will continue to grow as the airline expands its fleet. Here's an idea of some of the lowest-level prices you can expect to pay for one-way flights using VIPorter points:
  • Toronto–New York: 5,000 VIPorter points
  • Toronto–Edmonton: 8,000 VIPorter points
  • Toronto–Halifax: 7,000 VIPorter points
  • Toronto–Vancouver: 8,000 VIPorter points
  • Toronto–Miami: 7,000 VIPorter points
  • Toronto–Los Angeles: 7,000 VIPorter points
One of the best ways to squeeze extra value out of your VIPorter points is to use the Points + Cash option, which lets you pay more in cash and less in points on redemptions. In some cases, the value of your VIPorter points can exceed 5 or even 6 cents per point, which is four times our base valuation! If you're ready to make a redemption, be sure to compare all options at your disposal, and also check to see what the flight's corresponding cash value is to make sure you're happy with the value from your points.

The BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®* comes packed with a number of perks & benefits which are mostly specific to Porter Airlines. As a cardholder, you'll automatically be granted VIPorter Venture Avid Traveller status. With Venture Avid Traveller status, you'll enjoy a free carry-on bag with any Porter Airlines fare, as well as a free checked bag. Better yet, these perks apply for you and up to eight guests travelling with you on the same Porter Airlines booking. You'll also enjoy free PorterClassic seat selection for yourself and up to one other passenger, which means you can choose a pair of seats in row 8 and beyond at no extra cost. In the event of flight delays, you'll get priority re-accommodation over other passengers, and you'll also have priority check-in, security, and early boarding as a cardholder. In the first year as a cardholder, you'll earn a round-trip companion pass upon spending $9,000 within the first 180 days after the account open date. Then, in subsequent years, you can earn one by spending $50,000 on your card each calendar year. The round-trip companion pass gives a second passenger a $0 base fare on a booking. When used strategically, this can be a valuable benefit, especially during peak travel periods and on more expensive fares. If you fly with Porter Airlines often, your credit card spending will give your Avid Traveller status a boost, since you'll earn $1 in Qualifying Spend for every $25 spent on your card. Plus, you'll get an additional boost of $1,000 in Qualifying Spend in the first year as a cardholder after spending $9,000 on the card. This card also comes with a complimentary lounge membership in the Mastercard Travel Pass program, powered by DragonPass. Each lounge visit you make will cost $32 (USD). Your VIPorter points will never expire as long as you're a cardholder. You'll also enjoy six months of Instacart+ and a $10 monthly Instacart credit when you enroll with BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®*

The BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®* also includes Instacart-related benefits, which add real, everyday value if you already use grocery delivery.

Primary cardholders are eligible for:

  • Six months of complimentary Instacart+ membership
  • $10 Instacart credit each month, valid on orders of $50 or more
  • Credits apply to non-alcohol purchases only and do not roll over month to month

As a premium co-branded travel card, the BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®* comes packed with a strong suite of insurance benefits. Simply charge your travel or eligible purchases to the card to take advantage of the following coverages:
  • Emergency medical insurance: Up to $5 million of coverage for the first 21 consecutive days of your out-of-province or out-of-country trip, for travellers aged 64 or under.
  • Trip cancellation: Up to $1,500 per insured person, to a maximum of $5,000 total per trip for all insured persons, when your trip is cancelled for an eligible reason.
  • Trip interruption insurance: Up to $2,000 per insured person, to a maximum of $10,000 total per trip for all insured persons, if your trip is disrupted due to a covered event.
  • Flight delay insurance: Up to $500 total per trip for all insured persons, for expenses like meals and accommodation when your flight is delayed by 4+ hours.
  • Baggage delay insurance: Up to $500 per insured person, to a maximum of $1,000 total per trip for all insured persons, for essential items purchased when baggage is delayed 6+ hours.
  • Lost or stolen baggage insurance: Up to $500 per insured person, to a maximum of $1,000 total per trip for all insured persons, for lost, stolen, or damaged checked or carry-on baggage.
  • Hotel burglary insurance: Up to $1,000 total per incident for stolen or damaged personal property due to burglary at your travel accommodation.
  • Travel accident insurance (Common Carrier): Up to $500,000 of Accidental Death and Dismemberment Insurance while travelling as a passenger on a licensed common carrier.
  • Car rental theft and damage insurance: Comprehensive coverage for up to 48 consecutive days for rental vehicles with an MSRP up to $65,000, including loss, damage, towing, and loss-of-use charges.
Additionally, the card also offers Purchase Security Insurance and Extended Warranty Insurance:
  • Purchase protection covers theft or damage of eligible items for up to 90 days after purchase.
  • Extended warranty doubles the manufacturer’s warranty for up to 1 additional year.
  • Combined lifetime coverage across both is capped at $60,000 per account.

colum_block.col1_text: As a World Elite®* Mastercard®* product, this credit card has a minimum personal annual income requirement of $80,000 or a minimum household income of $150,000. You'll also need to be a Canadian resident and of the age of majority in your province/territory of residence to be eligible.

acf.legal_disclaimers: * Terms and conditions apply. BMO is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the BMO eclipse rise Visa* Card, a no annual fee products that earns up to 5x BMO Rewards points per $2 spent.

acf.subtitle: Up to 20,000 BMO Rewards points

features.feature: No annual fee + free supplementary cards
5x the points per $2 spent on recurring bill payments, groceries, and restaurants
Mobile device, purchase protection, and extended warranty insurance

acf.featured_info:
• Get 20,000 BMO Rewards points when you spend $1,500 in the first 3 months
  • Plus earn up to 5,000 bonus BMO Rewards points every year: 2,500 bonus BMO Rewards points for redeeming at least 12,000 points annually towards your statement balance with Pay with points - that's just 1,000 points per month.
  • 2,500 bonus BMO Rewards points for paying your full credit card balance on time for 12 consecutive months.

card_benefits.b_content:
Get 20,000 BMO Rewards points when you spend $1,500 in the first 3 months
Plus earn up to 5,000 bonus points every year:
2,500 bonus BMO Rewards points for redeeming at least 12,000 points annually towards your statement balance with Pay with points 
2,500 bonus BMO Rewards points for paying your full credit card balance on time for 12 consecutive months.
Total of 25,000 BMO Rewards points

21.99% purchases
23.99% cash advances (21.99% for Quebec residents)
23.99% balance transfers (21.99% for Quebec residents)

5 BMO Rewards points per $2 spent on eligible recurring bill payments, groceries, dining, and takeout
1 BMO Rewards point per $2 spent on all other eligible purchases

Three months of complimentary Instacart+ membership
$5 Instacart credit each month, valid on orders of $35 or more
Limited

card_content.c_content: BMO’s newest credit card offering is the BMO eclipse rise Visa* Card. With a low barrier to acceptance, the card is ideal if you're looking to get into the BMO credit cards ecosystem and work your way up to more premium cards. Plus, with no annual fee, the card can be used to continue a longstanding relationship with BMO. [read-more title="Read More" ids="30114"]

Despite being a no annual fee card, the BMO eclipse rise Visa* Card offers up to 20,000 BMO Rewards points as a welcome bonus. All you need to do to get those points is to spend $1,500 in the first three months of holding the card. With our valuation of BMO Rewards points set at 0.67 cents per point, the welcome bonus is approximately worth $134. In addition to the points, you’ll receive an introductory 0.99% interest rate on balance transfers for nine months with a 2% transfer fee should you need it. The primary card and its supplementary cards have no annual fee. Since it’s a no annual fee product, you can keep it open as a primary or backup credit card, all while supporting the longevity of your credit history.

The BMO eclipse rise Visa* Card earns BMO Rewards points as follows:
  • 5 BMO Rewards points per $2 spent on eligible recurring bill payments, groceries, and restaurants
  • 1 BMO Rewards point per $2 spent on all other eligible purchases
Given that BMO Rewards points are 0.67 cents apiece, the 5x accelerated earn rate on eligible categories corresponds to a 1.68% return, which is good for a no annual fee card. However, it bears repeating that the accelerated earn rate is per $2 spent, not per dollar spent. For all other purchases, the return is approximately 0.34%, which is weaker than most cards. Hence, it may be prudent to use this card mainly for the accelerated earn categories then consider a different no-fee card for all other purchases.

BMO Rewards points are worth 0.67 cents per point when redeemed against the cost of travel booked via the BMO Rewards online portal (at a fixed rate of 150 BMO Rewards points = $1). BMO Rewards points can also be redeemed at 0.5 cents per point against any statement purchase (at the rate of 200 BMO Rewards points = $1). However, the value in doing so is clearly not as strong, so you should always aim to use your BMO Rewards points to book travel.

Annually, you may receive a bonus of up to 5,000 BMO Rewards points broken down as follows:
  • 2,500 BMO Rewards points if you pay your balance in full for 12 consecutive months
  • 2,500 BMO Rewards points if you annually redeem at least 12,000 points as a statement balance using the Pay with Points feature

The BMO eclipse rise Visa* Card also includes Instacart-related benefits, which add real, everyday value if you already use grocery delivery.

Primary cardholders are eligible for:

  • Three months of complimentary Instacart+ membership
  • $5 Instacart credit each month, valid on orders of $35 or more
  • Credits apply to non-alcohol purchases only and do not roll over month to month

The card offers the following insurance benefits:
  • Mobile device insurance of up to $1,000, protecting against loss, theft, or accidental damage
  • Purchase security insurance in case of theft or damage for up to 90 days following the purchase date
  • Extended warranty insurance that doubles the manufacturer's warranty for up to one year

colum_block.col1_text:
As a basic no annual fee product, the BMO eclipse rise Visa* Card has no minimum income requirement.

Follow the link below to the BMO website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers:
†Terms and conditions apply. BMO is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site.

Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the BMO CashBack® Mastercard®*, a no-fee cash back credit card by BMO that earns modest rewards on daily spending.

acf.subtitle:
5% cash back

$125 first-year value

features.feature: Up to 20% off National and Alamo Car Rentals

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 5% cash back in the first three months, up to $2,500 spent ($500 on groceries, $500 on recurring bill payments, and $1,500 on everything else)†
  • Then, earn 3% cash back on groceries†
  • Up to 20% discount on car rentals at National and Alamo
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content: 5% cash back in the first three months, up to $125 total cash back for spending $2,500†

21.99% purchases
23.99% cash advances (21.99% for Quebec residents)
23.99% balance transfers (21.99% for Quebec residents)

3% cash back on groceries
1% cash back on recurring payments
0.5% cash back on all other purchases

Discounts at National Car Rental, Alamo Rent A Car, and Enterprise Rent-A-Car<sup>®††</sup>
Three months of complimentary Instacart+ membership
$5 Instacart credit each month, valid on orders of $35 or more
Basic

card_content.c_content: The BMO CashBack® Mastercard®* is a solid choice for anyone who prefers cash back and spends a fair amount every month at grocery stores. Its 3% return on groceries is outstanding for a no-fee card, and the flexibility of BMO's cash back redemption options also counts in its favour. However, the excellent 3% earning rate only applies on the first $500 in groceries per month, so you may wish to compare this card against other options if your spending volume is higher. [read-more ids="30114"]

As a welcome bonus, the BMO CashBack® Mastercard®* offers 5% cash back on $2,500 spent within the first three months, up to a total of $125 in cash back rewards.† The breakdown of the welcome bonus cash back offer is as follows:
  • 5% cash back on up to $500 spent on groceries in the first three months†
  • 5% cash back on up to $500 spent on recurring bill payments in the first three months†
  • 5% cash back up on to $1,500 spent on all other qualifying purchases in the first three months†
The card has no annual fee, making it a great choice to keep around as a free primary or backup credit card, all while supporting the longevity of your credit history. You could also downgrade to this card after collecting the bonus on a higher-tier BMO CashBack® credit card to preserve your account history. It's also free to add authorized users. Get a 0.99% introductory interest rate on Balance Transfers for 9 months, 2% fee applies to balance amounts transferred*

The BMO CashBack® Mastercard®* earns cash back at the following rates:
  • 3% cash back on groceries
  • 1% cash back on recurring bill payments
  • 0.5% cash back on all other purchases.
A 3% return on groceries is a ridiculously high rate for a no-fee card, and even higher than many premium cards out there. The 1% cash back on recurring payments is also a fairly competitive rate for a no-fee product. However, you’ll only get the bonus rates on your first $500 spent per month. The card isn’t great for high spenders, but if you’re in the market for a card with no annual fee, you’re probably pretty budget-conscious to begin with. The 0.5% cash back rate for uncategorized purchases is rather uninspiring, but it isn’t too far off of the value of the rewards on BMO’s other no-fee cards. All other things being equal, cash back is certainly a more versatile rewards currency, and a strong grocery category only sweetens the deal.

There are three ways to redeem your cash back on the BMO CashBack® Mastercard®*:
  • Deposit into a BMO chequing or savings account
  • Deposit into a BMO InvestorLine account
  • Redeem as a credit against your credit card's statement
Any of these three options can be requested at any time when you've earned at least $1 of cash back. You can also set up automatic payments, which will automatically trigger one of the above three redemption options every time you reach $25 of cash back. BMO's cash back redemption options are relatively flexible compared to its peers, many of which only allow you to claim your cash back earnings once a year.

As a no-fee card, the BMO CashBack® Mastercard®* comes with relatively few perks. Save up to 20% on National Car Rental®†† and Alamo Rent A Car®†† and up to 5% on Enterprise Rent-A-Car®†† at locations worldwide using the Car Rental Booking tool partners.rentalcar.com/bmo.

The card also includes Instacart-related benefits, which add real, everyday value if you already use grocery delivery.

Primary cardholders are eligible for:

  • Three months of complimentary Instacart+ membership
  • $5 Instacart credit each month, valid on orders of $35 or more
  • Credits apply to non-alcohol purchases only and do not roll over month to month

Insurance benefits on the BMO CashBack® Mastercard®* are also limited. The card does not offer any types of travel insurance; instead, its coverage is limited to extended warranty (doubling the manufacturer's standard warranty, up to one additional year) and purchase protection (against accidental damage or theft within 90 days).

colum_block.col1_text:
As a basic no-fee product, this credit card has no minimum income requirement.

Consider supporting Prince of Travel by applying for the card via our affiliate link below.

acf.legal_disclaimers: † Terms and conditions apply. BMO is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

Up to $480 cash back

$480 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Earn $40 cash back per month in your first year, up to $480 total, when you spend at least $2,000 per billing cycle*
  • Then, earn 1%–5% cash back for different categories*
  • Complimentary six months of Instacart+ and a $10 monthly Instacart credit
  • Minimum income: $80,000 personal or $150,000 household*
  • Annual fee: $120 (rebated in the first year)*

card_benefits.b_content: $40 cash back per month in the first year, up to $480 total, when you spend at least $2,000 per billing cycle
$120 (rebated in the first year)*

21.99% purchases
23.99% cash advances (21.99% for Quebec residents)
23.99% balance transfers (21.99% for Quebec residents)

5% cash back on groceries*
4% cash back on transit*
3% cash back on gas and EV charging*
2% cash back on recurring bill payments*
1% cash back on all other purchases*

15% off Cirque du Soleil's Canadian tours and 20% off resident shows in Las Vegas*
Complimentary basic BMO Roadside Assistance Program
Six months of complimentary Instacart+ membership
$10 Instacart credit each month, valid on orders of $50 or more
Comprehensive

card_content.c_content: The BMO CashBack® World Elite®* Mastercard®* is a solid cash back option for higher-income Canadians who don’t spend heavily in any single category and can make consistent use of the card’s elevated earn rates within their monthly caps. While the structure looks generous on paper, this card rewards controlled, predictable spending rather than big monthly outlays. [read-more ids="30114"]

As a welcome bonus, this card is currently offering up to $480 in cash back, credited as a monthly cash back bonus structure. You’ll earn $40 cash back each month in your first year, up to $480 total, as long as you spend at least $2,000 per billing cycle. This is effectively a monthly loyalty bonus rather than a traditional upfront welcome offer. The annual fee of $120 is also rebated in the first year.* You can give the card a try at no cost before deciding whether to commit long-term, with the option to downgrade to the no-fee version if you’d prefer.* Supplementary cardholders come at a cost of $50 apiece.*

The BMO CashBack® World Elite®* Mastercard®* has a 5-4-3-2-1 tiered earning structure, structured as follows:
  • Earn 5% cash back on groceries, up to $500 spent per month*
  • Earn 4% cash back on transit, up to $300 spent per month*
  • Earn 3% cash back on gas and electric vehicle charging, up to $300 spent per month*
  • Earn 2% cash back on recurring payments, up to $500 spent per month*
  • Earn 1% cash back on everything else*
These accelerated rates are among the best in the market for their respective categories, so on the surface, this looks like a great cash back card. Unfortunately, the category bonuses are capped at a low level of spending per month ($300–500), so the higher earning rates are only beneficial if you don't spend a lot in each category every month. Any purchases above the monthly limits will only earn 1% cash back.

There are three ways to redeem your cash back on the BMO CashBack® World Elite®* Mastercard®*:
  • Deposit into a BMO chequing or savings account*
  • Deposit into a BMO InvestorLine account*
  • Redeem as a credit against your credit card's statement*
Any of these three options can be requested at any time when you've earned at least $1 of cash back. You can also set up automatic payments, which will automatically trigger one of the above three redemption options every time you reach $25 of cash back. BMO's cash back redemption options are relatively flexible compared to its peers, many of which only allow you to claim your cash back earnings once a year.

Among the perks specific to BMO credit cards, you'll enjoy discounts at select shows: 15% off Cirque du Soleil's Canadian tours and 20% off resident shows in Las Vegas.* You'll also be enrolled in a free membership in the Dominion Automobile Association, with the benefits of the basic tier of the BMO Roadside Assistance Program.* Furthermore, as a standard benefit on World Elite Mastercards, you'll get a complimentary DragonPass membership (although you do need to pay for each visit to an airport lounge).*

You’ll also receive Instacart benefits, including:

  • Six months of Instacart+ membership, and
  • A $10 Instacart statement credit each month, on orders of $50 or more
  • Credits apply to non-alcohol purchases only and do not roll over month to month

The insurance package on the BMO CashBack® World Elite®* Mastercard®* is quite strong overall, including common carrier accident insurance of $500,000, flight delay insurance of $500 on delays of over four hours, and $500 of baggage delay insurance on delays of over six hours.* The card's lost and stolen baggage insurance covers $500 per person and up to $1,000 per trip, and the rental car collision insurance covers vehicles up to $65,000 (MSRP).* Extended warranty is also included on this card, doubling the manufacturer's standard warranty by up to an additional one year.*

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite product, the BMO CashBack® World Elite®* Mastercard®* has a minimum annual income requirement of $80,000 (individual) or $150,000 (household).

If you're interested in this card, consider supporting Prince of Travel by applying for the card via our affiliate link below.

historical_offers.notes: Waived first year. $40 cash back per month for spending $2k a month

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. BMO is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the BMO eclipse Visa Infinite* Card, the flagship lifestyle credit card issued by BMO.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 70,000 BMO Rewards points

$549 first-year value

features.feature: 5x points on dining, groceries, gas, and transit†

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 30,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending $4,000 in the first 110 days
  • Earn 20,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending a total of $7,000 in the first 180 days
  • Plus, earn 20,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending a total of $12,000 in the first 12 months
  • Total of up to 70,000 BMO Rewards points
  • Earn 5x BMO Rewards points on groceries, dining, gas, and transit
  • Receive an annual $50 lifestyle credit 
  • Receive $20 monthly streaming subscription credit
  • Annual fee: $0 for the first year, then $120

card_benefits.b_content:
30,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending $4,000 in the first 110 days
20,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending a total of $7,000 in the first 180 days
20,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending a total of $12,000 in the first 12 months
Total of 70,000 BMO Rewards points
$120, waived for the first year

21.99% purchases

23.99% balance transfers (21.99% for Quebec residents)

5 BMO Rewards points per dollar spent on dining, groceries, gas, and transit†
1 BMO Rewards point per dollar spent on all other purchases
10% more points when you add a supplementary card ($50 per year)

$50 annual lifestyle credit
$20 monthly streaming subscription credit
Six months of complimentary Instacart+ membership
$10 Instacart credit each month, valid on orders of $50 or more
Comprehensive

card_content.c_content:
The BMO eclipse Visa Infinite* Card offers a high welcome bonus, along with an extremely competitive earning structure of 5 points per dollar spent on dining, gas, groceries, and drugstores. The $50 annual credit also goes a long way towards offsetting the annual fee.
[read-more ids="30114"]

The BMO eclipse Visa Infinite* Card offers a welcome bonus of up to 70,000 BMO Rewards points, distributed as follows:
  • 30,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending $4,000 in the first 110 days
  • 20,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending a total of $7,000 in the first 180 days
  • 20,000 BMO Rewards points upon spending a total of $12,000 in the first 12 months

All bonus points are awarded up to eight weeks after each spending milestone is met, and your account must remain open and in good standing to receive them.

The card comes with an annual fee of $120, which is waived for the first anniversary. Supplementary cards can be added for an annual fee of $50 apiece.

The BMO eclipse Visa Infinite* Card launches with the following earning rates:

  • 5 BMO Rewards points per dollar spent on dining, groceries, gas, and transit (up to specific annual purchase limits per category)
  • 1 BMO Rewards point per dollar spent on all other purchases

The accelerated earn rate applies to the following annual purchase limits, calculated from January 1st to December 31st each year:

  • Dining: First $6,000 in annual purchases
  • Grocery: First $6,000 in annual purchases
  • Gas: First $20,000 in annual purchases
  • Transit: First $20,000 in annual purchases

For greater clarity, the bonus categories are defined as follows, and are limited to purchases made in Canada:

  • "Dining" includes restaurants, bars, and cafes, as well as food delivery services like Uber Eats and meal-kit companies like Hello Fresh and GoodFood.
  • "Groceries" includes any grocery store or supermarkets, but excludes wholesale retailers like Costco or general-goods retailers like Walmart.
  • "Gas" includes all gas stations.
  • "Transit" includes subways, metros, buses, commuter rail, ferries, taxis, and rideshare services.

The 5x return on these five categories is among the strongest in Canada, putting the BMO eclipse Visa Infinite* Card (as well as its Visa Infinite Privilege counterpart) right up there alongside the Cobalt Card and the Scotiabank Gold American Express® Card as the only 5x-earning Canadian credit cards.

Since BMO Rewards points are valued at 0.67cpp as outlined above, the return of 5x BMO Rewards points can also be thought of as a very competitive 3.35% return on these categories. Moreover, as an introductory benefit, if you add a supplementary card to your BMO eclipse Visa Infinite* account, you'll receive a 10% boost to the earning rates, which is the first credit card feature of its kind in Canada. As a result, you'll enjoy the following elevated earning rates.
  • 5.5 BMO Rewards points per dollar spent on dining, groceries, gas, transit,
  • 1.1 BMO Rewards point per dollar spent on all other purchases
In this case, you're getting an effective 3.69% return on spending in bonus categories! However, on purchases outside of these five categories, the BMO eclipse Visa Infinite* only offers 1 point per dollar spent. This is therefore equivalent to a 0.67% return on spending, so this wouldn't really be the strongest card for day-to-day spending outside of the bonus categories.

BMO Rewards points are worth 0.67 cents per point (cpp) when redeemed against the cost of travel booked via the BMO Rewards online portal (at a fixed rate of 150 BMO Rewards points = $1).

As such, the full welcome bonus of 70,000 points can be redeemed for up to $467 in travel, assuming you redeem at the travel rate.

BMO Rewards points can also be redeemed at 0.5cpp against any statement purchase (at the rate of 200 BMO Rewards points = $1), but the value in doing so is clearly not as strong, so you should always aim to use your BMO Rewards points to book travel, if possible.

The card’s standout benefit is the $50 annual lifestyle credit, which is triggered by a single purchase of $50 or more once per annual period. The credit posts after the account anniversary date and helps offset the card’s annual fee.

In addition, the card may offer monthly streaming subscription credits of up to $20 per month (to a maximum of $240 in the first year), subject to minimum monthly spending requirements and eligible merchant categories.

The BMO eclipse Visa Infinite Card* also includes Instacart-related benefits, which add real, everyday value if you already use grocery delivery.

Primary cardholders are eligible for:

  • Six months of complimentary Instacart+ membership
  • $10 Instacart credit each month, valid on orders of $50 or more
  • Credits apply to non-alcohol purchases only and do not roll over month to month

Like BMO's other premium travel credit cards, the BMO eclipse Visa Infinite* boasts an outstanding insurance package, including:

  • Emergency medical insurance

  • Common carrier accident insurance of up to $500,000

  • Car rental insurance and Collision Damage Waiver benefits, which allow you to save on the insurance fees that car rental companies usually charge

The most powerful part of the travel insurance benefits is that all of these benefits are valid as long as you charge the full or partial cost of the trip to your BMO eclipse Visa Infinite* Card.

This is a benefit which is unique to BMO's suite of travel credit cards, making it an ideal card for whenever you’re travelling on points or award tickets. As long as you charge the taxes and fees of your trip to the card, you’re fulfilling the “partial cost” criterion and therefore eligible to receive insurance coverage on your trip.

[read-more ids="456"] Furthermore, as a card geared towards the modern frequent traveller, the BMO eclipse Visa Infinite* Card also comes with the following additional perks:
  • Mobile device insurance that covers you in the case of loss, damage, or theft of your mobile device for up to $1,000
  • Extended warranty benefits that automatically doubles the manufacturer's warranty up to a maximum of one years
  • Purchase security insurance that insures your items against theft or damage within the first 90 days after purchase.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a Visa Infinite product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $60,000 (individual) or $100,000 (household).

Consider supporting Prince of Travel by applying for the card via our affiliate link below.

historical_offers.notes: +$50 lifestyle credit, can also spend $2k per month in months 4-15 to earn 2500 per month

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the RBC® Cash Back Mastercard, an entry-level cash back credit card option issued by RBC®.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 7% cash back†

$70 first-year value†
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 2% cash back on groceries up to $6,000 per year, then 1% cash back with no limit thereafter.
  • Earn 0.5% cash back on all other purchases, including pre-authorized bill payments, up to $6,000 per year, then 1% cash back with no limit thereafter.

card_benefits.b_content: Earn up to 7% cash back<sup>†</sup> in the first three months, on up to $1,000 spent on eligible net purchases<sup>†</sup>

20.99% purchases 
22.99% cash advances (21.99% QC)
22.99% balance transfers (21.99% QC)

2% cash back<sup>†</sup> on grocery purchases up to $6,000, then 1% cash back<sup>†</sup> thereafter.
0.5% cash back<sup>†</sup> on all other purchases up to $6,000, then 1% cash back<sup>†</sup> thereafter.
Basic

card_content.c_content: The RBC® Cash Back Mastercard is a card geared towards students, newcomers to Canada, or individuals with new or recovering credit files who want to build a strong relationship with RBC® while earning some cash back. [read-more ids="30109"]

The RBC® Cash Back Mastercard† is currently offering up to 7% cash back^ in the first three months, on up to $1,000 in total purchases:

  • 5% bonus cash back + 2% standard grocery earn rate

  • 5% bonus cash back + 0.5% standard earn rate on all other purchases

This is a no-fee card, and supplementary cards are also free.

The RBC® Cash Back Mastercard earns 2% cash back† on groceries up to $6,000 spent per year (maximum $120), and unlimited 1% cash back on grocery purchases beyond that amount.† For all other purchases, including pre-authorized bill payments, you’ll earn 0.5% cash back† on the first $6,000 spent annually (maximum $30), and unlimited 1% cash back thereafter.†

There are two ways to redeem RBC® cash back:
  • Upon reaching a cash back balance of at least $25, call RBC® (1-800-769-2512) to credit the entire amount to your account
  • Automatically credited every January and appears on February statement

Despite being a no-fee card, the RBC® Cash Back Mastercard includes several useful partner benefits. Petro-Canada Savings† Link your RBC card to Petro-Points and automatically:
  • Save 3¢/L on fuel
  • Earn 20% more Petro-Points
Your linked card doubles as your Petro-Points card. Rexall: Earn More With Be Well™† When you scan your Be Well card and pay with your linked RBC card, you earn:
  • 50 Be Well points per $1 spent on eligible purchases
A simple way to accelerate pharmacy rewards. DoorDash: Six Month Complimentary DashPass† Add your RBC card to DoorDash and activate your benefit to enjoy:
  • Free DashPass for six months
  • $0 delivery fees on eligible orders of $15+
Metro, Food Basics & More: Extra Moi Rewards† Earn 1 extra Moi point per $2 spent at Metro, Food Basics, Super C, Jean Coutu, Brunet, and Première Moisson (minimum purchase applies)
The RBC<sup>®</sup> Cash Back Mastercard offers a doubling of the manufacturer’s warranty up to one additional year<sup>†</sup> as well as provides 90 days of purchase protection<sup>†</sup>.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a basic no-fee product, the RBC® Cash Back Mastercard has no minimum income requirement.

Refer to RBC® page for up to date offer terms and conditions.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. RBC is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information. ^ This offer only applies to new applications for an RBC Cash Back Mastercard credit card received between December 10, 2025 and March 25, 2026 (“Offer Period”) and approved by Royal Bank of Canada (“Royal Bank”), subject to the conditions below. Additional cardholders(s) (co-applicant and authorized user(s)) on the RBC Cash Back Mastercard credit card account, as well as existing cardholders on any RBC Royal Bank personal credit card transferring to an RBC Cash Back Mastercard credit card during the Offer Period, are not eligible for this offer. You will earn an additional $5.00 back for every $100.00 (5% Cash Back Credit) in Net Purchases (including pre-authorized bill payments) charged on your RBC Cash Back Mastercard account within the first 3 months of account opening, up to a maximum total combined spend of $1,000 (“Welcome Bonus”). Thereafter, the standard Cash Back Credit earn rates of up to 2% on Grocery Store Purchases and up to 1% on other Net Purchases (that are not Grocery Store Purchases) apply. The Welcome Bonus is in addition to the standard Cash Back Credit earn rates. Cash Back Credits are not earned on cash advances (including balance transfers, cash-like transactions and bill payments that are not pre-authorized charges that you set up with a merchant), interest charges or fees, and credits for returns and adjustments will reduce or cancel the cash back credits earned by the amounts originally charged. If you close or change your RBC Cash Back Mastercard credit card for another type of RBC Royal Bank credit card before the Welcome Bonus has been credited to your account, you may lose the benefit of this offer. This offer may not be combined or used in conjunction with any other offer. Royal Bank reserves the right to cancel, modify or withdraw this offer at any time without notice, even after your application has been approved, including if we suspect that you may be manipulating or abusing it, or engaging in any suspicious or fraudulent activity, as determined by Royal Bank in its sole discretion. For complete details on the RBC Cash Back Program including standard earn rates and crediting of Cash Back Rewards, please refer to the RBC Cash Back Program Terms and Conditions at www.rbc.com/cashbackterms. Offer applies to new RBC Cash Back Mastercard accounts only. Unlimited means there is no maximum Cash Back Credits you can earn on Grocery Store Purchases and other purchases (up to your available credit limit) per Annual Period. For complete details, including the meaning of capitalized terms, please refer to the RBC Cash Back Program Terms and Conditions available at: www.rbc.com/cashbackterms

title.rendered: RBC® Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the RBC® Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard, a premium cash back credit card option issued by RBC®.

acf.subtitle:
12% cash back†

$141 first-year value†

features.feature: 1.5% cash back on all eligible purchases^

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 12% cash back† in the first three months, up to $2,000 spent on eligible net purchases†
  • Earn 1.5% cash back† on all eligible purchases, up to $25,000 spent†
  • Earn 1% cash back on all eligible purchases over $25,000†
  • Annual fee: $99

card_benefits.b_content: Earn 12% cash back<sup>†</sup> in the first three months, up to $2,000 spent on eligible net purchases<sup>†</sup>
$99<sup>†</sup>

20.99% purchases 
22.99% cash advances (21.99% QC)
22.99% balance transfers (21.99% QC)

1.5% cash back on all qualifying purchases up to $25,000 spent<sup>†</sup>
1% cash back on all qualifying purchases over $25,000<sup>†</sup>
Basic

card_content.c_content: The RBC<sup>®</sup> Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard is marketed as as premium cash back card, with a simple earning structure for those who want a decent return on their spending without having to concern themselves with bonus categories.

The RBC<sup>®</sup> Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard is currently offering 12% cash back<sup>†</sup> in the first three months, up to $2,000 spent on all eligible net purchases<sup>†</sup>.

The annual fee is $99<sup>†</sup>, with free supplementary cards<sup>†</sup>.

Outside the welcome bonus period, the card earns:
  • 1.5% cash back† on all qualifying purchases up to $25,000 spent per Annual Period†
  • 1% cash back† on all qualifying purchases over $25,000, with no maximum†

There are no bonus categories to track, and cash back is earned on net purchases only (not on cash advances, fees, or interest).

There are two ways to redeem RBC® cash back:
  • Upon reaching a cash back balance of at least $25, call RBC® (1-800-769-2512) to credit the entire amount to your account
  • Automatically credited every January and appears on February statement

The RBC® Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard includes a handful of benefits†. DashPass from DoorDash† Get a complimentary 12-month DashPass subscription when you add your eligible RBC credit card and activate the offer. This gives you:
  • $0 delivery fees on orders of $15+
  • Reduced service fees at eligible restaurants
Petro-Canada: Fuel Savings + Points Boost† Link your RBC card and automatically enjoy:
  • 3¢/L off every fill-up
  • 20% more Petro-Points on qualifying purchases
No need to swipe your Petro-Points card—your linked RBC card does the work. Rexall: Earn More With Be Well™† Scan your Be Well card and pay with your linked RBC card to earn:
  • 50 Be Well points per $1 spent on eligible purchases
This can be a powerful stacking opportunity when combined with Be Well promotions. Moi Rewards at Metro, Food Basics & More† With your Moi Rewards card and linked RBC card, earn:
  • 1 extra Moi point for every $2 spent at Metro, Food Basics, Super C, Jean Coutu, Brunet, or Première Moisson (minimum purchase thresholds apply)
Mastercard Travel Pass (DragonPass)† As a World Elite Mastercard cardholder, you receive:
  • Complimentary DragonPass membership, with access to 1,300+ lounges
  • Pay-per-visit pricing (currently US$32 per person)

The RBC® Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard card offers basic insurance coverage, which is below par for a World Elite card.

Auto Rental Collision/Loss Damage Waiver Insurance†

Covers eligible rental cars with an MSRP up to $65,000†.

Purchase Security†

Protects eligible purchases against loss, theft, or damage for 120 days†.

Extended Warranty†

Triples the manufacturer’s original Canadian warranty, up to two additional years†.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite product, this credit card has a minimum personal annual income requirement of $80,000 or a minimum household income of $150,000 to be eligible.

Refer to RBC® page for up to date offer terms and conditions.

historical_offers.notes: 12% cash back up to $2,000 spend in the first three months

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. RBC is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information. ^ This offer only applies to new applications for an RBC Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard credit card received between December 10, 2025 and March 25, 2026 (“Offer Period”) and approved by Royal Bank of Canada (“Royal Bank”), subject to the conditions below. Additional cardholders(s) (co-applicant and authorized user(s)) on the RBC Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard credit card account, as well as existing cardholders on any RBC Royal Bank personal credit card transferring to an RBC Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard credit card during the Offer Period, are not eligible for this offer. You will earn a total of up to 12% cash back credit on Net Purchases (“Welcome Bonus”) made with your RBC Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard within the first 3 months from your account open date, up to a maximum of $2,000 in Net Purchases per credit card account (“Offer”) provided that your RBC Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard credit card account (“Credit Card Account”) remains open and in good standing at the time the Welcome points are credited to your account. “Good standing” meaning that your Credit Card Account must not be past due for more than two (2) consecutive credit card account statement periods, closed, charged off or in credit revoked status. Once you have reached the $2,000 or 3 months from your account open date, you will continue to earn at the standard rate of 1.5% cash back up to a maximum of $25,000 in Net Purchases per Annual Period, and 1% cash back thereafter, unlimited when you pay with your RBC Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard. Cash back credits are earned on net purchases only; they are not earned on cash advances (including balance transfers, cash-like transactions and bill payments that are not pre-authorized charges that you set up with a merchant), interest charges or fees, and credits for returns and adjustments will reduce or cancel the cash back credits earned by the amounts originally charged. Additional cardholders(s) (co-applicant and authorized user(s)) on the RBC Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard credit card account, as well as existing cardholders on any RBC Royal Bank personal credit card transferring to an RBC Cash Back Mastercard credit card during the Offer Period, are not eligible for this offer. If you close or change your RBC Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard credit card for another type of RBC Royal Bank credit card before the Welcome Bonus have been credited to your account, you may lose the benefit of this offer. This offer may not be combined or used in conjunction with any other offer. Royal Bank reserves the right to cancel, modify or withdraw this offer at any time without notice, even after your application has been approved, including if we suspect that you may be manipulating or abusing it, or engaging in any suspicious or fraudulent activity, as determined by Royal Bank in its sole discretion. For complete details on the RBC Cash Back Program including standard earn rates and crediting of Cash Back Rewards, please refer to the RBC Cash Back Program Terms and Conditions at www.rbc.com/cashbackterms. Offer applies to new RBC Cash Back Mastercard accounts only.

itemListElement.name: RBC® Cash Back Preferred World Elite Mastercard

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the National Bank® World Elite® Mastercard®, the flagship travel rewards credit card issued by National Bank.

acf.subtitle:
Up to $150 refund on annual travel expenses

$150 annual fee
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 5x À la carte Rewards points on grocery and restaurant spend†
  • Get travel insurance on award travel, as well as medical coverage on longer trips for ages up to 75†
  • Receive $150 in annual credits for airport parking, baggage fees, seat selection fees, lounge access, and airline ticket upgrades†
  • Minimum income: $80,000 personal or $150,000 household
  • Annual fee: $150

card_benefits.b_content: There is no welcome bonus with the current offer

20.99% purchase
22.49% balance transfer
22.49% cash advance

5 À la carte Rewards points per dollar spent on groceries and restaurants†
2 À la carte Rewards points per dollar spent on gas, EV charging, recurring bill payments, and with À la carte Travel†
1 À la carte Rewards point per dollar spent everywhere else†

$150 annual travel credit
Complimentary access to the National Bank Lounge at YUL
Comprehensive

card_content.c_content: The National Bank® World Elite® Mastercard® is an excellent card for travel insurance, where it's in a class of its own. With annual credits to offset the annual fee, it's a great premium keeper card for paying the taxes and fees on award ticket bookings. This card will especially be of interest to those that are older or anyone who enjoys longer trips, due to its generous emergency medical coverage.
The National Bank® World Elite® Mastercard® is currently not offering a welcome bonus. The card has an annual fee of $150, and there's a $50 fee for each authorized user added to your account.

The National Bank® World Elite® Mastercard® has a rewards structure that varies based on the amount of money you've spent on the card:
  • Earn 5 À la carte Rewards points per dollar spent on grocery and restaurant purchases†
  • Earn 2 À la carte Rewards points per dollar spent on gas, electric vehicle charging, recurring bills, and travel booked through À la carte Travel†
  • Earn 1 À la carte Rewards point per dollar spent on all other purchases†
If your total gross monthly amount of purchases (regardless of category) is under $2,500, the 5x multiplier on groceries and restaurant purchases will apply. However, if your purchases in one month exceed $2,500, the earning rate on those food purchases will fall to 2 À la carte Rewards points per dollar spent.†

National Bank's À la carte Rewards points can be redeemed through their online travel booking portal. Through this method, points have a fixed value of 1 cent per point. In the portal, you can book travel, or you can redeem points for a statement credit against travel expenses booked directly with vendors. Note that offsetting the cost of travel not booked through rewards points reduces the value of the points. If you redeem fewer than 55,000 points, you'll get 0.83 cents per point, and if you redeem more than 55,000 points, you'll get 0.91 cents per point. While having to book through a portal isn’t the most flexible redemption option for maximizing value, it is a far better value than the 0.83cpp that National Bank provides on using rewards points for straight cash back. Your National Bank rewards points never expire as long as you remain a cardholder.

The card provides a $150 annual travel credit, which can be applied to a range of travel expenses:
  • Airport parking
  • Baggage fees
  • Seat selection
  • Airport lounge access
  • Airline ticket upgrades
These credits renew every year on January 1, but unused credits do not carry over. Cardholders also have unlimited access to the National Bank Lounge at Montréal Pierre Elliott Trudeau International Airport for themselves plus one additional guest per visit. The lounge is available for passengers departing from the international terminal.

The National Bank® World Elite® Mastercard® provides outstanding insurance coverage. In particular, the card can be used for insurance on award tickets. Coverage is valid for trip cancellation, trip interruption, or flight delays, as long as the ticket was paid at least in part on the card. The card also offers fantastic emergency medical insurance when travelling. Cardholders are covered for up to $5,000,000 in damages, for trips up to 60 days, up to age 75.
  • Age 54 and under: trips up to 60 days
  • Age 55 to 64: trips up to 31 days
  • Age 65 to 75: trips up to 15 days
Likewise, the card's car rental insurance grants coverage for longer rentals of up to 48 days, which is longer than the industry standard. Additionally, the card has industry-leading purchase protection. The extended warranty and purchase protection periods are double what many competitors offer, with up to two additional years for extended warranty and up to 180 days of protection against stolen or damaged merchandise. Mobile device insurance is also a benefit of the card, as long the purchase and your monthly payments are charged to the card. You'll get coverage up to $1,000 in the event of breakage, loss, or theft for up to two years after the purchase date.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $80,000 (individual) or $150,000 (household).

Follow the link below to the National Bank website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. National Bank is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.
