title.rendered: CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Business

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the CIBC Aventura Visa Card for Business, the small business travel rewards card by CIBC that earns CIBC Aventura points.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 70,000 CIBC Aventura Points

$1,050 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 30,000 CIBC Aventura Points upon making your first purchase†
  • Plus, earn 40,000 CIBC Aventura Points upon spending $35,000 in the first 12 months†
  • Plus, earn 2x CIBC Aventura Points on all purchases through the CIBC Rewards Travel Centre†
  • Plus, earn 1.5x CIBC Aventura Points on gas, EV charging, and other travel purchases
  • Qualify for the card with a registered business
  • Annual fee: $120 (rebated for the first year)†

card_benefits.b_content: †Terms and Conditions apply. The information for the CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Business has been collected independently  by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

30,000 CIBC Aventura Points upon making your first purchase†
40,000 CIBC Aventura Points upon spending $35,000 in the first 12 months†
Total of up to 70,000 Aventura Points†
$120 (rebated for the first year)

19.99% purchase
21.99% cash advance
21.99% balance transfer

2 Aventura Points per dollar spent on travel purchases via the CIBC Travel Rewards Centre
1.5 Aventura Points per dollar spent on gas, EV charging, and other travel purchases
1 Aventura Point per dollar spent on all other purchases
Average

card_content.c_content:
The CIBC Aventura Visa Card for Business is a mid-tier card targeted at business owners that want to earn CIBC Aventura Points from their day to day spending.

The information for the CIBC Aventura Visa Card for Business has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

When you apply for the CIBC Aventura Visa Card for Business, there is a sign up bonus of up to 70,000 CIBC Aventura Points structured as follows:
  • Earn 30,000 CIBC Aventura Points upon making your first purchase†
  • Plus, earn 40,000 CIBC Aventura Points upon spending $35,000 in the first eight months†
The $120 annual fee is currently waived for the first year. You can add $60 to the annual fee if you need low-interest financing for your business cashflow.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

We value CIBC Aventura Points at 1 cent per point, which makes the CIBC Aventura® Visa Card for Business* a strong contender for small business owners looking to earn flexible travel rewards without a hefty upfront cost. Here’s how the welcome bonus is structured:
  • 30,000 Aventura Points upon first purchase = $300
  • 40,000 Aventura Points after spending $35,000 in the first 12 statement periods = $400
That’s a total of 70,000 Aventura Points, worth $700. Spending $35,000 at the base earn rate of 1 point per dollar earns you an additional 35,000 points, worth $350. That brings your total first-year value to $1,050. The $120 annual fee is rebated in the first year, so there’s no upfront cost to unlock the full bonus. This gives you a net first-year value of $1,050, assuming you meet the spending requirements. This is a solid return for a business card with a modest spend threshold — and with flexible redemption options for flights, hotels, and other travel expenses, it’s a practical choice for business owners who want to earn rewards without overcomplicating their spending strategy.

This credit card earns CIBC Aventura Points on daily spending at the following rates:

  • Earn 2 Aventura Points per dollar spent on travel booked through the CIBC Travel Rewards Centre
  • Earn 1.5 Aventura Points per dollar spent on gas, EV charging, and other travel purchases
  • Earn 1 Aventura Point per dollar spent on everything else

CIBC Aventura Points can be redeemed in a variety of ways. However, the most valuable way to redeem Aventura points is on travel purchases. For example, you can redeem Aventura Points for flights booked through the CIBC Travel Rewards Centre at a rate of 1 cent per point.

You can also use CIBC Aventura Points towards a $1,000 (CAD) airline ticket in the “Canada and U.S. (long-haul)” category on the CIBC Airline Rewards Chart, increasing the value of your points from 1 cent per point (cpp) to up to 2.29cpp.

Generally speaking, it's recommended to redeem Aventura Points via the above chart whenever possible, since it offers the maximum value for your points. It's worth noting that this must be done through CIBC's in-house travel portal. However, Aventura Points can also be useful for offsetting the cost of travel that's otherwise difficult to book with points. For example, Aventura Points can be used to cover the cost of independent hotels, short-term rentals, car rentals, trains, or flights that can't be booked with other programs, such as Aeroplan or Marriott Bonvoy. [read-more title="Read More" ids="81635" ]

The CIBC Aventura Visa Card for Business doesn't feature any traditional perks and benefits; however, it's worthwhile to note that when you apply for the card, you're able to choose which annual fee and accompanying interest rates work best for your business.

The insurance on the CIBC Aventura Visa Card for Business is middle of the road, as it doesn't come with emergency out-of-province medical insurance, which is a pillar of many other cards.

With the CIBC Aventura Visa Card for Business, you'll get flight and baggage delay insurance, car rental loss and damage insurance (which can help you save on the daily insurance charged by car rental companies), $500,000 common carrier accident insurance, and protection against trip cancellation or interruption.

colum_block.col1_text:
This card is available for any business, whether you run a sole proprietorship or a corporation. You will need to show various types of business registration documents depending on the structure of your enterprise.

You can qualify with a minimum personal annual income of $35,000. Otherwise, you can be approved on the basis of your business financials if you apply for the CIBC Aventura Visa Card for Business Plus.

You can follow the below link to the CIBC website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. The information for the CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Business has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the MBNA True Line® Mastercard, one of the best credit cards for balance transfers in Canada.

acf.subtitle: 0% interest rate for balance transfers†
acf.featured_info:
• 0% annual interest rate (AIR) for 12 months on balance transfers made within three months of account opening†
  • 3% balance transfer fee†
  • Annual fee: $0
acf.membership_reward: 0% annual interest rate on balance transfers†

card_benefits.b_content: This card does not come with a signup bonus.

12.99% purchases
17.99% balance transfers
24.99% cash advances
This card does not earn rewards

card_content.c_content: The MBNA True Line<sup>®</sup> Mastercard is one of Canada's best options for balance transfers.

If you're approved for the MBNA True Line® Mastercard, you can enjoy a promotional 0% annual interest rate (AIR)† on balance transfers made within 90 days of account opening.† The promotional 0% interest rate is valid for 12 months†, and there's a 3% balance transfer fee.† Important Notice: Effective September 25, 2025
  • If the Balance Transfer fee is less than $3.50, MBNA will charge a minimum Balance Transfer fee of $3.50.

The primary benefit of this card is its promotional balance transfer rate.

You can save on Avis and Budget car rentals, with a minimum 10% off base rates in Canada and the US,† and a minimum of 5% off base rates internationally.†

For Avis, use Avis Worldwide Discount (AWD) #C078405 at the time of reservation and charge the full rental to your MBNA credit card.

For budget, use Budget Customer Discount (BCD) #A331704 at the time of reservation and charge the full rental to your MBNA credit card.

As a Mastercard cardholder, you won't be responsible for unauthorized purchases made with your credit card in store, over the telephone, online, mobile device or ATM transactions.†

colum_block.col1_text:
You must be a Canadian resident and of the age of majority in your province/territory of residence to apply.

Follow the link below to the MBNA® website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †, ✪, Terms and Conditions apply. This offer is not available for residents of Quebec. For residents of Quebec, please click here. Sponsored advertising. MBNA is a division of The Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD) and TD is not responsible for the contents of this site including any editorials or reviews that may appear on this site. For complete information on this MBNA credit card, please click on the “Apply Now” button. The Toronto-Dominion Bank is the issuer of this credit card. MBNA is a division of The Toronto-Dominion Bank. ®MBNA and other-trademarks are the property of The Toronto-Dominion Bank. excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Rogers Mastercard®, a no-fee cash back card issued by Rogers Bank.

acf.subtitle: Earn unlimited cash back
acf.reward_points: 2% cash back on all eligible non-U.S. dollar purchases†

features.feature: Earn unlimited 2% cash back on all eligible non-U.S. dollar purchases as an eligible Rogers, Fido, or Shaw customer
1.5x cash back for redeeming towards Rogers, Fido, Comwave and Shaw purchases

acf.featured_info:
• Earn unlimited 2% cash back on all eligible U.S. dollar purchases†
  • Plus, earn unlimited 2% cash back on all non-U.S. dollar purchases as an eligible Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw customer†
  • Otherwise, earn unlimited 1% cash back on eligible non-U.S. dollar purchases†
  • Plus, get 1.5x cash back when redeemed for Rogers, Fido, Comwave and Shaw purchases†
  • Get five free Roam Like Home days per year†
  • No minimum income requirement†
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content:
20.99–25.99% purchases*
22.99–27.99% cash advances*

2% cash back on all eligible purchases in U.S. dollars†
2% cash back on all eligible non-U.S. dollar purchases as an eligible Rogers, Fido or Shaw customer†
Otherwise, 1% cash back on all eligible non-U.S. dollar purchases as a non-customer†
1.5x more cash back every time you redeem for Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw purchases†
Basic
No income required

card_content.c_content: The Rogers Red Mastercard® is a good choice for anyone who's entrenched in the Rogers ecosystem and who wants to earn cash back on everyday purchases.

The card doesn't have an annual fee, and you can add up to nine supplementary cardholders at no cost.<sup>†</sup>

Currently, this card does not offer a welcome bonus.

The Rogers Red Mastercard® has a rewards structure that depends on your relationship with Rogers. If you're an eligible Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw customer, you'll earn 2% cash back on all eligible non-U.S. dollar purchases.† Qualifying services include postpaid Rogers or Fido consumer mobile plans (including wireless home phone and tablet data plans) or any Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw-branded internet, TV, home phone, home monitoring, or satellite plan. If you're not a Rogers, Fido, or Shaw customer, you'll earn 2% cash back on all eligible purchases in U.S. dollars†, and 1% cash back on all eligible non-U.S. dollar purchases.† It's important to note that the card has a 2.5% foreign transaction fee, so your net cash back may actually dip into the negative for foreign transactions. It's recommended to use a true no foreign transaction fee card for purchases outside of Canada instead.

Cash back can be redeemed a minimum of $10 at a time on any purchase made on the card in the last 90 days.

If you redeem your cash back for an eligible Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw purchase, you'll get 1.5x more cash back.

For example, you can redeem $100 in cash back toward an eligible $150 purchase with Rogers, Fido, Shaw, or Comwave.

This redemption option offers the best value for the cash back you’ve earned on the Rogers Red Mastercard<sup>®</sup>.

Aside from cash back, the Rogers Red Mastercard® provides cardholders with complimentary five free Roam Like Home days each year.† This feature lets Rogers subscribers use their mobile plan's call, text, and data allocations in 185 destinations abroad and be charged only a flat daily fee. As a cardholder, you can also purchase a new phone with a 0% interest rate through the Equal Payment Plan.† To take advantage of this perk, you’ll need to pay for a new device costing at least $250 with your Rogers Red Mastercard® at a Rogers or Fido store. Afterwards, you’ll be able to convert the purchase into a 36-month or 48-month Equal Payment Plan.† If you aren't able to pay for a phone outright, then this perk can be particularly useful, since you won't incur any interest or additional fees, as long as you make your monthly payments.†

The Rogers Red Mastercard® doesn't have a strong suite of insurance. Cardholders enjoy transaction monitoring and alerts, optional balance protection, as well as zero liability for unauthorized use. Please refer to the certificate insurance for full coverage limitations and exclusions.

colum_block.col1_text:
There is no minimum income requirement for this card.

Consider supporting Prince of Travel by applying for the card via our affiliate link below.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Rogers Red World Elite® Mastercard®, a no-fee cash back Mastercard® issued by Rogers Bank.

acf.subtitle: Earn unlimited cash back
acf.reward_points: 2% cash back on all eligible non-US dollar purchases†

features.feature: Earn unlimited 2% cash back on all eligible purchases as an eligible Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw customer

acf.featured_info:
• Earn unlimited 3% cash back on U.S. dollar purchases.†
  • Earn unlimited 2% cash back on eligible non U.S. dollar purchases–exclusively for eligible Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw customers.†
  • Non-customers earn unlimited 1.5% cash back on eligible non U.S. dollar purchases.†
  • Plus, get 1.5x cash back every time you redeem for Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw purchases.†
  • Minimum income: $80,000 personal or $150,000 household
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content:
20.99–25.99% purchases*
22.99–27.99% cash advances*

3% cash back on all eligible U.S. dollar purchases†
2% cash back on all eligible non U.S. dollar purchases as an eligible Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw customer†
Otherwise, 1.5% cash back on eligible non-US dollar purchases as a non customer†
1.5x more cash back every time you redeem for Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw purchases†

DragonPass membership (32 USD per person per visit)†
Exclusive perks for Rogers customers†
Standard
$80,000 personal or $150,000 household

card_content.c_content: The Rogers Red World Elite® Mastercard® is a solid choice for anyone looking for simplicity: one card for all purchases with no annual fee and a high cash back rate. It's also a good card if you're a Rogers customer and spend in US dollars, and don't mind paying foreign transaction fees. [read-more ids="30103"]
The card has no annual fee, which is a rare feature for a World Elite® product. Currently, this card does not offer a welcome bonus.

The Rogers Red World Elite® Mastercard® has a rewards structure that depends on your relationship with Rogers. If you're an eligible Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw, you'll earn 2% cash back on all eligible non-U.S. dollar purchases.† Qualifying services include postpaid Rogers or Fido consumer mobile plans (including wireless home phone and tablet data plans) or any Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw-branded internet, TV, home phone, home monitoring, or satellite plan. If you're not a Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw customer, you'll earn 3% cash back on all eligible purchases in U.S. dollars†, and 1.5% cash back on all eligible non-U.S. dollar purchases.† There are no bonus categories with this card. Its strength lies in the consistently high base earning rate, which makes it a solid option for uncategorized spending—or even at retailers like Costco, where category bonuses usually don’t apply. It's important to note that the card carries a 2.5% foreign transaction fee, which can reduce the net value of your cash back on purchases made in foreign currencies other than U.S. dollars. It's recommended to use a true no foreign transaction fee card if you frequently make purchases in currencies other than Canadian or U.S. dollars, such as euros.

Cash back can be redeemed a minimum of $10 at a time on any purchase made on the card in the last 90 days.

If you redeem your cash back for an eligible Rogers, Fido, Comwave or Shaw purchase, you'll get 1.5x more cash back.

For example, you can redeem $100 in cash back toward an eligible $150 purchase with Rogers, Fido, Shaw, or Comwave.

This redemption option offers the best value for the cash back you’ve earned on the Rogers Red World Elite<sup>®</sup> Mastercard<sup>®</sup>.

The card comes with a few standard Mastercard World Elite® perks for travellers. Cardholders get a DragonPass membership for access to 1,300 airport lounges worldwide, which is priced at $32 (USD) per visit. As a cardholder, you can also purchase a new phone with a 0% interest rate through the Equal Payment Plan.† To take advantage of this perk, you’ll need to pay for a new device costing at least $250 with your Rogers credit card at a Rogers or Fido store. Afterwards, you’ll be able to convert the purchase into a 36-month or 48-month Equal Payment Plan.† If you aren't able to pay for a phone outright, then this perk can be particularly useful, since you won't incur any interest or additional fees, as long as you make your monthly payments.† Another perk for Rogers Red World Elite® Mastercard® cardholders, you get 5 Roam Like Home days every year at no cost with an eligible Rogers mobile plan.

The Rogers Red World Elite® Mastercard® provides generous insurance provisions for a card that doesn't have an annual fee. The emergency medical assistance only covers trips up to 10 days for most cardholders. However, it also provides coverage for trips up to 3 days for cardholders aged 66 to 75. This would be valuable for weekend getaways for seniors, who usually aren't covered at all by credit card insurance. Cardholders will also get both trip cancellation and trip interruption insurance of up to $1,000 per insured person. You'll also get auto rental collision/loss damage insurance. The comprehensive coverage is for up to 31 consecutive days for cars with an MSRP of up to $65,000, which can help you save on the insurance fees that the car rental company would otherwise charge.† Lastly, the card also offers purchase security and extended warranty protection, covering most eligible items you've purchased with your card should they be stolen or damaged within 90 days of purchase, as well as doubled the warranty period compared to the manufacturer's warranty. Some of the insurance benefits cover the primary cardholder, their spouse, and dependent children. Additional cardholders are also insured, although their spouse and children are not. Please refer to the certificate insurance for full coverage limitations and exclusions.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite® product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $80,000 (individual) or $150,000 (household).

Consider supporting Prince of Travel by applying for the card via our affiliate link below.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the SimplyCash Card from American Express, a no-fee cash back credit card issued by American Express.

acf.subtitle:
$100 cash back

$100 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 5% cash back on all purchases in the first three months (up to $2,000 in purchases)
  • Then, earn 2% cash back on gas and groceries
  • Then, earn 1.25% cash back on all other purchases
  • Enjoy the exclusive benefits of being an American Express cardholder
  • Referral bonuses when friends and family sign up for the card
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content:
Earn 5% cash back on all purchases in the first three months (up to $2,000 in purchases)
Total of up to $100 cash back

$0

Interest Rates:
20.99% purchases
21.99% cash advances
21.99% balance transfers

2% cash back on gas
2% cash back on groceries
1.25% cash back on all other purchases
Basic
Credit card

card_content.c_content: The SimplyCash Card from American Express is a great entry-level Amex card. For a card with no annual fee, it earns rewards at quite a high rate, and also offers many standard benefits of the Amex lifestyle such as Amex Offers.

As a welcome bonus, this card offers 5% cash back on all purchases in the first three months (up to $2,000 in purchases). If you were to max this out, you'd get $100 cash back. This card has no annual fee, along with no fee for adding additional users. It’s a good choice to keep around in the long run to support the longevity of your credit history and your relationship with American Express.

The American Express SimplyCash Card has a simple earning structure: you’ll earn 2% cash back on groceries and gas, and 1.25% cash back on all purchases. It's important to note that the 2% cash back categories are limited to a maximum of $15,000 spent per cardholder anniversary. While other cards may have higher earning rates in a few niche categories, this card offers a very strong rate of return on two popular categories, and a good return on uncategorized purchases. If you prefer a slim wallet, frequently shop at stores that take Amex, and want to minimize annual fees, the SimplyCash Card can be suitable as an addition to your wallet. The card also has a refer-a-friend program. You can earn $75 for every successful referral, up to $375 per calendar year.
Your cash back on the SimplyCash Card can be redeemed annually. It's always issued automatically on your September statement, as a credit to your account.

The SimplyCash Card by American Express comes with all of the standard perks of being an American Express cardholder, including Front of the Line event presale tickets, exclusive invites for American Express Experiences, and periodic Amex Offers for additional rebates and rewards on everyday spending.

The insurance benefits on the American Express SimplyCash Card are fairly limited. The card's sole type of travel insurance is Common Carrier Accident insurance, which reimburses you up to $100,000 in the case of an unforeseen accident onboard scheduled transportation that results in bodily harm. Additionally, the card also offers Purchase Security and Extended Warranty Protection, covering most eligible items you’ve purchased with your card should they be stolen or damaged within 90 days of purchase, as well as an extended one-year warranty period compared to the manufacturer’s warranty.

colum_block.col1_text:
There is no minimum income requirement for this card, meaning that anyone is eligible to apply.

Follow the below link to the Amex website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

title.rendered: WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard for Businessǂ

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard for Businessǂ, the flagship WestJet Rewards credit card for business owners issued by RBC®.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 35,000 WestJet points†

$175 first-year value

features.feature: Instant Silver status until December 31 of the following year†
Two free checked bag on WestJet flights†

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 35,000 WestJet points upon making your first purchase†
  • Complimentary WestJet Silver status in your first year and the following year†
  • Status Lift up to 50 times per year and earn status without flying†
  • Automatic WestJet Biz Program Tier 1 enrollment†
  • WestJet points never expire
  • Minimum income: $80,000 personal or $150,000 household or minimum annual business revenue of $250,000
  • Annual fee: $175

card_benefits.b_content: 35,000 WestJet points upon making your first purchase†

$175†
$75† for supplementary cardholders (up to 9)

19.99% purchases
22.99% cash advances (21.99% QC)
22.99% balance transfers (21.99% QC)

3 WestJet points per dollar spent on eligible WestJet flights and WestJet vacation packages†
3 WestJet points per dollar spent on telecommunications, shipping and electronic purchases†
1.5 WestJet points per dollar spent on all other purchases†

Complimentary WestJet Silver status in your first year and the following year†
Status Lift up to 50 times per year†
Automatic WestJet Biz Program Tier 1 enrollment†
Strong

card_content.c_content:
The WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard‡ for Business is WestJet’s flagship business credit card in Canada, packed with travel perks tailored to business owners and frequent WestJet flyers. Its most compelling features include complimentary WestJet Silver status that comes with as part of the welcome bonus, accelerated earning rates on both travel and key business spending categories, and automatic enrollment in the WestJet Biz Program for discounted fares.† This card is an excellent choice for small business owners or entrepreneurs who regularly travel with WestJet — whether for meetings, conferences, or a much-needed getaway — and want to maximize rewards while enjoying premium travel benefits.

[read-more ids="30109,176976"]

For a limited time, the welcome offer on the WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard for Businessǂ is 35,000 WestJet points upon making your first purchase.† The annual fee on this card is $175,† with up to 9 additional users costing $75† each.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

We value WestJet points at 1 cent per point, and using that value, we’d estimate that the total welcome offer of 35,000 WestJet points† is worth $350. Of course, you can certainly squeeze more value out of the card by leveraging the category earning rates and the card’s other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value.

With the WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard for Businessǂ, you’ll earn the following on everyday spending:

  • 3 WestJet points per dollar spent on eligible WestJet flights and WestJet Vacation packages† plus additional 3 WestJet Points from the Silver status.
  • 3 WestJet points per dollar spent on telecommunications, shipping, and electronics purchases†
  • 1.5 WestJet points per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases†
All WestJet points earned by supplementary business cardholders automatically pool into the primary business cardholder’s WestJet Rewards account — no transfers required.

WestJet Rewards prides itself on being a straightforward loyalty program that's easy to understand. For the most part, each WestJet point is worth 1 cent per point, which means that 100 WestJet points is worth $1, 1,000 WestJet points is worth $10, 10,000 WestJet points is worth $100, and so on. At the time of booking, WestJet points can be redeemed against the base fare, Other Air Transportation Charges ("Other ATC"), extra baggage, and seat selection at a value of 1 cent per point. You need to redeem at least 2,500 WestJet points for a flight, or at least 5,000 WestJet points for a WestJet Vacations package. After you've made a full redemption for everything on the ticket, if you have enough WestJet points remaining to cover the taxes and fees, you can choose to do so at a rate of 105–110 WestJet points = $1 (CAD) towards the taxes and fees. This results in a value of 0.91–0.95 cents per point. Unlike many other loyalty programs, the opportunities to score outsized value from your points are quite limited with the WestJet Rewards program. One way to squeeze more value is by combining a WestJet points redemption with a Member Exclusive Fare booking. While WestJet advertises Member Exclusive Fares as offering up to 65% off of base fares, you're much more likely to come across discounts of 5–10%. Indeed, this is the most valuable way to redeem WestJet points. Just be sure to compare the fare inclusions and exclusions of Member Exclusive Fares, since you may be better off with other fare types. [read-more ids="165246"]

For WestJet travellers, the WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard for Businessǂ offers a number of meaningful benefits.

Complimentary WestJet Silver Status

The primary business cardholder receives complimentary WestJet Silver status until December 31 of the following year after approval.† Benefits include:
  • Two free checked bags for you and up to eight guests on the same reservation†
  • Priority check-in and boarding†
  • Lounge passes†
Silver status can be maintained by spending $30,000 annually on the card after the complimentary benefit period. Checked baggage fees cost $35 or more per person, so this benefit alone could be worth up to $140 per round-trip if travelling as a couple, or potentially even more if you're travelling as a larger group or checking in a second baggage. If you're able to use this benefit, it easily justifies the card's annual fee on a rolling basis.

Status Lift

One of the more interesting features on this card is Status Lift, which helps you earn WestJet status through credit card spending. For every $5,000 spent on the card, you'll earn $200 in Tier Qualifying Spend,† which helps you earn WestJet Rewards status. You can get up to 50 Status Lifts each year, which would result in you achieving top-tier Platinum status without ever having set foot in a plane. This would require spending $250,000 on your card. As a reminder, WestJet's Elite Tiers come with the following amounts of Tier Qualifying Spend:
  • Silver: $4,000 ($100,000 of credit card spending)
  • Gold: $6,000 ($150,000 of credit card spending)
  • Platinum: $10,000 ($250,000 of credit card spending)
[read-more ids="153490"]

WestJet Biz Program

Primary cardholders are automatically enrolled in Tier 1 of the WestJet Biz Program†, which includes:
  • Up to 15% off flights on domestic, transborder, and transatlantic routes
  • Flexible booking through westjetbiz.com, your TMC, or your travel agent

Other Benefits

Other benefits include the ability to save 3¢/L on fuel at Petro-Canada whilst earning 20% more Petro-Points at Petro Canada by linking your card with your Petro-Points membership,† access to airport lounges as part of the Mastercard Travel Pass provided by DragonPass† ($32 (USD) per lounge visit), and access to the Mastercard Concierge Services for last-minute restaurant and event bookings.†

Plus, by linking the WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard for Businessǂ to Rexall account, cardholders can earn 50 BeWell points per dollar spent at Rexall. Finally, cardholders receive a 12-month complimentary DashPass subscription±, to enjoy unlimited deliveries with $0 delivery fees on orders of $15 or more when you pay with your eligible RBC credit card.†

As a travel credit card, the WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard for Businessǂ offers a suite of insurance coverage. Notably, if you redeem WestJet points on your booking and pay the remainder of the balance with your WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard for Businessǂ, the insurance coverage will apply.†

The WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercardǂ comes with the following insurance coverage:

  • Emergency medical insurance for the first 15 consecutive days of any out-of-province trips for travellers under the age of 65 or the first three consecutive days for those 65 and up†
  • Trip interruption insurance of up to $5,000 per covered person per trip, up to a maximum of $25,000†
  • Travel accident insurance of up to $500,000 against accidental death or dismemberment†
  • Flight delay insurance of up to $250 per day per covered person, up to an overall total of $500 per occurrence for all covered persons. Coverage begins four hours after a missed connection, delayed flight, or denied boarding.†
  • Baggage delay insurance of up to $500 per occurrence per covered person beginning four hours after arrival when your baggage is lost or delayed, up to an overall total of $2,500 per occurrence for all covered persons†
  • Auto rental damage and theft insurance, which can help you save on the insurance fees that rental companies like to impose for vehicles up to a MSRP of $65,000 and up to 48 consecutive days†
  • Hotel/motel burglary insurance of up to $2,500 per occurrence in the aggregate†
  • Purchase security protection of 90 days and extended warranty that doubles  the original warranty up to 1 year†
  • Mobile device protection of up to $2,000†

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite product, the WestJet RBC® World Elite Mastercard‡ for Business requires that you meet at least one of the following:

– Minimum personal annual income of $80,000
– Minimum household annual income of $150,000
– Minimum annual business revenue of $250,000
– Minimum annual spend of $25,000 on purchases eligible for direct payment through a credit card
– Minimum $400,000 in assets under management (AUM)


Please refer to RBC® page for up to date offer terms and conditions.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. RBC is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Digital applications are not currently available for this card; applicants need to call the RBC Advice Centre or visit an RBC Branch.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the RBC® British Airways Visa Infinite†, the British Airways co-branded credit card issued by RBC®.

acf.subtitle:
60,000 Avios†

$1,235 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Collect a 30,000 Avios† Welcome Bonus when you spend $5,000 (CAD) in the first three months of account opening
  • Then, collect a further 30,000 bonus Avios† when you spend $5,000 (CAD) in months 4-6 of account opening
  • If you do not meet the $5,000 (CAD) spend in the first three months, you can still collect 30,000 bonus Avios if you meet the spend requirement in months 4-6.
  • Plus, earn 3 Avios per $1 (CAD) spent† on British Airways and British Airways Holidays†
  • And, earn 2 Avios per $1 (CAD) spent† on dining and food delivery purchases
  • Lastly, earn 1 Avios per $1 (CAD) spent† on all other eligible purchases
  • Get a Companion Award eVoucher for an Avios redemption on British Airways flights upon spending $30,000 in a calendar year†
  • Annual fee: $165†

card_benefits.b_content:
Collect a 30,000 Avios† Welcome Bonus when you spend $5,000 CAD in the first three months of account opening†
Collect a further 30,000 bonus Avios† when you spend $5,000 CAD in months 4-6 of account opening†
Total of up to 60,000 Avios†

20.50% purchases
22.99% cash advances (21.99% QC)
22.99% balance transfers (21.99% QC)

Earn 3 Avios per $1 (CAD) spent on British Airways and British Airways Holidays†
Earn 2 Avios per $1 (CAD) spent on dining†
Earn 1 Avios per $1 (CAD) spent on all other qualifying purchases†

Companion Award eVoucher when you spend $30,000 on the card in one calendar year†
10% off British Airways flights†
Strong

card_content.c_content:
The RBC® British Airways Visa Infinite† is the only British Airways co-branded card in Canada, making this a great choice if you're loyal to British Airways.

For other travellers, this card still offers a compelling signup bonus, and a Companion Award eVoucher should you spend at least $30,000 in one calendar year.†

The RBC® British Airways Visa Infinite† offers a signup bonus of up to 60,000 Avios, and here's how to receive them:

  • Collect a 30,000 Avios† Welcome Bonus when you spend $5,000 in the first three months of account opening†

  • Collect a further 30,000 bonus Avios† when you spend another $5,000 in months 4-6 of account opening†

If you do not meet the $5,000 CAD spend in the first three months, you can still collect 30,000 bonus Avios if you meet the spend requirement in months 4-6.

The annual fee on this card is $165†. If you have an RBC® VIP Banking Account, you'll be able to get a fee waiver on one of RBC®'s premium credit cards, including this one.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

Here’s a breakdown of what the welcome bonus for the RBC® British Airways Visa Infinite† is worth. Keep in mind that this doesn’t take into consideration the potential value of other card benefits, which we’ll outline in detail below. We value Avios® at 2 cents per point, placing the 60,000-point welcome bonus at $1200 in value. Spending $10,000 at the base earn rate of 1 point per dollar earns you an additional 10,000 Avios, worth $200. After subtracting the $165 annual fee, the net first-year value you can expect to get from this card’s welcome bonus is $1,235, and that’s before factoring in any ongoing card benefits. Of course, your actual value could be even higher by leveraging the card’s other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value.

Among other things, 60,000 Avios® can get you a one-way flight in economy class from Vancouver to Asia, up to three one-way flights from the West Coast to Hawaii or from Toronto to Dublin, or up to eight one-way flights on any short-haul trips you plan to take with Oneworld carriers along your travels around the world.

Earning those points for $165 is a pretty great deal, and definitely one to consider if you can manage the minimum spending of $10,000.

The RBC® British Airways Visa Infinite† Card offers the following earning rates:

  • Earn 3 Avios per $1 (CAD) spent on British Airways and British Airways Holidays†
  • Earn 2 Avios per $1 (CAD) spent on dining†
  • Earn 1 Avios per $1 (CAD) spent on all other qualifying purchases†

Unlike many other loyalty programs, Avios redemptions depends on the total mileage flown. Avios uses multiple mileage bands, divided into zones.

Furthermore, connecting flights can often be a detriment since by adding a connection, you might have to pay more miles, since your total flying distance will be higher.

Therefore, the best uses for British Airways Avios tend to be for direct flights, and those just under the threshold of the zone limits. Upon earning 70,000 Avios from this card in the first year (including the points earned upon completing the minimum spending requirement), you could:
  • Fly two people from Seattle to Hawaii roundtrip
  • Fly from Toronto to Dublin in one-way in business class
  • Fly multiple short-haul flights starting from 6,000 Avios one-way
  • Fly from Dubai to Paris in one-way in business class
  • Earn 15,000 more Avios, for a total of 70,000 Avios, and fly Qatar Airways Qsuites from North America to the Middle East
The sky really is the limit with Avios, since the price can depend on which carrier you're flying and which region you're flying in. [read-more ids="1263"]

This card also comes with a Companion Award eVoucher when you spend $30,000 on the card in one calendar year†. This voucher allows you to add a companion to your ticket for free when you redeem your Avios for a round-trip flight with British Airways originating in and returning to Canada.

You can also choose to apply the voucher to a solo booking, which would reduce the number of Avios required by 50%. [read-more ids="468"]

While this sounds like a great deal, keep in mind that Avios redemptions on British Airways long-haul flights come with significant fuel surcharges in the region of $600 in economy class or $1,000 in business class, which can eat into your savings.

However, if you value direct flights from Canada to London, this can be a very valuable benefit. Lastly, you'll also receive 10% off when booking paid flights with British Airways using this card†.

As a Visa Infinite† product, this credit card comes with comprehensive premium travel insurance, including:

  • Emergency medical insurance† for the first 31 consecutive days of any out-of-province trips for travellers under 65, or the first seven consecutive days for those 65 and up

  • Flight delay and emergency purchases insurance† in the event of a missed connection, delayed flight departure, or denied boarding

  • Auto rental damage† and theft insurance†, which can help you save on the insurance fees that car rental companies charge for vehicles up to a MSRP of $65,000

  • Hotel burglary insurance† of up to $2,500 in total

  • Travel accident insurance† of up to $500,000 against accidental death or dismemberment

The card also comes with strong purchase protection benefits†, including an extended warranty that doubles the original manufacturer's warranty up to a maximum of one year†, and coverage against accidental loss or damage of personal property for up to 90 days from the date of purchase†.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a Visa Infinite† product, this credit card has a minimum personal annual income requirement of $60,000 or a minimum household income of $100,000 to be eligible.

Refer to RBC® page for up to date offer terms and conditions.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. RBC is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card, a no-fee cash back credit card by CIBC that earns modest rewards on daily spending.

features.feature: 2% cash back on eligible grocery purchases†
1% cash back on eligible gas, EV charging, transportation, and dining†

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 2% cash back on eligible grocery purchases†
    • Plus, earn 1% cash back on eligible gas, transportation, dining, EV charging purchases, and recurring payments†
    • Also, earn 0.5% cash back on all other purchases†
    • Get a 12-month Skip+ free trial and other exclusive benefits when you link your CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card with Skip.†
  • Minimum income: $15,000 household
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content: †Terms and Conditions apply. The information for the CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.
No signup bonus

21.99% purchase†
22.99% cash advance† (21.99% for Quebec residents)
22.99% balance transfer† (21.99% for Quebec residents)

2% cash back on eligible grocery purchases†
1% cash back on eligible gas, transportation, dining, electric vehicle charging purchases, and recurring payments†
0.5% cash back on all other purchases†
Basic

card_content.c_content: The CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card is an excellent card for students, newcomers to Canada, or individuals with new or recovering credit files who want to build a strong relationship with CIBC while earning consistent cash back.

Currently there is no welcome bonus with the CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card. The lower interest rate on purchases may come in handy, in case you have an impending purchase that you'll need to finance in more manageable terms. Moreover, the card has no annual fee for both the primary cardholder and any supplementary cardholders.† It's a solid choice to keep open as a free primary or backup credit card, all while supporting the longevity of your credit history.

The CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card offers the following earning rates:
  • 2% cash back on eligible grocery purchases†
  • 1% cash back on eligible gas, EV charging, transportation, dining purchases, and recurring bill payments†
  • 0.5% cash back on all other eligible purchases†

With CIBC's cash back cards, the primary cardholder may redeem cash back at any time during the year, provided they have a minimum cash back balance of $10 at the time the redemption request is made.

That's a relatively flexible policy compared to other cash back credit cards on the market, some of which only allow you to claim your cash back earnings once a year.

As is typical for credit cards that earn cash back instead of travel rewards, the CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card comes with a limited set of perks. As a cardholder, you have the ability to save up to 10 cents per litre† at participating Chevron, Ultramar, Pioneer, and Fas Gas gas stations† when you link your card with the Journie Rewards gas loyalty program.†

The CIBC Dividend® Visa Card* offers extended warranty, which doubles the manufacturer’s warranty for up to one additional year, and purchase protection against accidental damage or theft within 90 days of purchase.†

colum_block.col1_text:
This credit card has a minimum household income requirement of $15,000.

You can follow the below link to the CIBC website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and Conditions apply. The information for the CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

acf.subtitle: 0% promotional interest rate for balance transfers†

features.feature: 1% balance transfer fee (BT fee does not apply for Quebec residents)

acf.featured_info:
• 0% interest rate for 10 months on balance transfers made at the time of application†
  • 1% balance transfer fee† (balance transfer fee does not apply for Quebec residents)
  • Annual fee: $29 (annual fee rebate for the first year)

card_benefits.b_content: †Terms and Conditions apply. The information for the CIBC Select Visa* Card has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.
0% interest for up to 10 months with a 1% transfer fee.† (balance transfer fee does not apply for Quebec residents)

13.99% purchases†
13.99% cash advances†
This card does not earn rewards
Basic

card_content.c_content: The CIBC Select Visa* Card is one of Canada's best options for balance transfers, with a promotional interest rate of 0%† for 10 months† and a balance transfer fee of 1%.† (balance transfer does not apply for Quebec residents)

If you're approved for the CIBC Select Visa* Card, you can enjoy a promotional 0% annual interest rate† on balance transfers made at the time of application.† The promotional 0% interest rate is valid for 10 months†, and there's a 1% balance transfer fee.†

You can transfer up to 50% of your assigned credit limit.†

As part of the current offer, the card's $29 annual fee is rebated for the first year.†

The primary benefit of this card is its promotional balance transfer rate. If you link your CIBC Select Visa* Card and your Journie Rewards card, you can save up to 10 cents per litre of gasoline at participating Pioneer, Fas Gas, Ultramar, and Chevron gas stations.† With the CIBC Select Visa* Card, there aren't any transaction fees when you send a CIBC Global Money Transfer™†.
The CIBC Select Visa* Card comes with optional CIBC Travel Medical Insurance†, as well as optional  CIBC Payment Protector™ Insurance for Credit Cards.†

colum_block.col1_text:
You must have a $15,000 minimum annual household income to apply.

Follow the link below to the CIBC® website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: This is a digital-exclusive offer.† The information for the CIBC Select Visa* Card has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the CIBC Costco Mastercard, a no-fee cash back credit card by CIBC that is geared towards Costco-spenders, with high earning rates on Costco spend.

acf.subtitle: Up to 3% cash back

features.feature: 3% cash back on at Costco gas and restaurants†

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 1% 3% cash back on restaurants and Costco Gas†
  • Plus, earn 1% 2% cash back on gas and Costco.ca†
  • Use your credit card as your Costco membership card
  • Mobile device insurance
  • Annual fee: $0
  • Minimum income: $15,000 household

card_benefits.b_content:
20.75% purchase
22.49% cash advance
22.49% balance transfer

3% cash back on restaurants and Costco gas†
2% cash back on non-Costco gas and Costco.ca purchases†
1% cash back on all other purchases†
Moderate

card_content.c_content: The CIBC Costco Mastercard is an excellent card for those those who primarily fill gas at Costco, as well as those who who purchase from Costco online. The information for the CIBC Costco Mastercard has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

The CIBC Costco Mastercard generally doesn't offer any welcome bonuses for new cardholders. The card has no annual fee for both the primary cardholder and any supplementary cardholders. It's a solid choice to keep open as a free primary or backup credit card, all while supporting the longevity of your credit history.

The CIBC Costco Mastercard offers the following earning rates:
  • 3% cash back on Costco gas, and restaurants†
  • 2% cash back on non-Costco gas, and at Costco.ca†
  • 1% cash back on all other purchases†
All elevated cash back on gas is limited to the first $5,000 (1% thereafter) per calendar year, so it's in your best interest to only use this card at Costco gas stations, where you'll earn a 3% return. The 2% cash back when buying Costco online also only applies to the first $8,000 spent, and will earn 1% thereafter per calendar year. For a no-fee credit card, the return on spend is excellent until the prescribed limit. In fact, a 3% return on restaurant spend without any spending cap is ridiculously good for a free card, and even higher than many premium cards. Further, no other no-fee credit cards can compete with 3% cash back earnings on gas. After reaching $5,000, you can switch over to another Mastercard when buying gas at Costco, such as the Tangerine World Mastercard, which also has no annual fee and earns a respectable 2% cash back if you select it as one of your three preferred categories. A 2% return at Costco.ca is also a high return for a no-fee card, given the uniqueness of the category; every other credit card would have this in the base earning category, making this extremely competitive, even among premium cards – at least until you reach the $8,000 cap. This is easily optimized by making large electronic purchases online instead of in-store (since you only earn 1% cash back in-store) and still benefit from Costco's fair pricing and excellent warranty.

As is always the case with Costco-affiliated credit cards, members will receive their cash back every January in the form of a gift certificate.

This is a relatively weaker proposition than other cash back credit cards, since you receive a Costco gift certificate rather than cash. However, card members of this particular card likely do spend regularly at Costco, so the gift certificate can be valued at face value.

Note that there is no option to receive cash back at any other time of year, as they are only issued every January.
The CIBC Costco Mastercard doesn't offer any additional benefits or perks.

The insurance benefits on the CIBC Costco Mastercard are fairly limited, given the nature of this card for those who spend regularly at Costco rather than the avid traveller. The most notable insurance inclusion is Mobile Device Insurance, offering $1,000 in coverage per occurrence. This insurance is relatively new to credit cards in general, and is good to see on a no-fee card. CIBC themselves do not offer this insurance on any of their other no-fee cards, and the only other no-fee card to offer it is the Brim Mastercard; but unlike the CIBC Costco Mastercard, Brim has a deductible as well as a much less generous payout policy, subject to depreciation. In addition, it offers extended warranty (doubling the manufacturer's standard warranty, up to one additional year) and purchase protection (against accidental damage or theft within 90 days).

colum_block.col1_text:
This credit card has a $15,000 minimum annual income (individual).

You can follow the below link to the CIBC website to learn more about this card.

title.rendered: CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege* Card

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege* Card, the premium travel rewards card by CIBC that earns CIBC Aventura Points.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 80,000 CIBC Aventura Points

$276 first-year value

features.feature: Six complimentary Visa Airport Companion Program lounge visits†

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 25,000 CIBC Aventura points upon spending $3,000 in the first four months†
  • Plus, earn 25,000 CIBC Aventura points upon spending $6,000 in the first four months†
  • And, earn 30,000 CIBC Aventura points as an additional anniversary bonus upon spending $25,000 in the first 12 monthly statement periods†
  • Plus, earn 3x CIBC Aventura Points on all purchases through the CIBC Rewards Centre†
  • Plus, earn 2x CIBC Aventura Points on eligible gas stations, groceries, dining, entertainment, transit, and EV charging purchases†
  • Plus, receive an annual $200 travel credit†
  • Visa Airport Companion Program membership with six free lounge visits per year†
  • $200 NEXUS application credit†
  • Minimum income: $200,000 personal or household
  • Annual fee: $499

card_benefits.b_content: †Terms and Conditions apply. The information for the CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege* Card has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

25,000 Aventura Points upon spending $3,000 in the first four months†
25,000 Aventura Points upon spending $6,000 in the first four months†
30,000 Aventura Points as an additional anniversary bonus upon spending $25,000 in the first 12 monthly statement periods†
Total of up to 80,000 Aventura Points†

20.99% purchase
22.99% cash advance (21.99% QC)
22.99% balance transfer (21.99% QC)

3 Aventura Points per dollar spent on eligible travel purchases via the CIBC Rewards Centre†
2 Aventura Points per dollar spent on eligible dining, entertainment, transportation, gas stations and grocery purchases†
1.25 Aventura Points per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases†

$200 annual travel credit†
Six Visa Airport Companion Program lounge visits†
$200 NEXUS application fee rebate every four years†
Very strong

card_content.c_content:
The CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege* Card, is the top-tier Aventura offering from CIBC, with a $200,000 household income requirement.

This card is best suited for those who actively collect Aventura Points to offset the cost of travel.
The information for the CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege* Card, has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.
[read-more ids="30119,30107"]

The CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege* Card is currently offering up to 80,000 Aventura Points as a signup bonus,† structured as follows:

  • Earn 25,000 Aventura Points upon spending $3,000 in the first four months as a cardholder†
  • Earn 25,000 Aventura Points upon spending $6,000 in the first four months as a cardholder†
  • Earn 30,000 Aventura Points as anniversary bonus when you spend $25,000 in the first year†
The initial bonus is decent for a premium card, so this offer would be a good choice for those looking to rack up a large sum of Aventura Points quickly.

The card has an annual fee of $499; however, there are a few ways to reduce it.

You'll get a rebate of $139 if you have a CIBC Smart Plus Chequing Account. The premium account has monthly fees of $29.95, which can be waived with a $6,000 minimum balance.† Cardholders receive a $200 annual travel credit each year on January 1.† It can't be rolled over into the next calendar year, and it must be booked over the phone with the CIBC Travel Rewards Centre.† If you take advantage of both of these rebates, you can reduce your net annual fee to $160.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

Here’s a breakdown of the minimum value you can expect from the CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege Card’s welcome bonus. Note that this doesn’t factor in the card’s ongoing perks, which we’ll outline separately below. We value CIBC Aventura points at 1 cent per point, so the initial 50,000-point welcome bonus is worth $500.
Assuming you spend the required $6,000 to unlock both portions of the first year welcome bonus and earn at the card’s base rate of 1.25 points per dollar spent, you’d earn at least 7,500 Aventura points, $75 in value. Add in the $200 annual travel credit, which is easy to use toward any travel booked via CIBC Rewards, and you’re looking at $775 in total value.
After subtracting the $499 annual fee, your net first-year value comes out to $276. If you choose to renew the card into the second year and meet the $25,000 anniversary spend requirement, you’ll unlock an additional 30,000 Aventura points. That brings your total welcome bonus to 80,000 points, or $800. Assuming you spend $25,000 over the first year at 1.25x points, that earns you 31,250 points — worth $312.50.
Add two years of travel credits ($400 total), then subtract two annual fees ($998), and your minimum net value across two years comes to approximately $514.50. Of course, this is a conservative valuation based on minimum spend and baseline earn rates. Your value could be higher if you take advantage of elevated earning categories, insurance coverage, lounge access, or Visa Infinite Privilege perks.

This credit card earns CIBC Aventura Points on daily spending at the following rates:

  • Earn 3 Aventura Points per dollar spent on eligible travel booked through the CIBC Rewards Centre†
  • Earn 2 Aventura Points per dollar spent on eligible gas stations, grocery, electric vehicle charging, and drugstore purchases†
  • Earn 1.25 Aventura Point per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases†

CIBC Aventura Points can be redeemed in a variety of ways. However, the most valuable way to redeem Aventura Points is on travel purchases. For example, you can redeem Aventura Points for flights booked through the CIBC Rewards Centre at a rate of 1 cent per point.† You can also use CIBC Aventura Points towards a $1,000 (CAD) airline ticket in the “Canada and U.S. (long-haul)” category on the Aventura Airline Rewards Chart, increasing the value of your points from 1 cent per point (cpp) to 2.28cpp. Generally speaking, it's recommended to redeem Aventura Points via the above chart whenever possible, since it offers the maximal value for your points. It's worth noting that this must be done through CIBC's in-house travel portal. However, Aventura Points can also be useful for offsetting the cost of travel that's otherwise difficult to book with points. For example, Aventura Points can be used to cover the cost of independent hotels, short-term rentals, car rentals, trains, or flights that can't be booked with other programs, such as Aeroplan or Marriott Bonvoy. [read-more title="Read More" ids="81635" ]

As the primary cardholder, you’ll enjoy six complimentary Visa Airport Companion Program lounge visits per membership year for you and any guests.†

Six lounge visits per year can be useful if you don't tend to travel very often and don't otherwise have lounge access with another credit card

Another unique feature is the NEXUS rebate, which covers up to $200 (CAD) once every four years when the fee is charged to your CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege* Card.†

NEXUS is a frequent traveller program designed to speed up your travels between Canada and the United States, and you’ll receive a rebate whether your application is approved or not, as long as your account is in good standing at the time of the charge.

The CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege* Card is marketed as a high-end credit card and therefore comes with all the key insurance policies that a frequent traveller would expect. As a premium card, coverage is excellent, on many of the same categories as other cards and features better coverage for those over the age of 65.

This includes out-of-province emergency medical insurance of up to $5,000,000,† plus coverage for flight and baggage delays,† trip cancellation and interruption,† hotel burglary,† and car rental loss or damage.†

There’s also a very strong Mobile Device Insurance policy.† By paying for your smartphone, tablet, or smartwatch using your CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege* Card, you’ll get up to $1,500 in the event your mobile device is lost, stolen or accidentally damaged, for up to 24 months!

colum_block.col1_text:
As a Visa Infinite Privilege product, the CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege* Card requires a minimum personal annual income $150,000 or a minimum household annual income of $200,000 to be eligible. You'll also need to be approved for a credit limit of at least $10,000.

Follow the below link to the CIBC website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

historical_offers.notes: 30,000 Aventura Points as an additional anniversary bonus when you have at least $25,000 in net eligible purchases posted on your account during the first 12 monthly statement periods†
30,000 Aventura Points as an additional anniversary bonus when you have at least $25,000 in net eligible purchases posted on your account during the first 12 monthly statement periods†

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. The information for the CIBC Aventura® Visa Infinite Privilege* Card for Business has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Scotia Momentum® Visa Infinite* Card, the flagship cash back credit card issued by Scotiabank.

acf.subtitle:
10% cash back

$200 first-year value

features.feature: Bonus cash back on groceries & bills

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 10% cash back in the first three months, up to $200 total cash back for spending $2,000
  • Plus, earn 4% cash back on groceries and recurring bill payments
  • Also, earn 2% cash back on gas and transit
  • Minimum income: $60,000 personal or $100,000 household
  • Annual fee: $120 (waived for the first year)

card_benefits.b_content: 10% in the first three months, up to $200 total cash back for spending $2,000<sup>†</sup>

20.99% purchases<sup>†</sup>
22.99% cash advances<sup>†</sup>
22.99% balance transfers<sup>†</sup>
$120 (waived for the first year)<sup>†</sup>

4% cash back for groceries and recurring bills<sup>†</sup>
2% cash back for gas and transit<sup>†</sup>
1% cash back for all other purchases<sup>†</sup>
Comprehensive

card_content.c_content:
The Scotia Momentum® Visa Infinite* Card targets high cash back returns in all the right categories, including groceries and gas. Notably, this card offers a 4% return on all recurring payments and groceries, as well as a 2% return on gas and transit. This card is well-suited to those looking for a strong cash back card.

[read-more ids="30102,30129"]

The Scotia Momentum® Visa Infinite* Card is currently offering 10% cash back in the first three months, up to $2,000 in spending. This is equal to $200 in cash back.

The annual fee of $120 is waived for the first year. Supplementary cards cost $50 apiece, which are also waived for the first year as part of the promotion.

The Scotia Momentum® Visa Infinite* card has a tiered rewards structure, earning higher cash back rates in popular categories:

  • 4% cash back on groceries and recurring payments
  • 2% cash back on gas and transit
  • 1% cash back on all other purchases
One of this card's unique features is that it offers quite a high return on recurring payments. This includes any pre-authorized payments to a biller, such as a utility company. Scotiabank has also partnered with popular subscription services, including digital streaming services and meal kit deliveries, to classify them as recurring payments. However, other non-recurring entertainment or delivery purchases won't qualify for the 4% rate. You can earn at an accelerated rate on up to $25,000 spent per year. That is, you have a combined limit of $25,000 on groceries and recurring payments, and a combined limit of $25,000 on gas and transit. If you spend more than these limits, you'll earn 1% on thereafter.

Cash back can be redeemed to your account at any time, as long as you’ve accumulated at least $25 and your account is open and in good standing.

The welcome bonus cash back is awarded separately from your regular rewards. Your regular cash back posts as usual on each statement, while the bonus portion becomes available for redemption approximately four months after your account open date.

For example, if you buy groceries during the welcome bonus period, you’d earn 4% regular cash back that can be redeemed once you reach the $25 threshold, plus 6% bonus cash back that becomes available for redemption about four months after you open the account, for a total welcome bonus rate of 10%.

Scotia Momentum® Visa Infinite* cardholders can save up to 25% on car rentals at participating Avis and Budget locations in Canada and the US.

The card also comes with all of the usual Visa Infinite benefits, including Concierge service, the Luxury Hotel Collection, and the Dining and Wine Country program.

The Scotia Momentum® Visa Infinite* card comes with strong insurance benefits. Coverage includes:

  • Emergency medical insurance of up to $1 million for you, your spouse, and dependent children on unexpected medical illness or injury when travelling out-of-province for 15 days

  • Trip cancellation and interruption insurance of up to $1,500 and $2,000 per person, respectively

  • Flight delay insurance for delays of more than four hours, up to $500 per person

  • Delayed and lost baggage insurance of up to $500

  • Rental car collision or damage insurance, which can help you save on the insurance fees usually charged by car rental companies for vehicles with a MSRP of up to $65,000

Plus, on the purchase protection side, you’ll get a 90-day protection period against damage, loss, or theft for most new items. In addition, you also get extended warranty of up to one year.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a Visa Infinite product, this credit card has a minimum personal annual income requirement of $60,000 or a minimum household income of $100,000, or have $250,000 in assets under management with Scotiabank to be eligible. You'll also need to be approved for a credit limit of at least $5,000.

Follow the link below to the Scotiabank website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. See Card Provider's website and Card Application for complete card details, terms and current offers. Reasonable efforts are made to maintain accuracy of information.

title.rendered: CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card for Students

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card for Students, a no-fee cash back card that earns modest rewards on daily spending.

acf.subtitle:
Up to $125 cash back†

$125 first-year value

features.feature: 2% cash back on eligible grocery store purchases†
1% cash back on eligible gas, EV charging, transportation, dining, and recurring bill payments†

acf.featured_info:
• Get $25 cash back after you make your first purchase within the first four months.†
  • Earn a 10% cash back welcome bonus of up to $100 within the first 4 monthly statements on net purchases up to $1,000.†
  • Then, earn 2% cash back on eligible grocery store purchases†
  • Also, earn 1% cash back on eligible gas, transportation, dining, EV charging purchases, and recurring payments†
  • Free SPC+ membership for students†
  • No minimum income requirement†
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content: †Terms and Conditions apply. The information for the CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card for Students has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

Get $25 cash back after you make your first purchase within the first four months.†
Earn a 10% cash back welcome bonus of up to $100 within the first 4 monthly statements on net purchases up to $1,000.†

21.99% purchase
22.99% cash advance (21.99% for Quebec residents)
22.99% balance transfer (21.99% for Quebec residents)

2% cash back on eligible grocery store purchases†
1% cash back on eligible gas, transportation, dining, electric vehicle charging purchases, and recurring payments†
0.5% cash back on all other eligible purchases†
Basic

card_content.c_content: The CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card for Students is an excellent card for students who want to build a strong relationship with CIBC while earning consistent cash back.

Currently the welcome bonus on the CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card for Students is as follows:
  • Get $25 cash back after you make your first purchase within the first four months.†
  • Earn a 10% cash back welcome bonus of up to $100 within the first 4 monthly statements on net purchases up to $1,000.†
The card has no annual fee for both the primary cardholder and any supplementary cardholders.† It's a solid choice to keep open as a free primary or backup credit card, all while supporting the longevity of your credit history.

The CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card for Students offers the following earning rates:
  • 2% cash back on eligible grocery store purchases†
  • 1% cash back on eligible gas, EV charging, transportation, dining purchases, and recurring bill payments†
  • 0.5% cash back on all other eligible purchases†

With CIBC's cash back cards, the primary cardholder may redeem cash back at any time during the year, provided they have a minimum cash back balance of $10 at the time the redemption request is made.<sup>2</sup>

That's a relatively flexible policy compared to other cash back credit cards on the market, some of which only allow you to claim your cash back earnings once a year.

As is typical for credit cards that earn cash back instead of travel rewards, the CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card for Students comes with a limited set of perks. As a cardholder, you have the ability to save up to 10 cents per litre† at participating Chevron, Ultramar, Pioneer, and Fas Gas gas stations† when you link your card with the Journie Rewards gas loyalty program.† Eligible student clients can get a free SPC+ membership, which offers deals, discounts, and giveaways with major brands.†

The CIBC Dividend® Visa Card* for Students offers extended warranty, which doubles the manufacturer’s warranty for up to one additional year, and purchase protection against accidental damage or theft within 90 days of purchase.†

colum_block.col1_text:
This credit card has no minimum income requirement.

You can follow the below link to the CIBC website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and Conditions apply. The information for the CIBC Dividend® Visa* Card for Students has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

title.rendered: CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Students

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Students, a no-fee credit card that earns modest rewards in the CIBC Aventura program.

acf.subtitle:
12,500 CIBC Aventura Points†

$100 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 2,500 CIBC Aventura Points upon completing five qualifying activities within 60 days of account approval†
  • Then earn 10,000 Aventura Points upon spending $1,000 within the first four monthly statement periods†
  • Total of up to 12,500 Aventura Points
  • Plus, earn 1x CIBC Aventura Points on all eligible purchases through the CIBC Rewards Travel Centre†
  • Plus, earn 1x CIBC Aventura Points on eligible gas stations, grocery stores, EV charging, and drugstore purchases†
  • Get a 12-month Skip+ free trial and other exclusive benefits when you link your CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Students with Skip†
  • Free SPC+ membership
  • No minimum income requirement†
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content: †Terms and Conditions apply. The information for the CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Students has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

2,500 Aventura Points upon completing five qualifying activities within 60 days of account approval†
10,000 Aventura Points when you spend $1,000 in the first four monthly statement periods†
Total of up to 12,500 Aventura Points

21.99% purchase
22.99% cash advance (21.99% for Quebec residents)
22.99% balance transfer (21.99% for Quebec residents)

1 Aventura Point per dollar spent on eligible travel purchases via the CIBC Rewards Centre†
1 Aventura Point per dollar spent on eligible gas stations, grocery stores, EV charging, and drugstores†
1 Aventura Point per $2 spent on all other eligible purchases†
Weak

card_content.c_content: The CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Students is an entry-level credit card with no annual fee, suitable for anyone interested in earning Aventura Points for travel. This version of the card comes with no minimum income requirement.†

The CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Students is offering up to 12,500 Aventura Points for new cardholders,† distributed as follows:

  • Earn 2,500 CIBC Aventura Points upon completing five qualifying activities within 60 days of account approval†
  • Earn 10,000 Aventura Points when you spend $1,000 in the first four monthly statement periods†
The card has no annual fee for primary or supplementary cardholders.

The CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Students offers 1 Aventura Point per dollar spent† on eligible purchases at gas stations, EV charging, grocery stores, and drug stores, as well as on eligible travel purchases made through the CIBC Rewards Centre.† Otherwise, you'll earn 1 Aventura Point per $2 spent on all other eligible purchases.†

CIBC Aventura Points can be redeemed in a variety of ways. You can use CIBC Aventura Points towards an $800 (CAD) airline ticket in the “Canada and U.S. (Long-Haul)” category on the Aventura Flight Rewards Chart, which could increase the value of your points from 1 cent per point (cpp) to 2.28 cents per point. Generally speaking, it's recommended to redeem Aventura Points via the Aventura Flight Rewards Chart whenever possible, since it offers the maximum value for your points. It's worth noting that this must be done through the CIBC Rewards Centre. [read-more title="Read More" ids="159973"] However, Aventura Points can also be useful for offsetting the cost of travel that's otherwise difficult to book with points. For example, Aventura Points can be used to cover the cost of eligible independent hotels, short-term rentals, car rentals, trains, or flights that can't be booked with other airline- or hotel-specific loyalty programs. [read-more title="Read More" ids="81635" ]

Cardholders of the CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Students can save up to 10¢ per litre at participating Pioneer, Fas Gas, Ultramar, and Chevron gas stations.† To benefit from this discount, you'll need to link your card with Journie Rewards, and then make qualifying purchases at eligible gas stations.† As a cardholder, you'll also get a complimentary SPC+ membership†, which offers discounts of up to 30% at top brands, and more!†

The CIBC Aventura® Visa Card* for Students includes a standard insurance package for a no-fee card, featuring $100,000 in Common Carrier Accident Insurance, 90-day Purchase Security, and an Extended Warranty that adds up to one additional year on top of the manufacturer’s coverage.†

colum_block.col1_text:
This card has no minimum income requirement.

You can follow the below link to the CIBC website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: Terms and Conditions apply.† The information for the CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Students has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.
