card_content.c_content: The CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card is an entry-level credit card with no annual fee, suitable for anyone interested in earning Aventura Points for travel.

The CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card is offering up to 12,500 Aventura Points for new cardholders,† distributed as follows:
  • Earn 2,500 Aventura Points upon completing the following five activities within 60 days of account opening:
    • Share your email address with CIBC†
    • Sign up for eStatements for your new Aventura Visa card†
    • Add an authorized user on your new Aventura Visa card†
    •  Hold any eligible CIBC Chequing Account† 
    •  Add your Aventura Visa card with Apple Pay® or Samsung Pay® or Google Pay™†
  • Earn 10,000 Aventura Points when you spend $1,000 in the first four monthly statement periods

The card has no annual fee for primary or supplementary cardholders.

The CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card offers:
  • 1 Aventura Point per dollar spent† on eligible purchases at gas stations, EV charging, grocery stores, and drug stores, as well as on eligible travel purchases made through the CIBC Rewards Centre.†
  • 1 Aventura Point per $2 spent on all other eligible purchases.†

CIBC Aventura Points can be redeemed in a variety of ways. However, you can also redeem CIBC Aventura Points for eligible travel booked in any way at a rate of 1 cent per point (cpp). This is the an average flexible travel redemption rate for fixed-value points in Canada. You can also use CIBC Aventura Points towards an $800 (CAD) airline ticket in the “Canada and U.S. (Long-Haul)” category on the Aventura Flight Rewards Chart, which could increase the value of your points from 1 cent per point (cpp) to 2.28 cents per point. Generally speaking, it's recommended to redeem Aventura Points via the Aventura Flight Rewards Chart whenever possible, since it offers the maximum value for your points. It's worth noting that this must be done through the CIBC Rewards Centre. [read-more title="Read More" ids="159973"] However, Aventura Points can also be useful for offsetting the cost of travel that's otherwise difficult to book with points. For example, Aventura Points can be used to cover the cost of eligible independent hotels, short-term rentals, car rentals, trains, or flights that can't be booked with other airline- or hotel-specific loyalty programs. [read-more title="Read More" ids="81635" ]

Cardholders of the CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card can save up to 10¢ per litre at participating Pioneer, Fas Gas, Ultramar, and Chevron gas stations.† To benefit from this discount, you'll need to link your card with Journie Rewards, and then make qualifying purchases at eligible gas stations.†

The CIBC Aventura® Visa Card* includes a standard insurance package for a no-fee card, featuring $100,000 in Common Carrier Accident Insurance, 90-day Purchase Security, and an Extended Warranty that adds up to one additional year on top of the manufacturer’s coverage.†

colums.col2_text:
The card has a minimum income requirement of $15,000 (household).

You can follow the below link to the CIBC website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

If you don't qualify for this card, you might be eligible for the CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Students instead. It has all the same earn rates and perks, but with no income requirement.

You can follow the below link to the CIBC website to apply for the CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card for Students.

acf.legal_disclaimers: Terms and Conditions apply.† The information for the CIBC Aventura® Visa* Card has been collected independently by Prince of Travel. The card details on this page have not been reviewed or provided by the card issuer.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Neo World Elite® Mastercard, one of the newest credit cards from Neo Financial that offers cash back rewards.

acf.subtitle: Up to 7% cash back†

features.feature: Access to more cash back with Neo Partners

acf.featured_info:
• 5% cash back on groceries†
  • 3% cash back on gas and EV charging†
  • 4% cash back on recurring payments†
  • 1% cash back on everything else†
  • Boosted rate of up to 7% cash back when used in combination with the Neo Everyday account†
  • $80,000 minimum personal income†
  • $150,000 minimum household income†
  • Annual fee: $125

card_benefits.b_content: This card currently does not offer a welcome bonus
Standard World Elite benefits including Concierge service, discounts on Cirque du Soleil shows, extended warranty, purchase protection, and more
Strong

card_content.c_content: Neo Financial has three personal cash back credit cards in their line up. The Neo World Elite® Mastercard is Neo Financial’s most premium credit card that comes with high cash back earning potential. You can earn upwards of 7% on groceries if you meet Neo Financial’s criteria.

The Neo World Elite® Mastercard earns cash back rewards at the following rates for all card holders:
  • 5% cash back per $1 spent on groceries on the first $1,000 each month
  • 4% cash back per $1 spent on recurring bills on the first $500 each month
  • 3% cash back per $1 spent on gas and EV charging on the first $1,000 each month
  • 1% cash back per $1 spent on everything else on the first $3,300 each month
If you have a Neo Everyday account you’ll get a boost in your earning rate depending on how much you keep in your account. If you have at least $5,000 in your Neo Everyday Account you’ll earn:
  • 6% cash back per $1 spent on groceries on the first $1,000 each month
  • 4.5% cash back per $1 spent on recurring bills on the first $500 each month
  • 3.5% cash back per $1 spent on gas and EV charging on the first $1,000 each month
  • 1% cash back per $1 spent on everything else on the first $3,300 each month
If you have at least $10,000 in your Neo Everyday Account you’ll earn:
  • 7% cash back per $1 spent on groceries on the first $1,000 each month
  • 5% cash back per $1 spent on recurring bills on the first $500 each month
  • 4% cash back per $1 spent on gas and EV charging on the first $1,000 each month
  • 1% cash back per $1 spent on everything else on the first $3,300 each month

With the Neo World Elite® Mastercard you earn cash back instantly on your purchases. Once earned, your cash back is available immediately in your account. From there you can use the cash back to reduce the balance on your Neo World Elite® Mastercard, add the cash back to your Neo Everyday account, or redeem cash back for merchandise at the Neo Store.

The Neo World Elite® Mastercard offers elevated earning rates and includes standard World Elite Mastercard benefits, such as:
  • Discounts with Participating Partners: Special offers and savings with select merchants.
  • Mastercard Travel Rewards: Cash back offers when shopping and dining abroad.
  • Mastercard Travel Pass provided by DragonPass: Access to over 1,300 airport lounges worldwide (fees may apply).

The card offers the following insurance benefits: 
  • Travel Emergency Medical Insurance up to $1,000,000 per insurance person up to a maximum age of 60, and for for 14 days.
  • Trip Cancellation & Trip Interruption Insurance up to $1,000 per person per trip
  • Flight Delay Insurance up to $250 per day to a maximum of $500
  • Delayed Baggage and Lost/Damaged Baggage up to $1000 per person per trip for delayed, lost, or damaged bagged
  • Hotel and Motel Burglary up to $1,000
  • Auto Rental Collision/Loss Damage Insurance up to 48 consecutive days
  • Auto Rental Personal Effects Insurance up to $1,000 per person for personal effects that are damaged or lossed in a car rental
  • Auto Rental Accidental Death and Dismemberment Insurance up to $100,000
  • Purchase Protection Insurance up to $1,000 per item for 90 days from the purchase date
  • Extended Warranty Insurance doubling the original warranty to one additional year up to $1,000 per item

colum_block.col1_text:
The Neo World Mastercard® has a minimum personal annual income requirement of $80,000 or a minimum household income of $150,000. You'll also need to be a Canadian resident and of the age of majority in your province/territory of residence to be eligible. 

Follow the link below to the Neo Financial website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. Neo Financial is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the 'Apply Now' link for the most up to date information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the BMO VIPorter Mastercard®*, a mid-tier VIPorter co-branded credit card issued by BMO.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 40,000 VIPorter points

$420 first-year value

features.feature: Earn $1 in Qualifying Spend per $25 spent

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 10,000 VIPorter points upon spending $3,000 in the first 110 days
  • Plus, earn an additional 15,000 VIPorter points upon spending $6,000 in the first 180 days
  • Plus, earn an additional 15,000 VIPorter points upon spending $10,000 in the first 365 days
  • Then, earn 2x VIPorter points per dollar spent on Porter purchases
  • And, earn 1x VIPorter point per dollar spent on gas, transportation, dining, groceries, and hotels
  • Priority airport services & priority rebooking in the event of delays
  • Earn Qualifying Spend towards Avid Traveller status
  • Annual fee: $89 (rebated in the first year)

card_benefits.b_content:
Earn 10,000 VIPorter points upon spending $3,000 in the first 110 days
Earn 15,000 VIPorter points upon spending $6,000 in the first 180 days
Earn 15,000 VIPorter points upon spending $10,000 in the first 365 days
Total of up to 40,000 VIPorter points
$89 (rebated in the first year)

21.99% purchases
23.99% cash advances (21.99% for Quebec residents)
23.99% balance transfers (21.99% for Quebec residents)

2 VIPorter points per dollar spent on Porter purchases
1 VIPorter point per dollar spent on gas, transportation, dining, groceries, and hotels
0.5 VIPorter points per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases

Priority airport services
Priority rebooking in the event of delays
Spending counts towards Avid Traveller Qualifying Spend
Three months of complimentary Instacart+ membership
$5 Instacart credit each month, valid on orders of $35 or more
Moderate

card_content.c_content: The BMO VIPorter Mastercard®* is a great card for frequent flyers with Porter Airlines who don't qualify for the BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®*, with strong earning rates and great perks & benefits for Porter Airlines.

The BMO VIPorter Mastercard®* is currently offering a welcome bonus of up to 40,000 VIPorter points, which are awarded in three chunks as follows:
  • Earn 10,000 VIPorter points upon spending $3,000 in the first 110 days
  • Earn 15,000 VIPorter points upon spending $6,000 in the first 180 days
  • Earn 15,000 VIPorter points upon spending $10,000 in the first 365 days
In order to fulfill the full minimum spending requirement of $10,000 in the first 365 days, you'll need to spend an average of $834 per month. The final allotment of 15,000 VIPorter points is awarded in your second year as a cardholder, which means you'll need to pay the annual fee in the second year. This card comes with an annual fee of $89, which is rebated in the first year as part of this offer. Supplementary cardholders cost $35 apiece, and you'll enjoy an annual fee rebate for up to three authorized users as part of this offer.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

Here's a breakdown of what the welcome bonus for the BMO VIPorter Mastercard®* is worth. Keep in mind that this doesn't take into consideration the potential value of other card benefits, which we'll outline in detail below. We value VIPorter points at 1.5 cents per point, and using that value, we'd peg a value of $600 on the welcome bonus alone. Plus, assuming the card's baseline earning rate of 0.5 VIPorter points per dollar spent, you'd earn an additional 5,000 VIPorter points for meeting the minimum spending requirement. We'd value these points at an additional $75. Therefore, in the first two years as a cardholder, the minimum amount of net value you can expect to get from this card's welcome bonus is $586: $600 from the 40,000 points from the welcome bonus, $75 by spending the bare minimum to earn the welcome bonus (and assuming the card's baseline earning rate), and then minus the card's annual fee of $89 in the second year. Of course, you can certainly squeeze more value out of the card by leveraging the category earning rates and the card's other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value.

The BMO VIPorter World Elite®* Mastercard®* has a three-tiered earning structure, which allows you to earn VIPorter points on your daily spending that you can then redeem for travel with Porter Airlines and its partner airlines. The card's earning rates are broken down as follows:
  • Earn 2 VIPorter points per dollar spent on Porter purchases
  • Earn 1 VIPorter point per dollar spent on gas, transportation, dining, groceries, and hotels
  • Earn 0.5 VIPorter point per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases
It's important to note that there are spending caps in the elevated category earning rates, which are calculated based on the calendar year. The spending caps are as follows:
  • Earn 2 VIPorter points per dollar spent on up to $10,000 of Porter purchases (and earn 0.5 VIPorter points per dollar spent thereafter)
  • Earn 1 VIPorter point per dollar spent on up to $3,000 of gas & transportation purchases (and earn 0.5 VIPorter points per dollar spent thereafter)
  • Earn 1 VIPorter point per dollar spent on up to $5,000 of dining and grocery purchases (and earn 0.5 VIPorter points per dollar spent thereafter)
  • Earn 1 VIPorter point per dollar spent on up to $3,000 of hotel purchases (and earn 0.5 VIPorter points per dollar spent thereafter)
If you're someone who may tend to max out any of the elevated category earning rates each year, be sure to consider switching your purchases to a different card once you've hit the cap to ensure you're getting the best return on your spending.

You'll earn VIPorter points on the BMO VIPorter Mastercard®*, which can be redeemed for flights with Porter Airlines and its partners. The VIPorter program doesn't publish an award chart, which means that you might see different prices for the same flight, depending on the demand. Plus, you aren't able to redeem them against the taxes and fees for your booking, so be prepared to pay a modest amount on each flight, which usually doesn't exceed $100 or so. Porter Airlines has a growing network of flights across Canada and the United States, and it's likely that its route map will continue to grow as the airline expands its fleet. Here's an idea of some of the lowest-level prices you can expect to pay for one-way flights using VIPorter points:
  • Toronto–New York: 5,000 VIPorter points
  • Toronto–Edmonton: 8,000 VIPorter points
  • Toronto–Halifax: 7,000 VIPorter points
  • Toronto–Vancouver: 8,000 VIPorter points
  • Toronto–Miami: 7,000 VIPorter points
  • Toronto–Los Angeles: 7,000 VIPorter points
One of the best ways to squeeze extra value out of your VIPorter points is to use the Points + Cash option, which lets you pay more in cash and less in points on redemptions. In some cases, the value of your VIPorter points can exceed 5 or even 6 cents per point, which is four times our base valuation! If you're ready to make a redemption, be sure to compare all options at your disposal, and also check to see what the flight's corresponding cash value is to make sure you're happy with the value from your points.

The BMO VIPorter Mastercard®* comes with a handful of perks & benefits which are mostly specific to Porter Airlines. All BMO VIPorter Mastercard®* cardholders are granted VIPorter Passport Avid Traveller status, which grants cardholders the ability to earn 6 VIPorter points per dollar spent. Plus, in the event of flight delays, you'll get priority re-accommodation over other passengers, and you'll also have priority check-in, security, and early boarding. In the first year as a cardholder, you'll earn a 35% off voucher upon spending $6,000. Then, in subsequent years, you can earn one by spending $25,000 on your card each cardholder year. The voucher gives the cardholder and up to three guests travelling on the same Porter Airlines flight 35% off the base fare on a booking. If you fly with Porter Airlines often, your credit card spending will give your Avid Traveller status a boost, since you'll earn $1 in Qualifying Spend for every $25 spent on your card. Your VIPorter points will never expire as long as you're a cardholder.

The BMO VIPorter Mastercard®* also includes Instacart-related benefits, which add real, everyday value if you already use grocery delivery.

Primary cardholders are eligible for:

  • Three months of complimentary Instacart+ membership
  • $5 Instacart credit each month, valid on orders of $35 or more
  • Credits apply to non-alcohol purchases only and do not roll over month to month

The BMO VIPorter Mastercard®* comes with the following suite of insurance coverage:
  • Emergency medical insurance: Up to $5 million of coverage for the first 8 consecutive days of your out-of-province or out-of-country trip, for travellers aged 64 or under.
  • Flight delay insurance: Up to $500 total per trip (for all insured persons combined) if your flight is delayed 4+ hours, covering meals and accommodation.
  • Lost or stolen baggage insurance: Up to $500 per person, to a maximum of $1,000 total per trip for all insured persons, for baggage lost, stolen, or accidentally damaged while checked in or carried on a common carrier.
  • Baggage delay insurance: Up to $500 per person (max $1,000 per trip), if your baggage is delayed by 6+ hours during outbound travel, for the purchase of essential items.
  • Hotel burglary insurance: Up to $1,000 per occurrence for stolen or damaged personal property caused by burglary of your travel accommodation.
  • Travel accident insurance (Common Carrier): Up to $500,000 of Accidental Death and Dismemberment Insurance when travelling as a passenger on a licensed common carrier.
  • Car rental theft and damage insurance: Comprehensive coverage for rental cars with an MSRP of up to $65,000, for up to 48 consecutive days, including loss, theft, towing, and loss-of-use charges.
Additionally, the card includes Purchase Security Insurance and Extended Warranty Insurance:
  • Purchase Security covers eligible items against theft or damage for 90 days from the date of purchase.
  • Extended Warranty doubles the manufacturer’s warranty up to 1 additional year.
  • The combined lifetime maximum for these two benefits is $60,000 per account.

colum_block.col1_text: This credit card has no minimum personal annual income requirement. You'll also need to be a Canadian resident and of the age of majority in your province/territory of residence to be eligible.

acf.legal_disclaimers: * Terms and conditions apply. BMO is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the BMO CashBack® Business Mastercard®*, a no-fee cash back business credit card by BMO that earns modest rewards on daily spending.

acf.subtitle:
10% cash back

$150 first-year value

features.feature: 10% cash back on gas, office supplies, and recurring cell phone/internet bill payments for 3 months
1.75% cash back on purchases at Shell®†† gas stations

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 10% cash back on gas, office supplies, and recurring cell phone/internet bill payments for 3 months (up to $150 cash back)
  • Then, earn 1.75% cash back on eligible purchases at Shell® gas stations
  • Plus, earn 1.5% cash back on eligible gas, office supply purchases and cell phone/internet bill payments
  • Also get 0.75% cash back on all other purchases
  • Get up to 7 cents off per litre on all Shell®± fuel*
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content: 10% cash back on gas, office supplies, and recurring cell phone & internet bill payments in the first three months (up to $150 cash back)

20.99% purchase
23.99% cash advance
23.99% balance transfer

1.75% cash back on purchases at Shell® gas stations
1.5% cash back on eligible gas, office supply purchases, and cell phone/internet bill payments
0.75% cash back on all other purchases
Get up to 7 cents off per litre on all Shell®± fuel*
Basic

card_content.c_content: The BMO CashBack® Business Mastercard®* is a solid choice for a business owner who prefers cash back and spends a fair amount every month on gas, office supplies, and recurring cell/internet bill payments. Its 1.75% return on gas, office supplies, and recurring cell/internet bill payments is good for a no-fee card, and the flexibility of BMO's cash back redemption options also counts in its favour. [read-more ids="30114"]

As a welcome bonus, the BMO CashBack® Business Mastercard®* offers 10% cash back on the first $1,500 spent on gas, office supplies, and recurring cell phone & internet bill payments within the first three months, up to a total of $150 in cash back rewards. The card has no annual fee, making it a great choice to keep around as a free primary or backup business credit card, all while supporting the longevity of your credit history. It's also free to add authorized users. Get a 0.00% introductory interest rate on Balance Transfers for 9 months, 3% fee applies to balance amounts transferred*

Aside from the welcome offer, the BMO CashBack® Business Mastercard®* earns cash back at the following rates:
  • 1.75% cash back on on purchases at Shell® gas stations
  • 1.5% cash back on on eligible gas, office supply purchases, and cell phone/internet bill payments
  • 0.75% cash back on all other purchases
A 10% return on gas, office supplies, and recurring cell phone/internet bill payments for 3 months is a  competitive rate for a no-fee card. The 1.75% cash back on purchases at Shell® gas stations is also great if you fill gas at Shell®, to a maximum of $2,500 per account.  The 0.75% cash back rate for uncategorized purchases isn't the best baseline earning rate, but it isn’t too far off of the value of the rewards on BMO’s other no-fee cards.

There are three ways to redeem your cash back on the BMO CashBack® Business Mastercard®*:
  • Deposit into a BMO chequing or savings account
  • Deposit into a BMO InvestorLine account
  • Redeem as a credit against your credit card's statement
Any of these three options can be requested at any time when you've earned at least $1 of cash back. You can also set up automatic payments, which will automatically trigger one of the above three redemption options every time you reach $25 of cash back. BMO's cash back redemption options are relatively flexible compared to its peers, many of which only allow you to claim your cash back earnings once a year.

As a no-fee card, the BMO CashBack® Business Mastercard®* comes with relatively few perks. Cardholders can get up to 7 cents off per litre on all Shell®± fuel*. Eligible cardholders will receive 2 cents off per litre on regular fuel, and 7 cents off per litre on premium fuel, up to 1,000 litres per month. You'll enjoy an option to get up to 22 additional cards so that employees can easily make purchases on behalf of your business.

Insurance benefits on the BMO CashBack® Business Mastercard®* are also limited. The card does not offer any types of travel insurance; instead, its coverage is limited to extended warranty (doubling the manufacturer's standard warranty, up to one additional year) and purchase protection (against accidental damage or theft within 90 days).

colum_block.col1_text:
As a basic no-fee product, this credit card has no minimum income requirement.

Click on the "Apply Now" button to head to the BMO website and learn more.

acf.legal_disclaimers: *Terms and conditions apply. BMO is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

title.rendered: Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite Business* Card

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Scotiabank Passport Visa Infinite Business Card, a useful business credit card with no foreign transaction fees.

acf.subtitle:
40,000 Scene+ points

$176 first-year value

features.feature: Six free lounge visits per year

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 30,000 Scene+ points upon spending $5,000 in the first three months
  • Earn 10,000 Scene+ points upon spending $60,000 in the first 12 months
  • Plus, earn 1.5x Scene+ points on all purchases
  • Visa Airport Companion Program membership with six free lounge visits per year
  • No foreign transaction fees
  • Qualify on the basis of your business financials
  • $199 annual fee

card_benefits.b_title: Best offer available available publicly until October 31, 2025

card_benefits.b_content:
30,000 Scene+ points upon spending $5,000 in the first three months
10,000 Scene+ points upon spending $60,000 in the first 12 months
Total of up to 40,000 Scene+ points

19.99% purchases
22.99% cash advances
22.99% balance transfers
1.5 Scene+ points per dollar spent on all purchases

No foreign transaction fees
Six annual complimentary lounge visits
Strong

card_content.c_content: The Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite Business* Card is Scotiabank's premium travel credit card for small business owners. With an impressive 1.5% return on business spending and no foreign transaction fees (a unique benefit among business credit cards), this card is ideal for small business owners who are seeking a simplified and accessible rewards program in Scene+. [read-more ids="30129"]

The Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite Business* Card offers 30,000 Scene+ points after spending $5,000 in the first three months.

For the big spenders, there's also a bonus of 10,000 Scene+ points after spending $60,000 in the first year.

The card has an annual fee of $199 for the primary cardholder. Also, the first supplementary card is free, while subsequent supplementary cards are charged at $50 apiece.

The card offers a uniform earning rate of 1.5 Scene+ points per dollar spent on all purchases, which equates to a 1.5% return.

Among small business credit cards, a flat 1.5% earning rate is fantastic, and it's technically one of the strongest fixed returns in the market. Some other small business credit cards, like the American Express Business Platinum Card or the RBC Avion Visa Infinite Privilege Card, offer 1.25 points per dollar spent. That's weaker than the Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite Business* Card on paper, but it's worth noting that Amex and RBC's points can be transferred to airline partners and redeemed at a higher value, whereas Scene+ are a fixed-value points currency.

Scene+ is one of the most flexible fixed-value points programs in Canada. Points have a value of 1 cent per point, and can be easily credited towards any travel expense up to 12 months after the date of purchase.

This is far superior to fixed-value points programs from Canada's other major banks, as most of them force you to book your travel through their in-house travel agencies. With those programs, you’re effectively limited to using your points for flights, hotels, car rentals, and vacations at the rates set in the online travel agencies.

On the other hand, with Scene+ points, you can apply your points to things like vacation rentals, plane tickets, train tickets, hotel stays, award booking taxes and fees, and much more. Simply charge the travel expense to your credit card, and then redeem points to "offset" the charge.

The best part of this is that since you can book directly with travel vendors, you'll get the full entitlement to earning elite benefits, and you'll also be covered by the card's insurance coverage (outlined below).
Your Scene+ points won’t expire for as long as you’re a cardholder. If you cancel the card, you’ll have 60 days to redeem your Scene+ points before they’re gone.

The main draw of this card is the fact that it has no foreign transaction fees – indeed, it’s the first small business credit card in Canada to include such a benefit.

While most other credit cards charge a 2.5% markup on foreign exchange transactions, the Scotiabank Passport Visa Infinite Business Card uses the Visa mid-market rate. This is therefore an excellent card to use when you’re travelling abroad.

In terms of airport lounge benefits, the Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite Business* Card comes with a Visa Airport Companion Program membership with six complimentary lounge visits per year. You'll also get an Avis Preferred Plus membership, as well as access to dedicated Visa Infinite perks like a concierge service, dining experiences, and hotel stays.

The Scotiabank Passport® Visa Infinite Business* Card comes with fairly strong insurance benefits. Coverage includes:

  • Emergency medical insurance of up to $1 million for you, your spouse, and dependent children on unexpected medical illness or injury when travelling out-of-province for up to 25 days (10 days if aged 65 or older)

  • Trip cancellation and interruption insurance of up to $2,500 per person

  • Flight delay insurance for delays of more than four hours, up to $500 per person

  • Rental car collision or damage insurance, which can help you save on the insurance fees usually charged by car rental companies for vehicles up to a MSRP of $65,000

Plus, on the purchase protection side, you’ll get extended warranty of up to one more year, and a 90-day protection period against damage, loss, or theft for most new items.

colum_block.col1_text:
To be eligible for this card, you'll need one of the following: a minimum personal annual income of $60,000, a household annual income of $100,000, business sales revenue of $500,000, $250,000 in assets under management with Scotiabank, or a minimum annual spend of $30,000. You'll also likely need business registration documents, even if you apply as a sole proprietor, and be approved for minimum credit limit of $10,000 to be eligible.

Follow the below link to the Scotiabank website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. See Card Provider's website and Card Application for complete card details, terms and current offers. Reasonable efforts are made to maintain accuracy of information.

title.rendered: BMO® Ascend World Elite®* Business Mastercard®*

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the BMO® Ascend World Elite®* Business Mastercard®*, a small business credit card under the BMO Rewards points program.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 100,000 BMO Rewards points

$670 first-year value
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 20,000 BMO Rewards points when you make your first purchase
  • Then, earn up to 8,000 BMO Reward Points per month when you spend at least $3,000 per month within months 3–12
  • Earn 4 BMO Rewards points per dollar spent on gas, office supplies, and phone/internet bills
  • Get up to 7 cents off per litre on all Shell®± fuel*
  • Get five free virtual healthcare visits per year with Maple
  • DragonPass membership with two free visits per year
  • Qualify for the card with a registered business and minimum personal or business income
  • Annual fee: $0 for the first year, then $149

card_benefits.b_content:
20,000 BMO Rewards points when you make your first purchase
8,000 BMO Reward Points per month when you spend at least $3,000 per month within months 3 – 12
Total of 100,000 BMO Rewards points
$0 for the first year, then $149

18.99% on purchases
23.99% on cash advances and balance transfers

4 BMO Rewards points per dollar spent on gas, office supplies, and on recurring cell phone and internet payments
1.5 BMO Rewards points per dollar on all other purchases

Get up to 7 cents off per litre on all Shell®± fuel*
Virtual healthcare with Maple
DragonPass membership with 2 visits per year
Strong

card_content.c_content: The BMO® Ascend World Elite®* Business Mastercard®* is a good option for brick-and-mortar businesses who spend frequently on gas, office supplies, and phone and internet bills. Its welcome bonus is also fairly competitive, offering some unique benefits along with a first-year fee waiver.

With the current offer on the BMO® Ascend World Elite®* Business Mastercard®*, you can earn a up to 100,000 BMO Rewards points, broken down as follows:
  • Earn 20,000 BMO Rewards points when you make your first purchase
  • Earn 8,000 BMO Rewards points per month when you spend at least $3,000 per month within months 3–12 (total of up to 80,000 BMO Rewards points)
Based on our valuation of BMO Rewards points at 0.67 cents per point, the welcome offer alone is worth up to $670. The card has an annual fee of $149, which is waived for the first year as part of the current offer. The fee for adding authorized users is $50, and you can add up to 22 secondary cardholders on your account.

The BMO® Ascend World Elite®* Business Mastercard®* has a two-tier earning structure:
  • Earn 4 BMO Rewards points per dollar spent on gas, office supplies, and recurring internet/phone payments
  • Earn 1.5 BMO Rewards point per dollar spent on all other eligible purchases
These earning rates are equivalent to a 2.68% and 1.01% return, respectively, when you take into account our valuation of BMO Rewards points at 0.67 cents per point. The return on non-categorized spending is arguably a bit weak compared to business credit cards from other issuers; however, earning 4x points on gas, office supplies, and telecommunications bills can certainly build up the points quickly. Keep in mind that there's an annual spending cap of $50,000 for the 4x category; once you've hit this threshold, your earning rate on gas, office supplies, and recurring internet/phone payments will revert to the card's baseline earning rate of 1.5 BMO Rewards points per dollar spent. Additionally, you'll automatically be enrolled for offers on Mastercard® Easy Savings, which is good for 1–25% cash back rebates at select merchants, on top of your regular rewards.

BMO Rewards points are worth a maximum of 0.67 cents per point (cpp) when redeemed against the cost of travel booked with any travel vendor or for a deposit into your BMO investment account (at the rate of 15,000 points = $100). Fortunately, you don't have to book through the BMO Rewards travel portal to get the best-possible value. While that's also an option, we recommend booking directly with travel vendors whenever possible. You also have the option of redeeming points for gift cards, merchandise, or statement credits, albeit at a lower value. Generally speaking, if you're thinking about pursuing one of these redemptions, it'd be a better idea to open a BMO investment account and redeem the points there instead for greater value.

Cardholders will get five free virtual care visits per year with Maple, an online health platform. Maple’s Canadian-licensed doctors can diagnose and treat many conditions, including reproductive health, mental health, and other specialties. They can also write prescriptions and sick notes for you. You can connect on-demand 24/7 or with an appointment, via text, voice, or video. You'll also get a DragonPass membership, which comes with two free visits per year to a network of over 400 airport lounges. You can use visits for yourself or a guest. Additional visits will cost $32 (USD). In addition, cardholders can get up to 7 cents off per litre on all Shell®± fuel*. Eligible cardholders will receive 2 cents off per litre on regular fuel, and 7 cents off per litre on premium fuel, up to 1,000 litres per month.

The BMO Ascend World Elite®* Business Mastercard®* provides travel insurance to cardholders. There's the usual coverage for out-of-province and out-of-country medical, trip cancellation, trip interruption, flight delay, lost or stolen baggage, and more. As far as car rentals go, this card covers up to an MSRP of $65,000. This card will also double the manufacturer's warranty for purchases made with the card, up to one additional year.

colum_block.col1_text:
This card is available for any business, whether you run a sole proprietorship or a corporation. You may need to show various types of business registration documents and proof of income depending on the structure of your enterprise.

Click the "Apply Now" button to head to the BMO website and learn more.

acf.legal_disclaimers: † Terms and conditions apply. BMO is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

title.rendered: Cathay World Elite® Mastercard® – powered by Neo

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Cathay World Elite® Mastercard® – powered by Neo, the only card that directly earns Asia Miles in Canada.

acf.subtitle:
Up to 30,000 Asia Miles

$380 first-year value

features.feature: Earn Asia Miles on everyday purchases
Bonus Asia Miles with Neo's partners

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 15,000 Asia Miles upon card activation†
  • Plus, earn 15,000 Asia Miles upon spending $5,000 in the first three months†
  • Then, earn 4 Asia Miles per dollar spent on Cathay Pacific flights†
  • And, earn more Asia Miles at Neo's partners†
  • Enjoy a 15% discount on Cathay Pacific flights†
  • Minimum income: $80,000 (personal), $150,000 (household)
  • Annual fee: $180

card_benefits.b_content:
Earn 15,000 Asia Miles upon card activation<sup>†</sup>
Earn 15,000 Asia Miles upon spending $5,000 in the first three months<sup>†</sup>
Total of up to 30,000 Asia Miles<sup>†</sup>

19.99% purchases
22.99% cash advances
22.99% balance transfers

4 Asia Miles per dollar spent on Cathay Pacific flights†
2 Asia Miles per dollar spent in foreign currencies†
1 Asia Mile per dollar spent elsewhere†
Elevated earning rates with Neo partners†

15% discount on Cathay Pacific flights
Virtual card
Moderate

card_content.c_content:
The Cathay World Elite<sup>®</sup> Mastercard<sup>®</sup> – powered by Neo is the only credit card in Canada that directly earns Asia Miles.

If you're loyal to Cathay Pacific, this card will help pad your Asia Miles balance and can also help you score a discount on flights.

[read-more title="Read More" ids="171721"]

Presently, the Cathay World Elite® Mastercard® – powered by Neo offers up to 60,000 Asia Miles as its welcome bonus, structured as follows:
  • 15,000 Asia Miles upon activating the card†
  • 15,000 Asia Miles upon spending $5,000 in the first three months†
This is the highest offer seen to date on this card, making it an ideal time to apply. The card has an annual fee of $180, which isn't rebated or waived with the current offer.

How Much Is This Offer Worth?

Here’s a breakdown of what the welcome bonus for the Cathay World Elite® Mastercard® – powered by Neo is worth. Keep in mind that this doesn’t take into consideration the potential value of other card benefits, which we’ll outline in detail below. We value Asia Miles at 1.6 cents per point, and using that value, we’d peg a value of $480 on the welcome bonus alone. Plus, assuming the card’s baseline earning rate of 1 Asia Mile per dollar spent, you’d earn an additional 5,000 Asia Miles for meeting the minimum spending requirement. We’d value these points at an additional $80. Therefore, in the first year as a cardholder, the minimum amount of net value you can expect to get from this card’s welcome bonus is $380: $480 from the 30,000 Asia Miles from the full welcome bonus, $80 by spending the bare minimum to earn the welcome bonus (and assuming the card’s baseline earning rate), and then minus the card’s annual fee of $180 in the first year. Of course, you can certainly squeeze more value out of the card by leveraging the category earning rates and the card’s other benefits; however, this is unique to each cardholder, and our valuation assumes a minimum amount of value rather than a maximum amount of value.

In terms of the returns on everyday spending, the Cathay World Elite® Mastercard® – powered by Neo has the following earning rates:
  • 4 Asia Miles per dollar spent on Cathay Pacific flights†
  • 2 Asia Miles per dollar spent in foreign currencies†
  • 1 Asia Mile per dollar spent elsewhere†
Using our valuation of 1.6 cents per Asia Mile, you're looking at an effective return of 6.4% on Cathay Pacific purchases, 3.2% on eligible foreign currency purchases, and 1.6% on all other eligible purchases. Furthermore, you can benefit from elevated earning rates when you use your card at Neo's exclusive partners. However, the earning rates aren't publicly disclosed, they vary over time and locations, and you'll only see them once you're a cardholder. However, you can get an idea of the earning rates by creating a free Neo profile and then exploring partner offers through the app or in your browser. While the exact Asia Miles earning rates won't be listed, it should give you a rough idea of the businesses that offer elevated earning rates. [read-more title="Read More" ids="164368"] It's worth noting that this card levies foreign transaction fees, which means that you'll pay an additional 2.5% for transactions in currencies other than Canadian dollars. This eats into the value from the elevated earning rate of 2 Asia Miles per dollar spent, and you may want to consider a card with no foreign transaction fees instead.

Cathay Pacific Asia Miles is the loyalty program for Hong Kong-based Cathay Pacific. [read-more title="Read More" ids="17479"] Through the program, you can book flights with Cathay Pacific, as well as other oneworld airlines and other partner airlines. Booking flights with Asia Miles is subject to distance-based pricing, and you can expect to pay more as you fly longer distances and in higher classes of service. You can also expect to pay a modest amount of taxes and fees on award bookings. In Canada, Cathay Pacific flies to Vancouver and Toronto. To book a flight between Vancouver and Hong Kong, you can expect to find the following one-way prices:
  • Economy: 27,000 Asia Miles
  • Premium economy: 50,000 Asia Miles
  • Business class: 88,000 Asia Miles
Meanwhile, to book a flight from Toronto to Hong Kong, you can expect to find the following one-way prices:
  • Economy: 38,000 Asia Miles
  • Premium economy: 75,000 Asia Miles
  • Business class: 115,000 Asia Miles
Outside of these routes, here are some examples of what you can do once you've amassed a healthy sum of Asia Miles:
  • Fly Cathay Pacific First Class from Hong Kong to Beijing for 43,000 Asia Miles
  • Fly Cathay Pacific First Class from Hong Kong to Tokyo for 50,000 Asia Miles
  • Fly British Airways business class from North America to London for 63,000 Asia Miles
  • Fly American Airlines business class from the US to Australia for 89,000 Asia Miles
  • Fly Cathay Pacific First Class from Hong Kong to London or Los Angeles for 125,000 Asia Miles 
  • Fly Japan Airlines First Class from Tokyo to New York for 135,000 Asia Miles
In addition to these examples, Asia Miles are great for short-haul flights within Asia with Cathay Pacific and other oneworld airlines, such as Japan Airlines and Malaysia Airlines. [read-more title="Read More" ids="342"]

Until December 31, 2026, the Cathay World Elite® Mastercard® – powered by Neo offers cardholders a 15% discount on Cathay Pacific flights. Head to the offer's landing page to learn more, and to read the terms and conditions. Otherwise, the card doesn't offer any unique benefits for Cathay Pacific flights that aren't available to anyone who's an Asia Miles member. As a World Elite® Mastercard® product, you can enjoy paid lounge access ($32 USD per visit) through Mastercard Travel Pass, powered by DragonPass You'll also enjoy a virtual card, which you can begin using as soon as you've been approved.

When it comes to insurance, the Cathay World Elite® Mastercard® – powered by Neo has the same suite of insurance provided on other Neo products. Notably, the insurance doesn't apply for award tickets. Therefore, if you'd like to be covered for an Asia Miles booking, you'll want to use a credit card that provides insurance on award bookings instead. The insurance provided on the Cathay World Elite® Mastercard® – powered by Neo is as follows:
  • Emergency medical insurance: up to $1 million for trips of up to 14 days, for those aged 60 and younger.
  • Trip interruption: up to $1,000 per person, up to $5,000 per trip
  • Trip cancellation: up to $1,000 per person, up to $5,000 per trip
  • Flight delay: up to $500
  • Lost/delayed baggage: up to $1,000 for delays of six hours or longer
  • Auto rental collision/damage: up to 48 consecutive days, MSRP of up to $65,000
  • Hotel/motel burglary insurance: up to $1,000
  • Purchase protection: 90 days from date of purchase
  • Extended warranty: doubles the manufacturer's warranty, up to one additional year
As always, be sure to read the insurance certificates to understand if they apply in your unique situation, and contact the insurer with any questions about coverage.

colum_block.col1_text:
The Cathay World Elite® Mastercard® – powered by Neo has a minimum income requirement of $80,000 (personal) or $150,000 (household), to be eligible. If you don't meet these requirements, you can apply for a secured version of the card instead.

Click the "Apply Now" button below to learn more about the card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: † Terms and conditions apply. Please refer to Neo website for up-to-date offer details.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the RBC Avion Visa Infinite Business, a higher-end business credit card that’s great for frequent travellers.

acf.subtitle:
35,000 RBC Avion points

$700 first-year value

features.feature: Redeem points at 2cpp for premium flights

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 35,000 RBC Avion points when you get approved for the card
  • Plus, earn 1.25x RBC Avion points on all purchases
  • Use your rewards for any business class or First Class flight with no restrictions on dates, seats, or airlines
  • Transfer points to one of four airline partners
  • Qualify for the card with a registered business
  • Annual fee: $175

card_benefits.b_content: 35,000 Avion points upon approval

19.99% purchase
22.99% cash advance
22.99% balance transfer

19.99% purchases
22.99% cash advances
22.99% balance transfers
1.25 Avion points per dollar spent on all purchases
2cpp redemption for business class & First Class flights
Strong

card_content.c_content: The RBC Avion Visa Infinite Business Card is RBC's premium travel credit card geared towards small business owners. With a base 1.25x earning rate and a unique redemption pathway for premium flights, this card is a good fit for business owners who travel frequently, have high spending volumes, and who prefer the convenience of having a Visa product for their spending needs. [read-more ids="30109"]

The signup bonus on the RBC Avion Visa Infinite Business Card is 35,000 Avion points upon approval. The card has an annual fee of $175 for the primary cardholder. Supplementary cards are priced at $75 apiece.

The RBC Avion Visa Infinite Business Card offers a flat earning rate of 1.25 Avion points per dollar spent on all purchases. This earning rate applies to the first $75,000 spent per calendar year, and drops to 1 Avion point per dollar spent after that. This is one of the strongest base earning rates on any small business credit card in Canada, beaten only by the American Express Business Platinum Card (which earns 1.25x Membership Rewards points with no annual cap).

There are a variety of ways to redeem RBC Avion points for a high value. As a transferrable currency, Avion points can be converted into British Airways Avios and Cathay Pacific Asia Miles at a 1:1 ratio, WestJet Rewards at a 100:1 ratio, as well as American Airlines AAdvantage at a 1:0.7 ratio. Avios, Asia Miles, and AAdvantage will all provide a means to book business class and First Class flights on oneworld airlines, which will be by far the best value for your Avion points if you're interested in travelling internationally in style. Look out for 10–50% transfer bonuses to these partners too, which can boost the value of your points even further.

These can be some of highest value uses of RBC Avion points. If you signed up via the current 35,000 Avion points offer, for example, and transferred the points over to Avios during a 50% promotion, you'd get 52,500 Avios – good for a whopping number of short-haul flights, or enough to make significant progress towards a multi-carrier ticket.

You can also redeem via the Air Travel Redemption Schedule, which lets you use Avion points to book round-trip economy class tickets departing from Canada, up to a certain maximum ticket price. As a baseline, you can also apply Avion points to any travel purchase at a rate of 1 cent per point. [read-more title="Read More" ids="149296"] Furthermore, a benefit unique to the RBC Avion Visa Infinite Business Card and the RBC Avion Visa Infinite Privilege Card is the ability to apply your points directly to a business class or First Class flight at a fixed rate of 2 cents per point. Your Avion points never expire for as long as you're a cardholder. If you cancel or switch to a different RBC product, you have 90 days to redeem your Avion points before they go away.

The RBC Avion Visa Infinite Business Card comes with a DragonPass lounge membership, but does not offer any complimentary lounge visits. Each visit to an airport lounge would be charged $32 (USD) apiece. The card comes with access to the Visa Infinite Business Concierge, which can help you do things like book restaurant reservations, select gifts or obtain event tickets right when they go on sale. You'll also have access to a series of dedicated Visa Infinite perks for dining and hotel stays. Finally, all RBC cardholders can save 3¢/L on fuel at Petro-Canada, and will earn 20% more Petro-Points and Avion points upon linking their card to their Petro-Points account.

As RBC's high-end business travel credit card, the RBC Avion Visa Infinite Business Card offers an impressive set of insurance provisions. The card offers 15 days of emergency medical travel insurance for travellers aged under 65, and three days of coverage for travellers aged 65+. There is no maximum coverage amount. Moreover, there's coverage for car rental loss or damage for vehicles up to a MSRP of $65,000 There's also trip cancellation and trip interruption insurance of up to $1,500 and $5,000 per person, respectively, along with $500,000 of common carrier accident insurance. While flying, cardholders are covered for up to $500 in flight delay insurance, up to $2,500 for delayed baggage, and up to $1,000 for lost or stolen baggage. (The travel insurance items will take effect as long as the full amount of the trip is charged to your card, booked directly using your RBC Avion points, or some combination of the two.) And when making purchases, the card offers extended warranty of up to one additional year, purchase protection that insures you against damage or theft of an item for up to 90 days after your purchase, and dedicated mobile device insurance with two years of protection of up to $2,000.

colum_block.col1_text:
Any small business owner can qualify for this card. RBC doesn't have a minimum income requirement, or a minimum revenue requirement, although they may require a business license.

You can follow the below link to the RBC website to learn more about this card, although you'll need to submit your application in-branch or over the phone.

title.rendered: Air France KLM World Elite Mastercard®

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Air France KLM World Elite Mastercard®, with which you can earn Flying Blue miles on your daily spending.

acf.subtitle: Earn 60 XP
acf.reward_points: Earn Flying Blue miles on daily spending

features.feature: Earn Flying Blue miles on daily spending

acf.featured_info:
• Get 60 XP in the first year, plus 30 XP in subsequent years
  • Earn 5 Flying Blue miles per euro spent on Air France KLM flights
  • Earn 2 Flying Blue miles per dollar spent on food and drink
  • Earn 1 Flying Blue mile per dollar spent everywhere else
  • Minimum income: $80,000 personal or $150,000 household
  • Annual fee: $132 (waived in the first year)

card_benefits.b_content: 60 XP
$132 (waived in the first year)

19.99% purchases
21.50% cash advances
19.99% balance transfers

Up to 30 Flying Blue miles per dollar spent at select merchants
5 extra Flying Blue miles per euro spent on Air France KLM flights
2 Flying Blue miles per dollar spent at restaurants and bars
1 Flying Blue mile per dollar spent on other purchases

30 XP yearly
Receive a virtual card instantly upon approval
DragonPass membership
Strong

card_content.c_content: The Air France KLM World Elite Mastercard is the sole Air France KLM co-branded credit card available in Canada. This card is great for anyone who's loyal to the Air France KLM Flying Blue loyalty program, with an unmatched return on Air France and KLM flights.

As the welcome bonus, you'll also get 60 XP points in the first year, which fast-track your way to earning Flying Blue Elite status. Then, you'll earn 30 XP points as an ongoing annual benefit to help boost your status in subsequent years. As a reminder, XP points are what Flying Blue uses to determine your status with the program. The card's annual fee is $132, and additional cards cost $50 apiece. The first-year annual fee is waived as part of the current offer.

The Air France KLM World Elite Mastercard offers a tiered rewards structure, with earning rates as follows:
  • Up to 30 Flying Blue miles per dollar spent at select Brim Financial merchant partners
  • 5 bonus Flying Blue miles per euro spent on Air France/KLM flights
  • 2 Flying Blue miles per dollar spent at restaurants and bars
  • 1 Flying Blue mile per dollar spent thereafter
The Brim Financial merchant partners range from large brands to small local businesses. You can check which vendors are eligible for bonus Flying Blue miles in the Brim app, and you can read more about Brim's individual partnerships program on their website. Be sure to keep an eye out for Milestone Bonuses from specific vendors in the Brim Marketplace. If you tend to purchase revenue flights with Air France or KLM, this card offers an unmatched earning rate, which is only elevated further with Flying Blue Elite status. As for purchases at restaurants and bars, the earning rate is competitive with other airline co-branded credit cards in Canada.

You can redeem Flying Blue Miles for flights with Air France, KLM, and other SkyTeam airlines. It's easy to redeem Flying Blue miles for flights in economy, premium economy, or business class. Flying Blue is a great program to use for flights between North America and Europe, due to its wide coverage on both continents. Be sure to keep an eye out for the monthly Promo Rewards, which offer reduced costs for redemptions on specific routes, sometimes including business class.

As soon as you have been approved for the card, you can generate a virtual credit card, which you can then begin using immediately. As a World Elite Mastercard, you'll automatically get a complimentary DragonPass membership (although, this comes without any free visits included). You'd also get 30 XP yearly upon renewal to help you progress towards Flying Blue status; however, it's important to note that you'll still need to do a good amount of flying to reach the lowest tier of Silver going forward, which requires 100 XP. Unfortunately, unlike other Brim credit cards, this card has a 2.5% foreign exchange fee.

As a World Elite Mastercard, this Air France KLM card offers comprehensive insurance, with a few unique inclusions from its partner Brim Financial. Here are a few features that make the Air France KLM World Elite Mastercard stand out for its insurance coverage:
  • Car rental insurance up to a MSRP of $85,000
  • Generous coverage for trip cancellation or trip interruption of up to $2,000 and $5,000 respectively
  • Flight delay and baggage delay insurance of up to $500 and $1,000 respectively
Notably, Brim offers event ticket protector insurance, a unique policy that's not seen on any other credit cards. If you can't attend an event due to one of the covered reasons (such as emergency medical or common carrier accident), you'll be reimbursed for the cost of your event tickets up to $1,000. You'll also get mobile device protection for loss, theft, or damage of a new phone or tablet for up to $1,500 for two years. The card also includes purchase security insurance for items charged to your card anywhere in the world, if they get damaged or stolen within 90 days from the purchase date. In addition, you'd also get extended warranty doubling the manufacturer's warranty of up to one year.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite product, this credit card has a minimum personal annual income requirement of $80,000 or a minimum household income of $150,000 to be eligible.

You can follow the link below to the Brim website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. Brim Financial is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the Apply Now link for the most up to date information.

acf.featured_info:
• Earn up to 35,000 Avion points upon approval†
  • Then, earn 20,000 Avion points upon spending $5,000 in the first six months†
  • Then, earn 15,000 Avion points as a one-time anniversary bonus upon renewal†
  • Plus, earn 1.25x RBC Avion points† on all qualifying purchases
  • Use your rewards for any business class flight with no restrictions on dates, seats, or airlines†
  • Transfer points to one of four airline partners†
  • DragonPass membership with six free lounge visits per year†

35,000 Avion Points upon approval†
20,000 bonus Avion points when you spend $5,000 in your first 6 months†
15,000 one-time anniversary bonus Avion points after 12 months of account opening†
Total of up to 70,000 Avion points†

20.99% purchases

1.25 RBC Avion points† per dollar spent on all qualifying purchases

Six annual complimentary visits to Visa Airport Companion program lounges†

2cpp redemption for business class & First Class flights†
Very strong

card_content.c_content: The RBC® Avion Visa Infinite Privilege† is RBC®'s flagship premium travel credit card, geared towards high-income earners and frequent travellers who are interested in maximizing RBC Avion points. Offering a uniform 1.25x earning rate and a unique redemption pathway for premium flights, this card is a great fit for high-spenders who prefer to keep a single credit card in their wallet while being able to redeem points for higher value in a variety of ways. [read-more ids="30109"]

The current welcome bonus on the RBC® Avion Visa Infinite Privilege† card is up to 70,000 RBC Avion points,† structured as follows:
  • Earn 35,000 Avion Points upon approval†
  • an additional 20,000 bonus Avion points when you spend $5,000 in your first six months†
  • Lastly 15,000 one-time anniversary bonus Avion points after 12 months of account opening†
The card has an annual fee of $399 for the primary cardholder†, with supplementary cards priced at $99 apiece†.

The RBC® Avion Visa Infinite Privilege† Card offers a flat earning rate of 1.25 RBC Avion points per dollar spent on all purchases†. This is one of the strongest uniform earning rates on any credit card in Canada, matched only by the American Express Business Platinum Card (which earns 1.25x Membership Rewards points) and RBC's premium business card, the RBC® Avion Visa Infinite† Business Card (which earns 1.25x Avion points as well).

There are a variety of ways to redeem RBC Avion points for a high value. As a transferable currency, RBC Avion points can be converted into British Airways Executive Club† and Cathay Pacific Asia Miles† at a 1:1 ratio, WestJet Rewards† at a 100:1 ratio, as well as American Airlines AAdvantage† at a 1:0.7 ratio. Avios, Asia Miles, and AAdvantage will all provide a means to book business class and First Class flights on Oneworld airlines, which will be by far the best value for your Avion points if you're interested in travelling internationally in style. Look out for 10–50% transfer bonuses to these partners too, which can boost the value of your points even further.

These can be some of highest value uses of RBC Avion points. If you signed up via the current offer for 70,000 Avion points, for example, and transferred the points over to Avios during a 30% promotion, you'd get 91,000 Avios — good for a number of short-haul flights, a one-way flight in Qatar Airways Qsuites from North America to Doha, or enough to make solid progress towards a multi-carrier ticket.

You can also redeem via the Air Travel Redemption Schedule†, which lets you use Avion points to book round-trip economy class tickets departing from Canada, up to a certain maximum ticket price. As a baseline, you can also apply Avion points to any travel purchase at a rate of 1 cent per point†. With this card, you can also redeem your Avion points for business class or First Class flights at a rate of 2 cents per point. If you can find a great deal on premium flights, this can make for a very valuable redemption! Your RBC Avion points never expire for as long as you're a cardholder†. If you cancel or switch to a different RBC® product, you have 90 days to redeem your Avion points before they go away†. [read-more ids="17474"]

As with all Visa Infinite Privilege† products, this card offers a DragonPass lounge membership with six complimentary lounge visits per year†. Cardholders also enjoy priority airport services† (including fast-track security) at select Canadian airports: Ottawa, Montreal, Vancouver, and Toronto Billy Bishop. The card comes with access to the Visa Infinite Privilege Concierge†, which can help you do things like book restaurant reservations, select gifts or obtain event tickets right when they go on sale. You'll also have access to a series of dedicated Visa Infinite Privilege† perks for dining, wine tasting, golf, and hotel stays. Another great perk for cardholders is the flexibility to book flights last-minute: up to 24hrs before departure.† All RBC cardholders can save 3¢/L on fuel at Petro-Canada†, and will earn 20% more Petro-Points and RBC Avion points upon linking their card to their Petro-Points account†. And lastly cardholders get a 12-month complimentary DashPass subscription± which is a value of almost $120. This allows eligible cardholders unlimited deliveries with $0 delivery fees on orders of $15 or more, when you pay with your eligible RBC credit card.

As RBC's highest-end travel rewards credit card, the RBC® Avion Visa Infinite Privilege† Card offers an industry-leading set of insurance provisions. The card offers a very impressive 31 days of emergency medical travel insurance† for travellers aged under 65, and seven days of coverage for travellers aged 65+. There is no maximum coverage amount. Moreover, there’s coverage for car rental loss or damage for vehicles up to a MSRP of $85,000 There's also trip cancellation† and trip interruption insurance† of up to $2,500 and $5,000 per person, respectively, along with $500,000 of common carrier accident insurance†. While flying, cardholders are covered for up to $1,000 in flight delay insurance†, up to $3,000 for delayed baggage†, and up to $2,500 for lost or stolen baggage†. (The travel insurance items will take effect as long as the full amount of the trip is charged to your card, booked directly using your RBC Avion points, or some combination of the two.) And when making purchases, the card offers extended warranty† of up to two additional years, purchase protection† that insures you against damage or theft of an item for up to 120 days after your purchase, and dedicated mobile device insurance† with two years of protection of up to $1,500.

colum_block.col1_text: As a Visa Infinite Privilege product, the RBC® Avion Visa Infinite Privilege† has a minimum personal or household annual income requirement of $200,000 to be eligible.

acf.legal_disclaimers: †Terms and conditions apply. RBC is not responsible for maintaining the content on this site. Please click on the 'Apply Now' link for the most up to date information.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Brim World Elite Mastercard, the top-tier lifestyle rewards credit card issued by Brim.

acf.subtitle: Earn Brim Rewards
acf.reward_points: 1 Brim Rewards point per dollar spent

features.feature: Mobile device protection & event ticket insurance

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 1 Brim Rewards point per dollar spent
  • 1.5% foreign transaction fee
  • Minimum income: $80,000 personal or $150,000 household
  • Annual fee: $89

card_benefits.b_content: This card does not offer a welcome bonus.

Up to 30 points per dollar spent at select Brim Partners
1 point per dollar spent on other purchases

1.5% foreign transaction fee
DragonPass membership
Strong

card_content.c_content: The Brim World Elite Mastercard offers modest rewards and features lower-than-standard foreign transaction fees.

There is currently no welcome bonus available on this card.

The Brim World Elite Mastercard has an annual fee of $89, while additional cards cost $50 apiece.

This card offers a tiered rewards structure:
  • Up to 30 points per dollar spent at select Brim partners
  • 1 point per dollar spent on all other purchases
Brim's partners range from large brands to small local businesses. You can check which vendors are eligible for bonus rewards in the Brim app. You can read more about Brim's individual partnerships program on their website. If you shop a lot at partner stores, you can take advantage of special offers in the Brim app. In particular, look for Milestone Bonuses from specific vendors in the Brim Marketplace. Otherwise, the card's baseline earning rate isn't particularly notable when compared to other cash back cards on the Canadian market.

Brim Rewards can be redeemed at a rate of 1 cent per point at any time as a statement credit. Given how easy it is to redeem points, it's safe to say they're equivalent to cash back.

Redeem your points by clicking the “Redeem” button next to your transactions in the Brim Portal in the app.

This card charges a 1.5% foreign transaction fee. Previously, it had no foreign transaction fees as one of its mainstay features, which was one of the primary reasons to get the card. As a World Elite Mastercard, you'll automatically get a DragonPass membership (although without any free visits included).

As the premium card in the Brim line-up, this card offers comprehensive insurance a cut above the other Brim cards. Here are a few features that make the Brim World Elite Mastercard stand out:
  • Car rental insurance up to $85,000 MSRP
  • Generous coverage for trip cancellation or trip interruption
  • Hotel burglary insurance
Notably, Brim offers event ticket protector insurance, a unique policy that's not seen on any other credit cards. If you can't attend an event due to one of the covered reasons (such as emergency medical or common carrier accident), you'll be reimbursed for the cost of your event tickets up to $1,000. You'll also get mobile device protection for loss, theft, or damage. That type of coverage is still quite uncommon, although it's slowly showing up on more credit cards.

colum_block.col1_text:
The Brim World Elite Mastercard has a minimum income requirement of $80,000 (personal) or $150,000 (household). As long as you meet the income requirement, it should be fairly easy to get approved.

You can follow the link below to the Brim website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Brim World Mastercard, the mid-tier lifestyle rewards credit card issued by Brim.

acf.subtitle: Earn Brim Rewards points

features.feature: Mobile device protection & event ticket insurance

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 1 Brim Rewards point per $2 spent
  • 1.5% foreign transaction fees
  • Annual fee: $99

card_benefits.b_content: There is currently no welcome bonus offered on this product

Up to 30 points per dollar spent on select Brim Partners
1 Brim Rewards point per $2 spent on other purchases

1.5% foreign transaction fee
DragonPass membership
Free Boingo Wi-Fi
Strong

card_content.c_content: The Brim World Mastercard has unique insurance policies; however, as a mid-tier card, it's not always as obvious whether the benefits are worth the cost, compared to what you'd get with other credit cards.

This card currently comes without a welcome bonus. The annual fee of $99 is not waived for the first year. In fact, if you can qualify on the basis of your income, you'd be better off applying for the superior Brim World Elite Mastercard, which has an annual fee of $89 and better insurance. You can add authorized users at no additional cost.

This card offers a tiered rewards structure:
  • Up to 30 Brim Rewards points per dollar spent on select Brim partners
  • 1 Brim Rewards point per $2 spent elsewhere
Brim's partners range from large brands to small local businesses. You can check which vendors are eligible for bonus rewards in the Brim app. You can read more about Brim's individual partnerships program on their website. If you shop a lot at partner stores, you can take advantage of special offers in the Brim app. In particular, look for Milestone Bonuses from specific vendors in the Brim Marketplace. Otherwise, this card doesn't have a competitive earning rate, which is in fact the same on the Brim Mastercard, which comes with no annual fee.

Brim Rewards can be redeemed at a rate of 1 cent per point at any time as a statement credit. Given how easy it is to redeem points, it's safe to say they're equivalent to cash back.

Redeem your points by clicking the “Redeem” button next to your transactions in the Brim Portal in the app.

This card comes with a 1.5% foreign transaction fee, which is less than the standard 2.5%. However, since other cards don't levy foreign transaction fees, you're better off applying for one of them instead, especially since this card doesn't have particularly strong earning rates or other features. Also, as a World Mastercard, you'll automatically get a DragonPass membership (although without any free visits included), as well as Boingo Wi-Fi.

As the mid-tier card in the Brim line-up, this card offers a solid travel insurance package, but with few standout features. Instead, Brim offers event ticket protector insurance, a unique policy that's not seen on any other credit cards. If you can't attend an event due to one of the covered reasons (such as emergency medical or common carrier accident), you'll be reimbursed for the cost of your event tickets up to $1,000. You'll also get mobile device protection for loss, theft, or damage. That type of coverage is still quite uncommon, although it's slowly showing up on more credit cards.

colum_block.col1_text:
The Brim World Mastercard has a minimum income requirement of $60,000 (personal) or $100,000 (household). As long as you meet the income requirement, it should be fairly easy to get approved.

You can follow the link below to the Brim website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Brim Mastercard, the no-fee lifestyle rewards credit card issued by Brim.

acf.subtitle: No annual fee

features.feature: Mobile device protection & event ticket insurance

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 1 Brim Rewards point per $2 spent
  • 1.5% foreign transaction fees
  • Minimum annual income: $15,000 personal
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content: This card does not currently offer a welcome bonus.

Up to 30 points per dollar spent at select Brim Partners
1 point per $2 spent on other purchases

1.5% foreign transaction fee
Free Boingo Wi-Fi
Average

card_content.c_content: The Brim Mastercard has clearly curated its target audience to be millennials with a trendy smartphone app and partner-based rewards, but it can still appeal to other demographics. Its unique features help it stand out in a competitive marketplace for cards with no annual fee.

There is currently no welcome bonus available on the card.

The card has no annual fee, making it a good long-term keeper for anyone.

This card offers a tiered rewards structure:
  • Up to 30 points per dollar spent at select Brim partners
  • 1 point per $2 spent on all other purchases
Brim's partners range from large brands to small local businesses. You can check which vendors are eligible for bonus rewards in the Brim app. You can read more about Brim's individual partnerships program on their website. If you shop a lot at partner stores, you can take advantage of special offers in the Brim app. In particular, look for Milestone Bonuses from specific vendors in the Brim Marketplace. Also, you're eligible for the same partner bonuses that you'd get with the higher-tier Brim cards. If you shop a lot at those stores, you don't need to shell out a large annual fee, or have a high income, to benefit. Even if you don't shop much at Brim's partners, you'll still earn rewards at other stores.

Brim Rewards can be redeemed at a rate of 1 cent per point at any time as a statement credit. Given how easy it is to redeem points, it's safe to say they're equivalent to cash back.

Redeem your points by clicking the “Redeem” button next to your transactions in the Brim Portal in the app.

This card charges a 1.5% fee for foreign transactions. While that's lower than the standard 2.5%, the Brim Mastercard used to offer no foreign transaction fees, which was one of the main reasons to get the card. You'll get a Boingo Wi-Fi membership as a cardholder, a nice perk that's usually only seen on higher-tier Mastercards.

As the entry level card in the Brim line-up, this card does not offer travel insurance, only limited common carrier accident coverage and the typical extended warranty you'll see on most credit cards. Instead, Brim offers event ticket protector insurance, a unique policy that's not seen on any other credit cards. If you can't attend an event due to one of the covered reasons (such as emergency medical or common carrier accident), you'll be reimbursed for the cost of your event tickets up to $1,000. You'll also get mobile device protection for loss, theft, or damage. Both of these policies are quite a treat to find on a no-fee card.

colum_block.col1_text:
The Brim Mastercard has a minimum personal income requirement of $15,000. As long as you meet the income requirement, it should be fairly easy to get approved.

You can follow the link below to the Brim website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Home Trust Preferred Visa Card, a basic no-fee credit card issued by Home Trust with no foreign transaction fees.

acf.subtitle: 1% cash back
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 1% cash back on all transactions in Canadian dollars
  • Plus, save money abroad with no foreign transaction fees
  • Minimum annual income: $15,000 personal
  • No annual fee

card_benefits.b_content: 1% cash back on all purchases in Canadian dollars
No foreign transaction fees
Poor

card_content.c_content: The Home Trust Preferred Visa Card is a basic choice for a Visa card with no annual fees and no foreign transaction fees. It would suit frequent travellers who prefer a simple wallet, keeping the same card year after year, and acceptance at a wide range of merchants globally.
The card has no welcome bonus, and no annual fee.

The card earns 1% cash back on all purchases in Canadian dollars, with no cap on earning. There is no foreign transaction fee levied for purchases made in other currencies; however, you won't earn cash back on purchases in currencies other than Canadian dollars.
Cash back rewards are accumulated from your January statement until your December statement, and they are automatically issued as a statement credited sometime in January of the following year.
The card has no significant perks or benefits, aside from no foreign transaction fees.
The Home Trust Preferred Visa Card does not offer any insurance, with the exception of basic purchase protection.

colum_block.col1_text:
You'll need a minimum personal income of $15,000 to apply. Even if you have a weaker credit score, you're likely to be approved.

Follow the below link to the Home Trust website to learn more about this card and submit an application.
