title.rendered: Canadian Tire Triangle World Elite Mastercard

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Canadian Tire Triangle World Elite Mastercard, a useful no-fee credit card issued by Canadian Tire Financial.

acf.subtitle: Earn cash back rewards
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 4% cash back at Canadian Tire, Sport Chek, Marks, and more
  • Plus, earn 3% cash back at grocery stores
  • And, earn up to 7 cents per litre in Canadian Tire money on eligible fuel purchases
  • Free Canadian Tire Roadside Assistance Gold Plan
  • No fee, no interest purchase financing at participating retailers
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content: This card does not currently have a welcome bonus

4% cash back at Canadian Tire and affiliated stores
3% cash back on groceries
1% cash back on all other purchases

Canadian Tire Roadside Assistance Gold Plan
Make bill payments from the credit card
Zero-interest financing at Canadian Tire stores
Basic

card_content.c_content: The Canadian Tire Triangle World Elite Mastercard has a collection of unique features. With no annual fee, it may appeal to a variety of people as a long-term keeper card. This card may be of interest to outdoor enthusiasts, road trippers, commuters, or anyone with bill payments to payees that don't normally accept credit cards. [read-more ids="30103"]
Currently, this card does not offer a welcome bonus. The card has no annual fee as an ongoing feature.

The card earns Canadian Tire Money, also known as Triangle Rewards:
  • Earn 4% back at Canadian Tire brands
  • Earn 3% back on groceries, up to $12,000 spent per calendar year
  • Earn 1% back on all other purchases
Canadian Tire brands include a variety of retailers, with an emphasis on sports and outdoors. Here are a few of their major partners:
  • Canadian Tire
  • Mark's
  • L’Équipeur
  • Party City
  • Sport Chek
  • Atmosphere
  • Pro Hockey Life
  • Hockey Experts
  • Sports Rousseau
  • L'Entrepôt du Hockey
  • participating Sports Experts stores
  • SportChek
Plus, you'll collect 7 cents per litre in Canadian Tire Money on premium fuel, as well as 5 cents per litre in Canadian Tire Money on all other fuel types at Gas+ and Essence+ locations.

Canadian Tire Money can be redeemed for purchases at Canadian Tire brands, including the stores mentioned above. If you shop at those stores, your rewards are effectively the same as cash back. Rewards expire after 18 months of inactivity, but you don't have to remain a cardholder to keep your account active. If you ever decide to close the credit card, you can still earn Canadian Tire Money by scanning your Triangle membership card when you make a purchase at a partner store. You can learn more about the Triangle Rewards program on the Canadian Tire website.

Canadian Tire's credit cards have a unique feature where you can make bill payments from your credit card to select payees. These payments can be set up by finding the biller in the Canadian Tire online banking login, rather than by providing the biller with your credit card number. This may allow you to pay bills with a credit card for certain billers that don't accept regular credit card payments. The Triangle World Elite Mastercard comes with a complimentary Roadside Assistance Gold Plan membership. It includes 24-hour assistance, towing to the destination of your choice within 250 kilometres in Canada and the US, and services such as fuel delivery, flat tire changes, and jumpstarting your battery. This is superior to the roadside assistance benefits on other cards, which often stipulate that they'll only tow you to the nearest service centre, not to your preferred location. Finally, the card offers zero-interest financing on purchases at Canadian Tire brands.
The card has no notable travel insurance coverage, just standard coverage for rental car damage, extended warranty, and purchase protection.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $80,000 (individual) or $150,000 (household).

Consider supporting Prince of Travel by applying for the card via our affiliate link below.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Scotia Momentum® No-Fee Visa* Card, one of the best credit cards for balance transfers in Canada.

acf.subtitle: 0% promotional interest rate for balance transfers<sup>†</sup>
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 5% cash back on up to $2,000 in purchases in the first three months†
  • 0% interest rate for six months on balance transfers†
  • 2% balance transfer fee†
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_title: Best offer is available until April 30, 2026<sup>†</sup>

card_benefits.b_content:
Earn 5% cash back on up to $2,000 in purchases in the first three months<sup>†</sup>
0% interest rate on balance transfers in the first six months<sup>†</sup>

20.99% purchases<sup>†</sup>
22.99% cash advances<sup>†</sup>

1% cash back on all eligible gas stations, grocery stores, drug stores purchases, and recurring payments<sup>†</sup>
0.5% cash back on all other eligible purchases<sup>†</sup>
Basic

card_content.c_content: The Scotia Momentum® No-Fee Visa* Card is a great option for balance transfers.<sup>†</sup>

If you're approved for the Scotia Momentum® No-Fee Visa* Card by April 30, 2026, you can enjoy a promotional 0% annual interest rate<sup>†</sup> on balance transfers made in the first six months,<sup>†</sup> with a 2% balance transfer fee.<sup>†</sup>

If you're not using this card for balance transfers, you can also earn 5% cash back on up to $2,000 in eligible purchases<sup>†</sup> in the first three months,<sup>†</sup> which is worth up to $100.<sup>†</sup>

This card has no annual fee.

With the Scotia Momentum® No-Fee Visa* Card, you'll earn the following cash back rates:
  • Earn 1% cash back on all eligible gas stations, EV charging, grocery stores, drug stores purchases, and recurring payments†
  • Earn 0.5% cash back on all other eligible purchases†

Cash back can be redeemed to your account at any time, as long as you’ve accumulated at least $25 and your account is open and in good standing.

The welcome bonus cash back is awarded separately from your regular rewards. Your regular cash back posts as usual on each statement, while the bonus portion becomes available for redemption approximately four months after your account open date.

For example, if you buy groceries during the welcome bonus period, you’d earn 1% regular cash back that can be redeemed once you reach the $25 threshold, plus 4% bonus cash back that becomes available for redemption about four months after you open the account, for a total welcome bonus rate of 5%.

The primary benefit of this card is its promotional balance transfer rate. You can also save up to 25% off base rates at participating AVIS locations and at participating Budget locations in Canada and the U.S. when you pay with your Scotia Momentum No-Fee Visa card.†

This card does not come with included insurance coverage; however, you can opt-in to the following insurance bundles:†
  • Basic Protection: Life Benefit, Critical Illness Benefit, Hospitalization Benefit†
  • Comprehensive Protection: Life Benefit, Critical Illness Benefit, Hospitalization Benefit, Disability Benefit, Job Loss Benefit, Strike or Lockout Benefit†

colum_block.col1_text:
You must have a $12,000 minimum annual personal income, be of the age of majority and a Canadian resident, and not have declared bankruptcy in the past seven years to apply.

Follow the link below to the Scotiabank® website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

acf.legal_disclaimers: † Terms and conditions apply.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Laurentian Bank Visa Infinite Card, the flagship travel rewards credit card issued by Laurentian Bank.

acf.subtitle: Earn cash back on everyday spending
acf.reward_points: Earn 2% cash back on travel, groceries, and recurring bills
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 2 points per dollar spent (equivalent to 2% cash back) on gas, groceries, travel, transportation, and recurring bills
  • Earn 1 point per dollar spent (equivalent to 1% cash back) on everything else
  • Minimum income: $60,000 personal or $100,000 household
  • Annual fee: $130
acf.membership_reward: Earn cash back on everyday spending

card_benefits.b_content: There is currently no welcome bonus.

2 points per dollar spent (equivalent to 2% cash back) on gas, groceries, travel, transportation, and recurring bills
1 point per dollar spent (equivalent to 1% cash back) on everything else

Virtual card
Visa Infinite Concierge Service
Moderate

card_content.c_content:
The Laurentian Bank Visa Infinite Card recently received a makeover, in partnership with Brim Financial.

With flexible points that can be redeemed as a general statement credit or against purchases, this refreshed card is definitely worth a closer look.

The Laurentian Bank Visa Infinite Card currently does not come with a welcome bonus. The card has an annual fee of $130. There is a $50 fee for each authorized user added to your account, but you'll receive a rebate on two additional users in the first year.

The card's standard earning rate is as follows:
  • Earn 2 points per dollar spent (equivalent to 2% cash back) on gas, groceries, travel, transportation, and recurring bills
  • Earn 1 point per dollar spent (equivalent to 1% cash back) on everything else
This isn't necessarily the most competitive earning structure out there, but if cash back is your goal, then you'll enjoy acceptance almost everywhere as a Visa product.

When it comes time to redeem, the points are worth 1 cent each, and can be used to offset purchases as a statement credit at a rate of 100 points = $1 statement credit. You can also have the partial or full amount of the points balance applied against specific purchases made on your card. With the ability to redeem points to offset purchases made on the card or to have it applied as a general statement credit, you shouldn't have any difficulties making use of your points. Flexible points like these can be useful for covering travel expenses that are usually difficult to book with points, such as independent hotels, cruises, or car rentals.

The card’s other features are fairly standard for a Visa Infinite product, including access to the Visa Infinite Concierge service and the Visa Infinite Luxury Hotel Collection. Laurentian partnered with Brim Financial with this card, and you'll enjoy some of Brim's standard features. If you’re approved for the card, you’ll immediately have access to a virtual credit card. This way, you can start chugging away at the minimum spending requirement without having to wait for the card to arrive in the mail. As with any other Brim-backed card, you can earn up to 30% extra points when you use Laurentian Bank’s eShop portal.

The insurance coverage on the card is OK, but not great, and includes the following:
  • Up to $5,000,000 in out-of-province coverage for you and your family for 31 days (aged 65 and under) or for 15 days (aged 66–75)
  • Up to $2,000 per person in case of trip cancellation and $5,000 in case of trip interruption
  • Up to $2,500 per family for delayed baggage
  • Up to 48 days for car rental coverage
Noticeably absent from the insurance inclusions is flight delay insurance, lost/stolen baggage insurance, and hotel/motel burglary insurance, which is odd for a travel rewards card.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a Visa Infinite product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $60,000 (individual) or $100,000 (household).

Follow the link below to the Laurentian Bank website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the American Express Business Edge Card, a small business credit card with 3x points on select categories.

acf.subtitle: 3x MR points on business essentials

features.feature: 3x MR points on office supplies, electronics, rides & gas, and eats & drinks

acf.featured_info:
• Also, earn 3x MR points on food and drinks, ridesharing, gas, office equipment, and electronics
  • Transfer MR points to Aeroplan, Avios, or Marriott Bonvoy
  • Enjoy the exclusive benefits of being an American Express cardholder
  • Annual fee: $99

card_benefits.b_title: This card is no longer open to new applications

card_benefits.b_content:
3 MR points per dollar spent on office supplies, electronics, rides & gas, and eats & drinks (including food delivery services, excluding groceries)
1 MR point per dollar spent on all other purchases
Average
Credit card

card_content.c_content:
The American Express Business Edge Card offers an impressive earning rate for its $99 annual fee, earning 3x the points on all office supplies, office supplies, electronics, rides, gas, and eats & drinks.

This card is no longer open to new applications.

The Business Edge Card’s specialty lies in its increased earning rate on select categories. You’ll earn:

  • 3 MR points per dollar spent on eligible business essentials including office supplies and electronics, rides & gas, and eats & drinks

  • 1 MR point per dollar spent on all other categories

To clarify, the “eats & drinks” category does not include purchases made at grocery stores, only restaurants, bars, coffee shops, and food delivery services. Moreover, the 3x spending category is capped at $25,000 in purchases per card membership year; after you’ve spent $25,000 in purchases on these categories, the earning rate will drop to 1x until the calculation resets next year.

With the 3x earnings on “rides & gas” and “eats & drinks”, the card seems ideally suited to the modern entrepreneur who’s constantly meeting clients on the go, while the office supplies and electronics category also caters to more established enterprises as well.

It would make a lot of sense to pay with your Business Edge Card when you’re buying office equipment or electronics, since no other cards in the Canadian market offer accelerated earnings on these categories.

Similarly, paying for rideshares and gas fill-ups using your Business Edge can also be a good idea, since the 3x earning rate represents a strong return.

On the dining category, however, the Cobalt Card for its higher 5x earnings on food purchases.

The Membership Rewards program offers outstanding redemption opportunities as well. This is primarily because it’s a flexible, transferable rewards currency.

You can transfer your MR points at a 1:1 ratio to Aeroplan and British Airways Avios, as well as a host of other global frequent flyer programs (including Cathay Pacific Asia Miles) at a 1:0.75 ratio. Moreover, you also have the option of converting points to Marriott Bonvoy at a 1:1.2 ratio for booking hotel stays.

Furthermore, you can redeem MR points directly for flights through the Amex Fixed Points Travel reward chart, although these are limited to round-trip flights departing from Canada. Essentially, you can use a fixed amount of points towards the base ticket price of a regular cash ticket, up to a certain maximum. The number of points required and the maximum ticket price depends on the geographical region you are visiting (you can only book roundtrip flights that originate in Canada). Lastly, you have the option of redeeming MR points directly against travel purchases at a rate of 1 cent per point (cpp), although this is typically not recommended, as the value pales in comparison to what you could get by booking flights through Aeroplan, Avios, or even the Fixed Points Travel chart.

The Business Edge Card is somewhat lacking when it comes to the insurance package. It doesn't come with provisions for Emergency Medical Insurance, Flight or Baggage Delay, or Trip Interruption & Cancellation, which are key protections that are found on many cards out there that have a stronger focus on travel.

The Business Edge Card does offer Car Rental Theft & Damage Insurance, as well as 90-day Purchase Protection in the case of loss or damage, as well as Buyer’s Assurance, which automatically extends the manufacturer’s warranty by up to one year. Both of these protections can be very useful, since you’re likely to buy many of your electronics using the Business Edge given the 3x points you earn.

colum_block.col1_text: This card is no longer open to new applications.

title.rendered: Desjardins Cash Back World Elite Mastercard

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Desjardins Cash Back World Elite Mastercard, one of Canada’s leading cash back credit cards.

acf.subtitle: Up to 4% cash back

features.feature: Earn more rewards on popular spending categories

acf.featured_info:
• Earn 1% 4% cash back on groceries
  • Earn 1% 3% cash back on restaurants, entertainment, and public transit
  • Up to 25% discount on car rentals at Hertz, Dollar, and Thrifty
  • Mobile device insurance
  • Minimum income: $80,000 personal or $150,000 household
  • Annual fee: $100

card_benefits.b_content:
$100 for the primary cardholder
$30 for additional cardholders

4% cash back on groceries
3% cash back on restaurants, entertainment, and transit
1% cash back on all other purchases

Discounts on car rentals at Hertz, Dollar, and Thrifty
DragonPass membership
Moderate

card_content.c_content:
The Desjardins Cash Back World Elite Mastercard is a solid choice for anyone who spends a lot on groceries and prefers to shop at stores that accept Mastercards. It's also useful for some types of travel insurance, although gaps in its coverage will leave you looking for another card to make up for these deficiencies.
[read-more ids="30102"]
This card has no welcome bonus, and no first year fee reduction. Instead, the annual fee is $100. Additional cards cost $30 per year, a fee which is waived if you're a Desjardins banking member.

This card offers a tiered rewards structure:
  • 4% back on groceries
  • 3% back on restaurants, entertainment, and transit
  • 1% back on all other purchases
While you can do better for uncategorized spending, these category rates are incredibly strong for a Mastercard. There's also no annual limit on cash back in bonus categories.
Cash back is issued as a statement credit when your rewards reach $100.

The card comes with all of the standard Mastercard World Elite benefits, including a DragonPass membership (although without any free passes). Also, the card offers car rental benefits, including no young driver surcharge, no fee for additional drivers, Hertz Five Star status, and discounts on luxury car rentals. Cardholders can get a discount of up to 25% at Hertz or 10% at Dollar and Thrifty on regular car rentals.

At first glance, the card appears to offer strong emergency travel medical coverage, up to $5,000,000 for cardholders up to age 75. However, this coverage is only valid on trips of 3 days or less. If your trip is longer, you'd have to buy additional days of coverage for even the first 3 days to be covered. I'd consider this useless, and I'd rely on my other cards for this type of coverage. The card can be used for insurance on award tickets. Coverage is valid as long as the fees were paid using the credit card. The card's car rental insurance grants coverage for cars valued at up to $85,000, higher than the industry standard for this type of insurance. Paired with discounts on luxury car rentals, this could make for some memorable road trips! Finally, the card has mobile device protection. If you buy your phone or tablet with this card, you'll be covered for up to $1,000 for two years, in the event of loss, theft, or accidental damage.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $80,000 (individual) or $150,000 (household).

Follow the below link to the Desjardins website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Desjardins Odyssey World Elite Mastercard, the flagship rewards Mastercard issued by Desjardins.

acf.subtitle: Up to 3% cash back

card_benefits.b_content:
3% cash back on groceries
2% cash back on restaurants, entertainment, and transit
1.5% cash back on all other purchases

Eight annual complimentary visits to the Desjardins Odyssey Lounge at YUL
Discounts on car rentals at Hertz, Dollar, and Thrifty
DragonPass membership
Very strong

card_content.c_content: The Desjardins Odyssey World Elite Mastercard is a solid choice for a one-card wallet. It's got a mix of wide Mastercard acceptance, above average earning rates, very strong insurance coverage, and a reasonable annual fee.
This card has no welcome bonus, and no first year fee reduction. Instead, the annual fee is $130. Additional cards cost $40 per year, a fee which is waived if you're a Desjardins banking member.

This card offers a tiered rewards structure:
  • 3% back on groceries
  • 2% back on restaurants, entertainment, and transit
  • 1.5% back on all other purchases
Rewards are denominated in Desjardins BonusDollars, which are worth a fixed value of $1.

BonusDollars can be redeemed 1:1 for all sorts of things, including statement credit, travel bookings, gift cards, or into your Desjardins investment accounts. Because the points have a fixed value and are so easy to redeem, I'd treat this card like a cash back card.

The card provides eight annual lounge passes to the Desjardins Odyssey Lounge at Montreal-Trudeau Airport, located in the international departures terminal. All primary and secondary cardholders on the account will receive eight passes. Also, the Desjardins Odyssey cards offer car rental benefits, including no young driver surcharge, no fee for additional drivers, Hertz Five Star status, and discounts on luxury car rentals. World Elite cardholders can get a discount of up to 25% at Hertz or 10% at Dollar and Thrifty on regular car rentals. The card comes with all of the standard Mastercard World Elite benefits, including a complimentary DragonPass membership (although you will need to pay the lounge entrance fee for each visit).

The card offers top-tier emergency medical insurance when travelling out of your home province. Cardholders are covered for up to $5,000,000 in damages, for trips up to 60 days, up to age 75.
  • Age 59 and under: trips up to 60 days
  • Age 60 to 64: trips up to 31 days
  • Age 65 to 75: trips up to 15 days
The card can be used for insurance on award tickets. Coverage is valid for trip cancellation or trip interruption, as long as the fees were paid using the credit card. Lost or stolen baggage is covered under the emergency medical policy, which doesn't require the trip to be paid on the card at all. This card also extends travel insurance to the cardholder's grandchildren, if they are travelling together. The card's car rental insurance grants coverage for cars valued at up to $85,000, higher than the industry standard for this type of insurance. Paired with discounts on luxury car rentals, this could make for some memorable road trips! Finally, the card has mobile device protection. If you buy your phone or tablet with this card, you'll be covered for up to $1,000 for two years, in the event of loss, theft, or accidental damage.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a World Elite product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $80,000 (individual) or $150,000 (household).

Follow the below link to the Desjardins website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Desjardins Odyssey Visa Infinite Privilege, the flagship rewards Visa credit card issued by Desjardins.

acf.subtitle: Up to 4% cash back
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 4% cash back on restaurant purchases
  • Earn 3% cash back on grocery and travel purchases
  • Earn 1.75% cash back on everything else
  • Access DragonPass airport lounges with six complimentary visits
  • Best in-class medical and travel insurance coverage
  • Minimum income: $200,000 household
  • Annual fee: $395

card_benefits.b_content:
$395 for the primary cardholder
$95 for additional cardholders

4% cash back on restaurants, entertainment, and transit
3% cash back on groceries & travel
1.75% cash back on all other purchases

Six annual complimentary visits to DragonPass lounges
12 annual complimentary visits to the Desjardins Odyssey Lounge at YUL
Discounts on car rentals at Hertz, Dollar, and Thrifty
Very strong

card_content.c_content: The Desjardins Odyssey Visa Infinite Privilege is a great choice for a premium Visa card. It has flexible redemptions, high earn rates on categorized and uncategorized spending, and a lower annual fee than competitors. But perhaps the biggest appeal is the card's generous insurance, among the best in the credit card market. Also, frequent international travellers based in Quebec will find themselves overflowing with lounge passes.

This card has no welcome bonus, and no first year fee reduction. Instead, the annual fee is $395, and $95 for additional cards. Desjardins banking members pay lower rates: $295 for the primary cardholder, and no fee for additional cards. Whether or not you bank with Desjardins, these fees are lower than many other Visa Infinite Privilege cards, with comparable benefits.

This card offers a tiered rewards structure:
  • 4% back on restaurants, entertainment, and transit
  • 3% back on groceries and travel
  • 1.75% back on all other purchases
Rewards are denominated in Desjardins BonusDollars, which are worth a fixed value of $1.

BonusDollars can be redeemed 1:1 for all sorts of things, including statement credit, travel bookings, gift cards, or into your Desjardins investment accounts.

Because the points have a fixed value and are so easy to redeem, I'd treat this card like a cash back card.

The card comes with all of the standard Visa Infinite Privilege benefits for primary and secondary cardholders, including concierge service and the Luxury Hotel Collection. In particular, you'll get a DragonPass membership with free vouchers for six annual visits to lounges and restaurants that participate in the program. The card also offers 12 annual lounge passes to the Desjardins Odyssey Lounge at Montreal-Trudeau Airport, located in the international departures terminal. All primary and secondary cardholders on the account will receive 12 passes. When you run out of passes, you can pay for additional visits at 50% off the regular price. Also, the Desjardins Odyssey cards offer car rental benefits, including no young driver surcharge, no fee for additional drivers, Hertz Five Star status, and discounts on luxury car rentals. Visa Infinite Privilege cardholders can get a discount of up to 25% at Hertz or 10% at Dollar and Thrifty on regular car rentals.

The Desjardins Odyssey Visa Infinite Privilege provides outstanding insurance coverage. The card offers top-tier emergency medical insurance when travelling out of your home province. Cardholders are covered for up to $5,000,000 in damages, for trips up to 60 days, up to age 75.
  • Age 59 and under: trips up to 60 days
  • Age 60 to 64: trips up to 31 days
  • Age 65 to 75: trips up to 15 days
The card can be used for insurance on award tickets. Coverage is valid for trip cancellation or trip interruption, as long as the fees were paid using the credit card. Lost or stolen baggage is covered under the emergency medical policy, which doesn't require the trip to be paid on the card at all. This card also extends travel insurance to the cardholder's grandchildren, if they are travelling together. The card's car rental insurance grants coverage for cars valued at up to $85,000, higher than the industry standard for this type of insurance. Paired with discounts on luxury car rentals, this could make for some memorable road trips! Additionally, the card has industry-leading purchase protection. The extended warranty and purchase protection periods are double what many competitors offer, with up to 2 additional years for extended warranty and up to 180 days of protection against stolen or damaged merchandise. Finally, the card has mobile device protection. If you buy your phone or tablet with this card, you'll be covered for up to $1,000 for two years, in the event of loss, theft, or accidental damage.

colum_block.col1_text:
As a Visa Infinite Privilege product, this credit card has a minimum annual income requirement of $200,000 (household).

Follow the below link to the Desjardins website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Desjardins Odyssey Gold Visa, the mid-tier cash back credit card issued by Desjardins.

acf.subtitle: Up to 2% cash back

features.feature: Visa Infinite Concierge & Luxury Hotel Collection

card_benefits.b_content:
$110 for the primary cardholder
$30 for additional cardholders

2% cash back on restaurants, entertainment, transit, and recurring payments
1% cash back on all other purchases

Half price entry to the Desjardins Odyssey Lounge at YUL
Discounts on car rentals at Hertz, Dollar, and Thrifty
Very strong

card_content.c_content: The Desjardins Odyssey Gold Visa is a fine card, but offers uninspiring rewards rates given the annual fee. Instead, it would appeal to anyone who wants premium insurance coverage on a lower budget. It offers a strong package with features usually only seen on cards with higher annual fees and higher income requirements.
This card has no welcome bonus, and no first year fee reduction. Instead, the annual fee is $110. Additional cards cost $30 per year, a fee which is waived if you're a Desjardins banking member.

This card offers 2% back on restaurants, entertainment, transit, and recurring payments. On all other purchases, you'll earn 1% back. Rewards are denominated in Desjardins BonusDollars, which are worth a fixed value of $1.

BonusDollars can be redeemed 1:1 for all sorts of things, including statement credit, travel bookings, gift cards, or into your Desjardins investment accounts.

Because the points have a fixed value and are so easy to redeem, I'd treat this card like a cash back card.

The card offers half price admission to the Desjardins Odyssey Lounge at Montreal-Trudeau Airport, located in the international departures terminal. Also, the Desjardins Odyssey cards offer car rental benefits, including no young driver surcharge, no fee for additional drivers, Hertz Five Star status, and discounts on luxury car rentals. Visa Infinite cardholders can get a discount of up to 25% at Hertz or 10% at Dollar and Thrifty on regular car rentals.

The card offers top-tier emergency medical insurance when travelling out of your home province. Cardholders are covered for up to $5,000,000 in damages, for trips up to 60 days, up to age 75.
  • Age 59 and under: trips up to 60 days
  • Age 60 to 64: trips up to 31 days
  • Age 65 to 75: trips up to 15 days
The card can be used for insurance on award tickets. Coverage is valid for trip cancellation or trip interruption, as long as the fees were paid using the credit card. Lost or stolen baggage is covered under the emergency medical policy, which doesn't require the trip to be paid on the card at all. This card also extends travel insurance to the cardholder's grandchildren, if they are travelling together. The card's car rental insurance grants coverage for cars valued at up to $85,000, higher than the industry standard for this type of insurance. Paired with discounts on luxury car rentals, this could make for some memorable road trips! Finally, the card has mobile device protection. If you buy your phone or tablet with this card, you'll be covered for up to $1,000 for two years, in the event of loss, theft, or accidental damage.

colum_block.col1_text:
This card has no minimum income requirement, and should be easier to get approved for if you don't qualify for premium cards.

Follow the below link to the Desjardins website to learn more about this card and submit an application.

excerpt.rendered: Everything you need to know about the Desjardins Cash Back Mastercard, a no-fee cash back credit card option issued by Desjardins.

acf.subtitle: Up to 2% cash back
acf.featured_info:
• Earn 2% cash back on restaurants, entertainment, transit, and recurring bill payments
  • Up to 15% discount on car rentals at Hertz, Dollar, and Thrifty
  • Mobile device insurance
  • Annual fee: $0

card_benefits.b_content:
2% cash back on restaurants, entertainment, transit, and recurring bills
0.5% cash back on all other purchases
Discounts on car rentals at Hertz, Dollar, and Thrifty
Moderate

card_content.c_content:
The Desjardins Cash Back Mastercard is a solid choice for big-city dwellers looking to earn cash back on frequent urban expenses. It's also useful for some types of travel insurance, and while not entirely comprehensive, it does offer some notable coverage for a card with no annual fee.
[read-more ids="30103"]

This card has no welcome bonus, but also no annual fee. Desjardins isn't known for offering signup incentives, so if you want to add the card to your wallet, feel free to apply at any time - I wouldn't recommend holding out for an offer.

This card offers a tiered rewards structure:
  • 2% back on restaurants, entertainment, transit, and recurring pre-authorized payments
  • 0.5% back on everything else
Clearly, this card is targeting young, on-the-go people who want to keep annual fees down while earning top dollar on "lifestyle" categories. It's a pretty broad mix of bonus categories, and a uniquely appealing combination among no-fee cards if they tick your boxes. While a grocery bonus is sorely lacking, these rates are incredibly strong for a no-fee Mastercard. There's also no annual limit on cash back in bonus categories.
Cash back is issued as a statement credit every time your rewards reach $25.

The card offers car rental benefits, including no young driver surcharge, and no fee for additional drivers. Cardholders can get a discount of up to 15% at Hertz or 10% at Dollar and Thrifty on eligible car rentals.

The insurance on this card is quite strong for a no-fee card, in line with some of the features you'll find on many premium credit cards. You'll get emergency medical insurance up to the age of 75, trip interruption/cancellation coverage, and flight and baggage delay coverage. However, it's worth noting that these policies are only valid for trips up to 3 days. If you're going away for more than a weekend, you'll have to buy a top-up to be covered at all. The card can be used for insurance on award tickets. Coverage is valid as long as the fees were paid using the credit card. The card's car rental insurance grants coverage for cars valued at up to $85,000, higher than the industry standard for this type of insurance. This could make for some memorable road trips! Finally, the card has mobile device protection. If you buy your phone or tablet with this card, you'll be covered for up to $1,000 for two years, in the event of loss, theft, or accidental damage.

colums.col2_text:
As an entry-level product, anyone can apply for this card.

Click here to visit the website and learn more about the Desjardins Cash Back Mastercard.

If you'd prefer a Visa card, Desjardins offers an identical product, the only difference is that it's not a Mastercard.

Click here to visit the website and learn more about the Desjardins Cash Back Visa.
